417	@dataclass
418	class ReleaseOutcome:
419	    """The always-release-aware release outcome over a D8 ``ReleaseDecision`` (I-perm-001).
420	
421	    ``released`` — a report ships (normal OR honest-insufficient-safety variant).
422	    ``hard_block`` — the no-fabrication hard line fired; NO report ships as clean.
423	    ``normal_release_blocked`` — the polished normal report is withheld (hard_block OR the clinical
424	        safety floor is insufficient), but an honest report may still ship.
425	    ``status`` — the terminal pipeline status.
426	    ``disclosed_gaps`` — the held reasons surfaced as DISPLAYED labels (not blockers).
427	    ``release_quality_score`` — the displayed coverage fraction (NOT a trap-door threshold).
428	    """
429	
430	    released: bool
431	    hard_block: bool
432	    normal_release_blocked: bool
433	    status: str
434	    disclosed_gaps: list[str]
435	    hard_block_reasons: list[str]
436	    release_quality_score: float
437	    safety_floor: str  # "ok" | "insufficient"
438	    # A18/A2-SEAM STRUCTURAL PROOF FIELDS (iarch007 SWEEP-P0 + RELEASE-P0). These three fields
439	    # let `assert_release_invariant` decide STRUCTURALLY (not by sniffing a gap-label string)
440	    # whether un-judged prose may ship. ALL THREE default to the SAFE (un-proven / fail-closed)
441	    # value so an outcome built without proof can never pass the invariant by omission:
442	    #   * adjudicated — the four-role D8 judge TRULY ran (backed by real final_verdicts). Defaults
443	    #     FALSE: a release that does not explicitly prove adjudication is treated as un-judged.
444	    #   * body_withheld — the findings body was suppressed (the seam fabrication-screen could not
445	    #     run / found a fabricated identity). Defaults FALSE (body ships) — explicit.
446	    #   * compensating_screen_passed — on a seam (no D8) the standalone fabrication screen ran
447	    #     CLEAN, so a body that ships is screen-safe. Defaults FALSE (no compensating proof).
448	    adjudicated: bool = False
449	    body_withheld: bool = False
450	    compensating_screen_passed: bool = False
451	
452	    def display_quality_score(self) -> str:
453	        """B11 C3 (#1362 deepfix): the HONEST display string for the release quality score.
454	
455	        ``release_quality_score`` is the coverage fraction. On a four-role D8 SEAM error (judge
456	        transport / HTTP-400) the judge NEVER adjudicated, so ``adjudicated`` is False and the raw
457	        float is 0.0 (or a coverage fraction that was never judged). Rendering that bare ``0.0`` as
458	        a quality score is a LIE — it reads as "this report scored zero quality" when the truth is
459	        "D8 could not score it at all". This helper renders ``N/A (D8 unadjudicated)`` whenever the
460	        judge did not adjudicate, and the numeric score otherwise. The raw float field is left
461	        unchanged for backward-compatible numeric consumers; the render/UI seam should consult THIS
462	        display string. Faithfulness-neutral: pure presentation of the already-computed state.
463	        """
464	        if not self.adjudicated:
465	            return "N/A (D8 unadjudicated)"
466	        return f"{self.release_quality_score:.3f}"
467	
468	
469	def always_release_enabled() -> bool:
470	    """PG_ALWAYS_RELEASE — production DEFAULT ON (B5/B7: "nothing shall hold the report").
471	
472	    OFF only on an EXPLICIT off token ('0'/'false'/'no'/'off'). Unset, EMPTY (''), OR any
473	    unrecognized value resolves to the default (ON) — a default-ON flag must never SILENTLY
474	    withhold a report on an unset/empty/typo value. The OFF switch is the byte-identical
475	    legacy-regression escape hatch; legacy callers pass it explicitly ('0' via env, or
476	    always_release=False to compute_release_outcome) and never as an empty string.
477	    """
478	    import os
479	
480	    return os.environ.get(_ENV_ALWAYS_RELEASE, "").strip().lower() not in _OFF_VALUES
481	
482	
483	def compute_release_outcome(
484	    decision: ReleaseDecision,
485	    *,
486	    zero_verified: bool,
487	    zero_usable_evidence: bool,
488	    safety_floor_insufficient: bool,
489	    coverage_fraction: float,
490	    always_release: bool | None = None,
491	    redaction_active: bool = True,
492	    seam_unadjudicated: bool = False,
493	    fabrication_screen_ran: bool | None = None,
494	    fabrication_screen_found_fabrication: bool = False,
495	) -> ReleaseOutcome:
496	    """Map a D8 ``ReleaseDecision`` to a release outcome under the always-release reframe.
497	
498	    When always-release is OFF, reproduce the LEGACY decision verbatim: ``released`` ==
499	    ``decision.release_allowed``, the held reasons stay blockers, and the status is the legacy
500	    held/success label — byte-identical behaviour. When ON, convert non-hard holds to disclosed
501	    gaps and release, keeping only the zero-grounding hard line and the clinical safety-floor
502	    normal-render block.
503	
504	    ``redaction_active`` (default True — production default; the kill-switch is test/offline-only):
505	    B5/B7 (operator-ratified 2026-06-14) NARROWS the FABRICATED block. With redaction active, a
506	    latched FABRICATED occurrence ships the report MINUS the fabricated claim (excised by the
507	    report_redactor) with a LOUD disclosed-gap label, rather than hard-blocking the whole report.
508	    Only TRUE zero-grounding remains a whole-report hard block. If redaction is disabled, FABRICATED
509	    stays a hard block (the fabricated prose could otherwise ship) — see ``is_hard_block``.
510	
511	    A2-SEAM (iarch007 RELEASE-P0) — the un-judged-release leak this CLOSES. When the four-role D8
512	    judge could not be reached (a transport error / HTTP-400), D8 NEVER adjudicated, so the outcome
513	    MUST carry the un-judged state honestly (``adjudicated=False``) and a non-empty seam disclosure,
514	    and a body may ship ONLY if a STANDALONE fabrication screen proves it safe:
515	      * ``seam_unadjudicated`` — this is the judge-seam path (no D8). Adjudicated is FALSE; the
516	        specific ``four_role_seam_unadjudicated`` gap is injected so the outcome can only resolve to
517	        released_with_disclosed_gaps, never success.
518	      * ``fabrication_screen_ran`` — TRI-STATE, FAIL-CLOSED on unknown. ``None`` (default / the
519	        screen result is UNKNOWN) and ``False`` (the screen could NOT run) BOTH WITHHOLD the body —
520	        an un-screened body never ships on a seam (P0 #3). Only an EXPLICIT ``True`` ships the body.
521	      * ``fabrication_screen_found_fabrication`` — even when the screen ran (``True``), if it FOUND
522	        a cited identity not in the evidence pool the body is WITHHELD (conservative). The body ships
523	        only when the screen ran AND found no fabrication, recorded as ``compensating_screen_passed``.
524	    On the non-seam (genuine-D8) paths ``adjudicated=True`` — the judge produced this decision.
525	    """
526	    if seam_unadjudicated:
527	        return _compute_seam_outcome(
528	            decision,
529	            zero_verified=zero_verified,
530	            zero_usable_evidence=zero_usable_evidence,
531	            safety_floor_insufficient=safety_floor_insufficient,
532	            coverage_fraction=coverage_fraction,
533	            always_release=always_release,
534	            redaction_active=redaction_active,
535	            fabrication_screen_ran=fabrication_screen_ran,
536	            fabrication_screen_found_fabrication=fabrication_screen_found_fabrication,
537	        )
538	    enabled = always_release_enabled() if always_release is None else always_release
539	    hard_block = is_hard_block(
540	        fabricated_occurrence_latched=decision.fabricated_occurrence_latched,
541	        zero_verified=zero_verified,
542	        zero_usable_evidence=zero_usable_evidence,
543	        redaction_active=redaction_active,
544	    )
545	    hard_block_reasons: list[str] = []
546	    if zero_verified and zero_usable_evidence:
547	        hard_block_reasons.append("zero_grounding")
548	    # The fabricated reason is a HARD-block reason ONLY while it actually hard-blocks (redaction
549	    # off). When the narrowing applies (always-release ON + redaction active) it is surfaced as a
550	    # disclosed-gap label below, never as a hard_block_reason.
551	    if decision.fabricated_occurrence_latched and not redaction_active:
552	        hard_block_reasons.append(_REASON_FABRICATED_OCCURRENCE)
553	    safety_floor = "insufficient" if safety_floor_insufficient else "ok"
554	
555	    if not enabled:
556	        # Legacy withhold path — byte-identical to `decision.release_allowed`.
557	        # Legacy hard_block surfacing is independent of the B5/B7 narrowing (redaction param),
558	        # so recompute it here on the pre-narrowing fabricated/zero-grounding line to preserve the
559	        # exact OFF-path fields (Codex slice-1 P2).
560	        legacy_hard_block = bool(
561	            decision.fabricated_occurrence_latched
562	            or (zero_verified and zero_usable_evidence)
563	        )
564	        legacy_hard_reasons: list[str] = []
565	        if decision.fabricated_occurrence_latched:
566	            legacy_hard_reasons.append(_REASON_FABRICATED_OCCURRENCE)
567	        if zero_verified and zero_usable_evidence:
568	            legacy_hard_reasons.append("zero_grounding")
569	        status = STATUS_SUCCESS if decision.release_allowed else STATUS_ABORT_FOUR_ROLE_HELD
570	        return ReleaseOutcome(
571	            released=decision.release_allowed,
572	            hard_block=not decision.release_allowed and legacy_hard_block,
573	            normal_release_blocked=not decision.release_allowed,
574	            status=status,
575	            disclosed_gaps=[],
576	            hard_block_reasons=legacy_hard_reasons if not decision.release_allowed else [],
577	            release_quality_score=coverage_fraction,
578	            safety_floor=safety_floor,
579	            # Non-seam: this decision was produced by a four-role D8 run that ACTUALLY adjudicated.
580	            adjudicated=True,
581	        )
582	
583	    # --- always-release ON ---
584	    if hard_block:
585	        # Under the B5/B7 narrowing, the ONLY thing that reaches here is true zero-grounding (a
586	        # fabricated occurrence with redaction active is NOT a hard block). Zero-grounding is
587	        # statuses-abort_no_verified; a fabricated-with-redaction-OFF hard block keeps the legacy
588	        # abort_evaluator_critical label.
589	        status = (
590	            STATUS_ABORT_FABRICATED
591	            if (decision.fabricated_occurrence_latched and not redaction_active)
592	            else STATUS_ABORT_NO_VERIFIED
593	        )
594	        return ReleaseOutcome(
595	            released=False,
596	            hard_block=True,
597	            normal_release_blocked=True,
598	            status=status,
599	            # A hard-block reason (fabricated) is NEVER a disclosed GAP (Codex slice-1 P2): it
600	            # lives in hard_block_reasons. Surface only the non-hard holds as disclosed gaps.
601	            disclosed_gaps=[
602	                r for r in decision.held_reasons if r != _REASON_FABRICATED_OCCURRENCE
603	            ],
604	            hard_block_reasons=hard_block_reasons,
605	            release_quality_score=coverage_fraction,
606	            safety_floor=safety_floor,
607	            adjudicated=True,
608	        )
609	
610	    # Every remaining held reason is a DISPLAYED disclosed-gap label, not a blocker.
611	    # The fabricated occurrence (now narrowed to ship-minus-the-claim) is NOT in
612	    # decision.held_reasons as a label — it lives there as the hard `_REASON_FABRICATED_OCCURRENCE`
613	    # blocker token. Replace it with the explicit "dropped and disclosed" gap so the disclosure is
614	    # LOUD and never mistaken for the hard-block token.
615	    disclosed_gaps = [
616	        r for r in decision.held_reasons if r != _REASON_FABRICATED_OCCURRENCE
617	    ]
618	    if decision.fabricated_occurrence_latched:
619	        disclosed_gaps.append(_GAP_FABRICATED_CITATION_DROPPED)
620	    if safety_floor_insufficient:
621	        # Clinical safety floor: ship the honest insufficient-safety report, block normal render.
622	        return ReleaseOutcome(
623	            released=True,
624	            hard_block=False,
625	            normal_release_blocked=True,
626	            status=STATUS_RELEASED_INSUFFICIENT_SAFETY,
627	            disclosed_gaps=disclosed_gaps,
628	            hard_block_reasons=[],
629	            release_quality_score=coverage_fraction,
630	            safety_floor="insufficient",
631	            adjudicated=True,
632	        )
633	    status = STATUS_SUCCESS if not disclosed_gaps else STATUS_RELEASED_WITH_DISCLOSED_GAPS
634	    return ReleaseOutcome(
635	        released=True,
636	        hard_block=False,
637	        normal_release_blocked=False,
638	        status=status,
639	        disclosed_gaps=disclosed_gaps,
640	        hard_block_reasons=[],
641	        release_quality_score=coverage_fraction,
642	        safety_floor="ok",
643	        adjudicated=True,
644	    )
645	
646	
647	# ── A2-SEAM + A18 HARD RELEASE-INVARIANT (iarch007 RELEASE-P0/SWEEP-P0) ──────────────────────
648	# The un-judged-release leak this whole block CLOSES: a four-role D8 seam error (the judge could
649	# not be reached) means D8 NEVER adjudicated. The OLD ReleaseOutcome had no way to RECORD that —
650	# the runtime seam builder defaulted to `adjudicated=True`, and the invariant accepted ANY
651	# non-empty disclosed_gaps as "seam proof". Either gap let an un-judged, un-screened body ship.
652	# These three structural fields + the prefix-aware detector + the fail-closed invariant make a
653	# seam release provable ONLY when (a) the body is withheld OR (b) a standalone fabrication screen
654	# ran clean AND the SPECIFIC seam gap is disclosed. Faithfulness: RECORDS the un-judged state and
655	# REFUSES an unsafe release — it relaxes nothing and marks nothing verified.
656	
657	# The SPECIFIC disclosed-gap label the A2 seam rescue injects (matches scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3
658	# SEAM_GAP_UNADJUDICATED + iarch007_release_invariant_check._SEAM_GAP_TOKEN — one vocabulary).
659	_GAP_FOUR_ROLE_SEAM_UNADJUDICATED = "four_role_seam_unadjudicated"
660	# The disclosed label surfaced when the standalone seam fabrication screen could NOT run (so the
661	# body is withheld fail-closed — the withhold is never silent).
662	_GAP_SEAM_FABRICATION_SCREEN_UNAVAILABLE = "four_role_seam_fabrication_screen_unavailable"
663	
664	
665	def _has_seam_unadjudicated_gap(disclosed_gaps: list[str]) -> bool:
666	    """True iff ``disclosed_gaps`` carries the SPECIFIC four_role_seam_unadjudicated label.
667	
668	    PREFIX-AWARE (not a blanket substring): the seam disclosure is either the bare constant OR the
669	    descriptive runtime form ``"four_role_seam_unadjudicated: <reason>"`` that
670	    build_seam_release_outcome emits. A gap that merely CONTAINS the token mid-string (e.g.
671	    ``"note: four_role_seam_unadjudicated happened"``) is NOT the seam label — accepting it would
672	    re-open the iarch007 SWEEP-P0 bypass where an arbitrary gap passed as seam proof. So the match
673	    is: equality with the constant, OR a prefix of ``"<constant>:"``.
674	    """
675	    for gap in disclosed_gaps or []:
676	        g = str(gap)
677	        if g == _GAP_FOUR_ROLE_SEAM_UNADJUDICATED:
678	            return True
679	        if g.startswith(f"{_GAP_FOUR_ROLE_SEAM_UNADJUDICATED}:"):
680	            return True
681	    return False
682	
683	
684	def _compute_seam_outcome(
685	    decision: ReleaseDecision,
686	    *,
687	    zero_verified: bool,
688	    zero_usable_evidence: bool,
689	    safety_floor_insufficient: bool,
690	    coverage_fraction: float,
691	    always_release: bool | None,
692	    redaction_active: bool,
693	    fabrication_screen_ran: bool | None,
694	    fabrication_screen_found_fabrication: bool,
695	) -> ReleaseOutcome:
696	    """The judge-SEAM release outcome (D8 never adjudicated). FAIL-CLOSED.
697	
698	    ``adjudicated`` is ALWAYS False on a seam (the judge did not run). A non-empty seam disclosure
699	    is ALWAYS injected so the outcome can only resolve to released_with_disclosed_gaps, never
700	    success. The body ships ONLY when a standalone fabrication screen PROVES it safe:
701	      * ``fabrication_screen_ran`` is None (UNKNOWN) or False (could not run) -> body WITHHELD, the
702	        unavailable-screen gap disclosed (P0 #3: an un-screened seam body never ships);
703	      * ``fabrication_screen_ran`` True AND found a fabricated identity -> body WITHHELD;
704	      * ``fabrication_screen_ran`` True AND found nothing -> body ships, compensating_screen_passed.
705	
706	    A true zero-grounding (no VERIFIED claim AND no usable evidence) or a fabricated occurrence with
707	    redaction OFF is STILL a whole-report hard block on a seam (the same no-fabrication hard line as
708	    the non-seam path); it is checked first so an un-grounded seam never ships.
709	    """
710	    enabled = always_release_enabled() if always_release is None else always_release
711	    safety_floor = "insufficient" if safety_floor_insufficient else "ok"
712	
713	    # The seam disclosure (bare constant; the runtime descriptive form is added by the caller in
714	    # scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.build_seam_release_outcome — the prefix-aware detector matches both).
715	    disclosed_gaps: list[str] = [_GAP_FOUR_ROLE_SEAM_UNADJUDICATED]
716	
717	    # The no-fabrication hard line still applies on a seam — a zero-grounding or
718	    # fabricated-with-redaction-off body must not ship even un-judged.
719	    hard_block = is_hard_block(
720	        fabricated_occurrence_latched=decision.fabricated_occurrence_latched,
721	        zero_verified=zero_verified,
722	        zero_usable_evidence=zero_usable_evidence,
723	        redaction_active=redaction_active,
724	    )
725	    if (not enabled) or hard_block:
726	        hard_block_reasons: list[str] = []
727	        if zero_verified and zero_usable_evidence:
728	            hard_block_reasons.append("zero_grounding")
729	        if decision.fabricated_occurrence_latched and not redaction_active:
730	            hard_block_reasons.append(_REASON_FABRICATED_OCCURRENCE)
731	        status = (
732	            STATUS_ABORT_FABRICATED
733	            if (decision.fabricated_occurrence_latched and not redaction_active)
734	            else STATUS_ABORT_FOUR_ROLE_HELD
735	            if not enabled
736	            else STATUS_ABORT_NO_VERIFIED
737	        )
738	        # Fail-closed seam hold: body withheld, never adjudicated.
739	        return ReleaseOutcome(
740	            released=False,
741	            hard_block=hard_block,
742	            normal_release_blocked=True,
743	            status=status,
744	            disclosed_gaps=disclosed_gaps,
745	            hard_block_reasons=hard_block_reasons,
746	            release_quality_score=coverage_fraction,
747	            safety_floor=safety_floor,
748	            adjudicated=False,
749	            body_withheld=True,
750	            compensating_screen_passed=False,
751	        )
752	
753	    # always-release ON, not hard-blocked: decide the body disposition from the screen result.
754	    # ALWAYS-RELEASE PRINCIPLE (operator-locked: the verifier is a LABEL, never a HOLD): the D8 safety
755	    # floor being unconfirmed does NOT withhold the body — the report ALWAYS ships and the unadjudicated
756	    # state is DISCLOSED so the user judges (status=released_insufficient_safety_evidence + the seam
757	    # disclosure below). safety_floor_insufficient is a LABEL, not a gate. (An earlier change wrongly
758	    # forced a withhold here; reverted per operator 2026-06-20 — see feedback_always_release.)
759	    screen_clean = fabrication_screen_ran is True and not fabrication_screen_found_fabrication
760	    body_withheld = not screen_clean
761	    compensating_screen_passed = screen_clean
762	    if not screen_clean:
763	        # The body is withheld — surface WHY so the withhold is never silent (P0 #3).
764	        disclosed_gaps.append(_GAP_SEAM_FABRICATION_SCREEN_UNAVAILABLE)
765	
766	    status = (
767	        STATUS_RELEASED_INSUFFICIENT_SAFETY
768	        if safety_floor_insufficient
769	        else STATUS_RELEASED_WITH_DISCLOSED_GAPS
770	    )
771	    return ReleaseOutcome(
772	        released=True,
773	        # The body is suppressed when withheld OR when the clinical safety floor blocks the normal
774	        # render; either way the polished normal report is not shipped as clean.
775	        hard_block=False,
776	        normal_release_blocked=body_withheld or safety_floor_insufficient,
777	        status=status,
778	        disclosed_gaps=disclosed_gaps,
779	        hard_block_reasons=[],
780	        release_quality_score=coverage_fraction,
781	        safety_floor=safety_floor,
782	        adjudicated=False,
783	        body_withheld=body_withheld,
784	        compensating_screen_passed=compensating_screen_passed,
785	    )
786	
787	
788	class ReleaseInvariantError(RuntimeError):
789	    """Raised by ``assert_release_invariant`` when a release-asserting outcome cannot PROVE the
790	    four-role D8 judge adjudicated, OR (on a seam) that the un-judged body is provably safe.
791	
792	    This is the in-process, structural twin of scripts/iarch007_release_invariant_check (the
793	    artifact-layer CI gate). It is the LAST fail-closed line before the success-path manifest
794	    write: a path that mis-builds the A2 seam rescue (auto-releasing un-judged content as a
795	    release-asserting status) raises here rather than shipping an un-judged report.
796	    """
797	
798	
799	# Release-asserting statuses: a report ships as clean/judge-final OR honest-disclosed. Each demands
800	# proof of adjudication OR a provably-safe seam disposition. abort_*/error_*/partial_* are NOT
801	# release-asserting (the invariant is a no-op on them — no false trip).
802	_RELEASE_ASSERTING_STATUSES = frozenset(
803	    {
804	        STATUS_SUCCESS,
805	        STATUS_RELEASED_WITH_DISCLOSED_GAPS,
806	        STATUS_RELEASED_INSUFFICIENT_SAFETY,
807	    }
808	)
809	
810	
811	def assert_release_invariant(outcome: ReleaseOutcome) -> ReleaseOutcome:
812	    """Fail-closed: refuse any release-asserting outcome that cannot PROVE it is judge-safe.
813	
814	    Returns ``outcome`` unchanged on PASS; raises ``ReleaseInvariantError`` on a violation. The
815	    proof is read STRUCTURALLY from the outcome fields (status + adjudicated + body_withheld +
816	    compensating_screen_passed + the prefix-aware seam-gap detector), NEVER by sniffing an
817	    arbitrary gap-label string. The accepted shapes (and ONLY these):
818	
819	      (a) the four-role D8 judge TRULY adjudicated — ``adjudicated=True`` — AND this is NOT a seam
820	          (a seam disclosure with adjudicated=True is a CONTRADICTION: the default-True-on-seam leak
821	          this closes), OR
822	      (b) the SPECIFIC ``four_role_seam_unadjudicated`` gap is disclosed AND a compensating
823	          standalone fabrication screen ran clean (``compensating_screen_passed`` — leg-3 is COUPLED
824	          to the specific seam gap; a bare screen flag without the disclosure is NOT proof), OR
825	      (c) the findings body is WITHHELD (``body_withheld`` — nothing un-judged ships).
826	
827	    It does NOT accept ``adjudicated=True`` by itself when a seam gap is present (that is the
828	    contradiction), and does NOT accept an arbitrary non-empty disclosed_gaps list as seam proof.
829	    Non-release-asserting statuses (abort_*/error_*/partial_*) pass untouched. A ``released``
830	    that contradicts an abort status is also a violation.
831	    """
832	    status = str(outcome.status or "")
833	    seam_gap = _has_seam_unadjudicated_gap(outcome.disclosed_gaps)
834	
835	    # Contradiction guard (fires for ANY status, before the per-status legs): a seam disclosure
836	    # means the judge did NOT adjudicate, so adjudicated=True alongside the seam gap is the
837	    # default-True-on-seam leak — fail closed.
838	    if seam_gap and outcome.adjudicated:
839	        raise ReleaseInvariantError(
840	            "release outcome carries the four_role_seam_unadjudicated gap AND adjudicated=True — a "
841	            "contradiction (a judge seam-error means D8 never adjudicated). This is the "
842	            "default-True-on-seam leak; the seam outcome MUST carry adjudicated=False."
843	        )
844	
845	    if status not in _RELEASE_ASSERTING_STATUSES:
846	        # Not release-asserting. The only remaining check is a released/abort contradiction:
847	        # a `released`-True outcome whose status is an abort is a self-contradiction.
848	        if outcome.released and status.startswith("abort"):
849	            raise ReleaseInvariantError(
850	                f"released=True but status={status!r} (an abort) — the binding release decision "
851	                "contradicts the terminal status."
852	            )
853	        return outcome
854	
855	    # The seam-rescue proof (the ONLY way a release-asserting status may ship un-judged): the
856	    # SPECIFIC seam gap is disclosed AND the body is provably safe — withheld OR a compensating
857	    # fabrication screen ran clean. An arbitrary disclosed gap is NOT proof (SWEEP-P0).
858	    seam_rescue_proven = seam_gap and (
859	        outcome.body_withheld or outcome.compensating_screen_passed
860	    )
861	
862	    if outcome.adjudicated or seam_rescue_proven:
863	        return outcome
864	
865	    # No real D8 adjudication and no proven-safe seam disposition. A body withheld (even without
866	    # the seam gap) is still a terminal SAFE disposition — nothing un-judged ships — so accept it.
867	    if outcome.body_withheld:
868	        return outcome
869	
870	    raise ReleaseInvariantError(
871	        f"status={status!r} asserts a release, but there is NO D8 adjudication "
872	        "(adjudicated=False) and NO PROVEN seam rescue (the specific four_role_seam_unadjudicated "
873	        "label PLUS a withheld body OR a passed compensating fabrication screen) and the body is NOT "
874	        "withheld. A released report with no real judging and no proven-safe disposition is a silent "
875	        "un-judged release — refused fail-closed."
876	    )
877	
878	
879	# ── U26 (I-deepfix-001): HONEST release-quality scorecard ────────────────────────────────────
880	# The manifest ``release_disclosure.release_quality_score`` was set VERBATIM to the coverage
881	# fraction. On the autopsy'd run that fraction was 1.0 while the evaluator rule-gate had returned
882	# ``gate_class=abort`` (a PT11 uncited-numeric failure) — so the scorecard read GREEN (1.000)
883	# while a real, disclosed deficiency existed. A required-entity slot that was left empty was
884	# masked the same way. This block re-derives the DISPLAYED quality score so a genuine deficiency
885	# pulls the scorecard BELOW green.
886	#
887	# FAITHFULNESS: presentation only. This changes NO gate, NO per-claim verdict, and NO verified
888	# flag. strict_verify / NLI entailment / the four-role D8 judge / provenance / span-grounding are
889	# all untouched — the score can only be LOWERED (never raised), and no un-adjudicated release is
890	# ever made green (an unadjudicated outcome keeps its "N/A (D8 unadjudicated)" display).
891	
892	# The lowest release-quality score the scorecard still renders GREEN. A run with a disclosed
893	# deficiency must land strictly below this. Env-overridable per LAW VI (zero hard-coding).
894	_ENV_GREEN_QUALITY_FLOOR = "PG_RELEASE_QUALITY_GREEN_FLOOR"
895	_DEFAULT_GREEN_QUALITY_FLOOR = 0.85
896	# The ceiling a run's quality score is capped at when the evaluator rule-gate returned an abort
897	# class (the strongest non-fatal deficiency signal). Well below the green floor.
898	_ENV_ABORT_QUALITY_CEILING = "PG_RELEASE_QUALITY_ABORT_CEILING"
899	_DEFAULT_ABORT_QUALITY_CEILING = 0.5
900	
901	
902	def _env_float(name: str, default: float) -> float:
903	    """Read a float env override; fall back to ``default`` on unset/empty/non-numeric."""
904	    raw = os.environ.get(name, "").strip()
905	    if not raw:
906	        return default
907	    try:
908	        return float(raw)
909	    except ValueError:
910	        return default
911	
912	
913	def green_quality_floor() -> float:
914	    """The lowest release-quality score still rendered GREEN (env-overridable)."""
915	    return _env_float(_ENV_GREEN_QUALITY_FLOOR, _DEFAULT_GREEN_QUALITY_FLOOR)
916	
917	
918	def honest_release_quality_score(
919	    coverage_fraction: float,
920	    *,
921	    eval_gate_aborted: bool = False,
922	    empty_required_slots: int = 0,
923	) -> float:
924	    """Re-derive the displayed release-quality score so real deficiencies show (U26).
925	
926	    The base is the coverage fraction (clamped to ``[0, 1]``). Two deficiency signals pull it
927	    below the green floor:
928	
929	      * ``eval_gate_aborted`` — the evaluator rule-gate returned ``gate_class=abort`` (e.g. a
930	        PT11 uncited-numeric failure). Caps the score at the abort ceiling (default 0.5).
931	      * ``empty_required_slots`` — one or more required-entity slots were left unverified. Caps
932	        the score just below the green floor, and each additional empty slot lowers it further,
933	        so more gaps read strictly worse.
934	
935	    A run with NO deficiency is returned unchanged (a genuinely-complete run stays green). The
936	    score can only be lowered, never raised — this is a faithfulness-neutral display recompute.
937	    """
938	    green_floor = green_quality_floor()
939	    abort_ceiling = _env_float(_ENV_ABORT_QUALITY_CEILING, _DEFAULT_ABORT_QUALITY_CEILING)
940	    score = max(0.0, min(1.0, float(coverage_fraction)))
941	    if empty_required_slots > 0:
942	        # Below green, scaled by how many required slots are empty (bounded so the abort ceiling
943	        # below can still bind lower). One empty slot alone already drops the run out of green.
944	        below_green = max(0.0, green_floor - 0.01)
945	        score = min(score, below_green / (1 + empty_required_slots))
946	    if eval_gate_aborted:
947	        score = min(score, abort_ceiling)
948	    return round(score, 3)
949	
950	
951	def apply_honest_scorecard_to_manifest(manifest: dict) -> dict:
952	    """Re-derive ``release_disclosure.release_quality_score`` in-place to reflect deficiencies (U26).
953	
954	    Reads the sibling manifest signals — ``evaluator_gate_advisory.gate_class`` and
955	    ``required_entity_coverage`` (total_required vs verified) — and lowers the displayed score
956	    when a deficiency is present. A no-op (byte-identical) when there is no ``release_disclosure``
957	    (the legacy always-release-OFF path), when the D8 judge did not adjudicate (the honest
958	    "N/A (D8 unadjudicated)" display is preserved), or when no deficiency applies. Presentation
959	    only: no gate / verdict / verified flag is changed. Returns the (mutated) manifest.
960	    """
961	    rd = manifest.get("release_disclosure")
962	    if not isinstance(rd, dict) or "release_quality_score" not in rd:
963	        return manifest
964	    # Never re-green a run the judge never scored — its display is already the honest N/A string.
965	    if not rd.get("adjudicated", False):
966	        return manifest
967	
968	    eval_adv = manifest.get("evaluator_gate_advisory") or {}
969	    eval_aborted = str(eval_adv.get("gate_class", "")).strip().lower() == "abort"
970	
971	    req_cov = manifest.get("required_entity_coverage") or {}
972	    try:
973	        total_required = int(req_cov.get("total_required", 0) or 0)
974	        verified = int(req_cov.get("verified", 0) or 0)
975	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
976	        total_required = verified = 0
977	    empty_required_slots = max(0, total_required - verified)
978	
979	    if not eval_aborted and empty_required_slots == 0:
980	        return manifest  # no deficiency — leave the score untouched.
981	
982	    try:
983	        base = float(rd.get("release_quality_score") or 0.0)
984	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
985	        base = 0.0
986	    honest = honest_release_quality_score(
987	        base,
988	        eval_gate_aborted=eval_aborted,
989	        empty_required_slots=empty_required_slots,
990	    )
991	    if honest < base:
992	        rd["release_quality_score"] = honest
993	        rd["release_quality_score_display"] = f"{honest:.3f}"
994	        rd["release_quality_deficiency"] = {
995	            "eval_gate_aborted": eval_aborted,
996	            "empty_required_slots": empty_required_slots,
997	            "base_coverage_fraction": round(base, 3),
998	            "green_floor": green_quality_floor(),
999	        }
1000	    return manifest
1001

content.text: This file is clean domain vocabulary - "sentinel_adapter" I'll check later. Continue to next files.

content.content:
1	"""Post-gate report.md reconciliation against the 4-role D8 verdicts (I-beatboth-fix-000 #1171).
2	
3	THE FAITHFULNESS LEAK this module closes
4	----------------------------------------
5	``scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py`` assembles ``report.md`` from strict_verify-KEPT
6	sentences (L5780) BEFORE the authoritative 4-role D8 seam runs (L6407+). The seam
7	re-judges every kept sentence with the stronger Mirror/Sentinel/Judge stack and can
8	flip a strict_verify-kept sentence to a non-VERIFIED verdict
9	(UNSUPPORTED / FABRICATED / UNREACHABLE / PARTIAL at ANY severity). Today the runner
10	consumes that verdict ONLY as a manifest flag (``release_allowed=False`` +
11	``needs_rewrite``); it NEVER reconciles the assembled ``report.md`` against the
12	verdicts. So a sentence the strongest verifier rejected still ships as asserted prose.
13	
14	drb_90 proof: ``needs_rewrite`` carried 7 material UNSUPPORTED claim_ids and at least
15	six of those sentences (e.g. the "$27,874 per violation" penalty figure, the
16	"UN Regulation No. 157 — ALKS" instrument line) were physically present in the shipped
17	``report.md`` body and carried-up Key Findings.
18	
19	WHAT THIS HELPER DOES (fail-closed)
20	-----------------------------------
21	For every claim whose 4-role ``final_verdict`` is non-VERIFIED, it locates the
22	claim's verbatim sentence in the rendered ``report.md`` and REPLACES it with the
23	existing visible gap language ("did not survive verification; curator-actionable gap").
24	Redaction is SEVERITY-INDEPENDENT (I-faith-003 #1174): a claim is redacted iff its verdict
25	is non-VERIFIED, at ANY severity. S3 "observe-only" governs the release LATCH only
26	(release_policy.py) — it never exempts an unverified claim from redaction.
27	
28	It is REFUSE-IN-PLACE, not a generative rewrite: no new spend, no new claims. The
29	redaction IS the "one rewrite/refuse-in-place attempt" the D8 policy docstring designs
30	but the runner never wired.
31	
32	The mapping is NOT 1:1 with strict_verify-kept (run_honest_sweep_r3.py:5660-5669 in-tree
33	caveat: downstream dedup/repair passes mutate the body). THREE TIERS of locating the prose
34	(I-redact-001 #1181 — high-recall detection, high-precision action):
35	  * TIER 1 (precise, single span): the claim's prose IS one discrete rendered sentence
36	    span (coverage >= ``_MIN_REDACTION_COVERAGE``) -> redact exactly that span, leaving
37	    every VERIFIED neighbor sentence and its [N] markers byte-for-byte (the leak fix).
38	  * TIER 2 (minimal containing unit): the claim's normalized prose is unambiguously
39	    PRESENT but it does not cover one span at the coverage floor — because a boundary
40	    under-split merged it with a VERIFIED neighbor, OR it straddles >=2 rendered spans.
41	    Redact the SMALLEST set of consecutive spans (or, only if no span set bounds it, the
42	    body line) whose concatenation contains the stem — over-redact SAFELY rather than
43	    abort (issue acceptance 1+2). The cross-line projection makes table/line-join
44	    rendering visible so this is never a silent ``already_absent``.
45	  * TIER 3 (genuinely absent): the claim's prose is genuinely ABSENT from the rendered
46	    report (downstream dedup/repair removed it) -> already not shipped; record
47	    ``already_absent`` and ship nothing extra (the SAFE state).
48	FAIL-CLOSED contract (I-redact-001 #1181): the helper RAISES ``ReportRedactionError`` ONLY
49	when a material non-VERIFIED claim's normalized prose is genuinely ABSENT from the normalized
50	report yet a real inconsistency demands a fail-closed abort — i.e. neither TIER-1 nor TIER-2
51	can bound the prose AND the prose is not cleanly absent. A merely hard-to-pin-to-one-sentence
52	claim is redacted via TIER 2, NOT aborted. The boundary hardening (``_SENTENCE_BOUNDARY_RE``,
53	I-redact-001) splits ``...risk[1] Cereal...`` / ``...adherence.[16] 87.3%...`` so the common
54	case resolves at TIER 1; TIER 2 is the defense-in-depth for any future under-split shape.
55	
56	MULTI-OCCURRENCE (I-redact-001 #1181, Codex iter-1 P1-1): the same non-VERIFIED stem can appear
57	more than once (e.g. once cleanly + once in an under-split merge). The tiered redaction LOOPS
58	per claim until the stem is absent from the line-join-normalized whole body (no occurrence left)
59	or no tier makes further progress (fail-closed) — so a second occurrence can never leak. The
60	claim is recorded in ``redacted`` exactly ONCE regardless of how many physical spans were removed.
61	
62	Pure function (string ops only; no network, no I/O). The CALLER reads/writes report.md.
63	"""
64	
65	from __future__ import annotations
66	
67	import os
68	import re
69	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
70	
71	# WS-5 FIX-b (I-deepfix-001): the effect-size conditional/threshold guard lives in the additive
72	# overstatement_guard leaf module (os/re only — no polaris imports, so no cycle). Imported lazily
73	# would also work; a module-level import is safe and keeps the annotate path self-describing.
74	from src.polaris_graph.generator.overstatement_guard import (
75	    effect_size_conditional_reason,
76	    figure_consistency_annotate_enabled,
77	)
78	
79	# REDACTION IS SEVERITY-INDEPENDENT (I-faith-003 #1174). Any claim the 4-role seam did NOT
80	# mark VERIFIED — PARTIAL/UNSUPPORTED/FABRICATED/UNREACHABLE — must not ship as asserted prose,
81	# regardless of severity. Severity ("S0".."S3") governs the RELEASE LATCH in release_policy.py
82	# (a non-required-entity S3 claim does not BLOCK release), NEVER this redaction path: previously
83	# the S3 "observe-only" scope guard wrongly let 26 UNSUPPORTED claims (incl. clinical-safety
84	# guidance) ship across the 5 beat-both questions (BB5-F01). VERIFIED survives; every other
85	# verdict is a leak if it ships as prose.
86	_NON_VERIFIED_VERDICTS = frozenset(
87	    {"UNSUPPORTED", "FABRICATED", "UNREACHABLE", "PARTIAL"}
88	)
89	_VERDICT_VERIFIED = "VERIFIED"
90	
91	# The visible gap sentence a redacted claim is replaced with. Mirrors the existing gap
92	# language already used by contract_section_runner ("did not survive strict
93	# verification; curator-actionable gap.") so a redacted body reads consistently.
94	_GAP_REPLACEMENT = (
95	    "A claim previously stated here did not survive 4-role verification and was "
96	    "redacted; this is a curator-actionable gap."
97	)
98	
99	# gaps.json kind for a post-gate redaction (mirrors release_policy._GAP_RESIDUAL_UNSUPPORTED
100	# vocabulary but is distinct so the audit trail shows the redaction happened in report.md).
101	GAP_KIND_REDACTED_UNSUPPORTED = "redacted_unsupported"
102	
103	# POLARIS provenance token: [#ev:evidence_id:start-end].
104	_PROVENANCE_TOKEN_RE = re.compile(r"\[#ev:[^\]]+\]")
105	# Rendered numbered citation marker: [1], [12]. The final report converts each provenance
106	# token into one of these, so the stem matcher must ignore them on BOTH sides.
107	_NUMBERED_MARKER_RE = re.compile(r"\[\d+\]")
108	# I-perm-005 (#1199): the annotator's appended confidence label. Stripped in the stem so an
109	# already-annotated sentence still matches its claim stem at the coverage floor (annotate is
110	# idempotent). The redactor never sees this marker (annotate XOR reconcile per PG_ALWAYS_RELEASE),
111	# so stripping it is a no-op on the redaction path.
112	_CONFIDENCE_MARKER_RE = re.compile(r"\s*\[confidence:[^\]]*\]")
113	_WHITESPACE_RE = re.compile(r"\s+")
114	# WS-5 FIX-a (I-deepfix-001): the provenance token carried by a claim's AUDIT sentence, used to
115	# re-key the confidence annotation on SPAN identity so a flagged claim's byte/figure-twin (bound
116	# to a DIFFERENT claim_id) inherits the caveat. Token form: [#ev:<evidence_id>:<start>-<end>].
117	_SPAN_IDENTITY_RE = re.compile(r"\[#ev:([^:\]]+):(\d+)-(\d+)\]")
118	# A numeric figure (integer or decimal) — links a flagged claim to a VERIFIED twin that re-lifts
119	# the SAME figure from the SAME cited span (surgical: same-span prose that repeats no flagged
120	# figure is NOT caveated).
121	_FIGURE_TOKEN_RE = re.compile(r"\d+(?:\.\d+)?")
122	# F10(b) (I-arch-004 A3): interior whitespace-before-punctuation, collapsed in
123	# _normalize so the redaction stem matches the SHIPPED form. The resolver runs
124	# `re.sub(r"\s+([.!?,;])", r"\1", ...)` on every sentence before it renders, so
125	# `word .` in the audit_map sentence ships as `word.`; the redactor must apply
126	# the same collapse or the stem misses the collapsed sentence. Same punctuation
127	# set as the resolver (provenance_generator).
128	_INTERIOR_SPACE_BEFORE_PUNCT_RE = re.compile(r"\s+([.!?,;])")
129	
130	# A4 (I-deepfix-001) — the inline attribution-origin caveat marker appended to a
131	# mis-attributed sentence in the VISIBLE report (see ``annotate_report_attribution_origin``
132	# below). It is a bracketed disclosure with no terminal period (so a sentence splitter never
133	# treats it as a new sentence), form: ``[attribution unverified — the cited source does not
134	# appear to be <ACTOR>; ...]``. Stripped inside ``_normalize`` so a caveated sentence still
135	# matches its own claim stem at the coverage floor for the redactor / annotator, both of which
136	# run AFTER the render-seam caveat pass. Additive: a sentence WITHOUT the caveat is byte-unchanged.
137	_ATTRIBUTION_CAVEAT_RE = re.compile(r"\s*\[attribution unverified[^\]]*\]")
138	
139	
140	class ReportRedactionError(RuntimeError):
141	    """Raised when a material non-VERIFIED claim is present-but-unlocatable for a discrete
142	    rendered-sentence replacement. The caller MUST fail closed (do NOT ship the report).
143	    """
144	
145	
146	@dataclass
147	class RedactedClaim:
148	    """One claim whose verbatim sentence was removed from report.md (-> gaps.json)."""
149	
150	    claim_id: str
151	    severity: str
152	    verdict: str
153	    claim_text: str  # the verbatim sentence (with its original provenance token)
154	    # I-wire-015 #1337 R5: "claim" = removed at span/line granularity (TIER 1/2). "section" = the
155	    # claim was un-boundable to a span/line, so its whole markdown section was withheld (TIER 4)
156	    # rather than aborting the entire report. Either way the unsupported claim NEVER ships.
157	    redaction_scope: str = "claim"
158	
159	
160	@dataclass
161	class RedactionResult:
162	    """Outcome of reconciling report.md against the 4-role verdicts."""
163	
164	    report_text: str
165	    redacted: list[RedactedClaim] = field(default_factory=list)
166	    # Material non-VERIFIED claims whose prose was already absent from the rendered report
167	    # (downstream dedup/repair had removed them) — recorded for the audit trail, not a leak.
168	    already_absent: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
169	
170	    @property
171	    def redacted_count(self) -> int:
172	        return len(self.redacted)
173	
174	    def gaps_json(self) -> list[dict]:
175	        """Serialize the redactions as gaps.json entries (one per removed claim)."""
176	        return [
177	            {
178	                "ref": rc.claim_id,
179	                "kind": GAP_KIND_REDACTED_UNSUPPORTED,
180	                "severity": rc.severity,
181	                # I-wire-015 #1337 R5: machine-readable scope ("claim" = span/line redaction;
182	                # "section" = the whole section was withheld because the claim was un-boundable to a
183	                # smaller unit). Lets a consumer count/triage section-level withholds programmatically.
184	                "redaction_scope": rc.redaction_scope,
185	                "note": (
186	                    f"claim re-judged {rc.verdict} by the 4-role seam and removed from "
187	                    "report.md (refuse-in-place); curator-actionable gap"
188	                    + (
189	                        "; SECTION withheld (claim was un-boundable to a span/line — "
190	                        "the whole section was conservatively withheld, not the whole report)"
191	                        if rc.redaction_scope == "section" else ""
192	                    )
193	                ),
194	            }
195	            for rc in self.redacted
196	        ]
197	
198	
199	def _prose_stem(text: str) -> str:
200	    """Strip provenance tokens + numbered citation markers, leaving the bare claim prose.
201	
202	    (The annotator's confidence marker is stripped LOCALLY in the annotator path only — never here —
203	    so the shared redaction normalization is byte-unchanged; Codex slice-2 P0-2.)"""
204	    text = _PROVENANCE_TOKEN_RE.sub("", text)
205	    text = _NUMBERED_MARKER_RE.sub("", text)
206	    return text
207	
208	
209	def _normalize(text: str) -> str:
210	    """Citation-insensitive, whitespace-insensitive prose for matching across the
211	    provenance-token -> numbered-marker render shift. Trailing punctuation stripped.
212	
213	    F10(b) (I-arch-004 A3): ALSO collapse interior whitespace-BEFORE-punctuation
214	    (``word .`` -> ``word.``), mirroring the resolver's own normalization
215	    (provenance_generator: ``re.sub(r"\\s+([.!?,;])", r"\\1", stripped)``). The
216	    audit_map sentence (the pre-resolve ``sv.sentence``) can carry ``word .``
217	    interior spacing that the resolver collapses before the sentence SHIPS as
218	    ``word.``; without this collapse the redaction stem would not match the
219	    shipped form and a present-but-collapsed non-VERIFIED claim was wrongly
220	    recorded ``already_absent`` (a silent leak — 07-001 / 06-006 interior-collapse
221	    case). This applies the SAME collapse to both stem and rendered side, so the
222	    match is consistent and idempotent; it never relaxes a match, only adds one.
223	    """
224	    text = _prose_stem(text)
225	    # A4 (I-deepfix-001): also strip the inline attribution-origin caveat so a sentence that
226	    # received the visible caveat still matches its claim stem at the coverage floor for the
227	    # redactor / annotator (both run AFTER the render-seam caveat pass). Additive — a sentence
228	    # without the caveat is byte-unchanged; nothing else in the pipeline emits this marker.
229	    text = _ATTRIBUTION_CAVEAT_RE.sub("", text)
230	    text = _WHITESPACE_RE.sub(" ", text)
231	    text = _INTERIOR_SPACE_BEFORE_PUNCT_RE.sub(r"\1", text)
232	    return text.strip().rstrip(".").strip()
233	
234	
235	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
236	# WS-5 (I-deepfix-001) — figure-consistency re-key helpers (annotate path only)
237	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
238	def _span_identities(sentence: str) -> set[tuple[str, int, int]]:
239	    """The (evidence_id, start, end) provenance tuples a claim's AUDIT sentence carries."""
240	    return {
241	        (m.group(1), int(m.group(2)), int(m.group(3)))
242	        for m in _SPAN_IDENTITY_RE.finditer(sentence or "")
243	    }
244	
245	
246	def _figure_tokens(sentence: str) -> set[str]:
247	    """Numeric figures in a claim's prose, EXCLUDING the digits inside its provenance token /
248	    numbered citation markers (a span offset like ``0-800`` is not a claimed figure)."""
249	    prose = _SPAN_IDENTITY_RE.sub("", sentence or "")
250	    prose = _NUMBERED_MARKER_RE.sub("", prose)
251	    return set(_FIGURE_TOKEN_RE.findall(prose))
252	
253	
254	def reconcile_report_against_verdicts(
255	    report_text: str,
256	    final_verdicts: dict[str, str],
257	    audit_map: dict[str, dict],
258	) -> RedactionResult:
259	    """Remove every non-VERIFIED claim's verbatim sentence from ``report_text``.
260	
261	    Redaction is severity-independent (I-faith-003 #1174): a claim is redacted iff its
262	    4-role verdict is not VERIFIED, at ANY severity (S0..S3). Severity governs the release
263	    latch in release_policy.py, never this redaction path.
264	
265	    Args:
266	      report_text: the assembled report.md content.
267	      final_verdicts: claim_id -> 4-role final verdict (UNSUPPORTED/VERIFIED/...).
268	      audit_map: claim_id -> {"sentence": <verbatim>, "severity": <S0..S3>, ...}
269	                 (the persisted four_role_claim_audit.json structure).
270	
271	    Returns a RedactionResult with the redacted text + per-claim records.
272	
273	    Raises:
274	      ReportRedactionError — fail closed when a material non-VERIFIED claim's prose is
275	      present in the report but cannot be pinned to a discrete sentence to replace.
276	    """
277	    result = RedactionResult(report_text=report_text)
278	    working = report_text
279	
280	    for claim_id, verdict in sorted(final_verdicts.items()):
281	        meta = audit_map.get(claim_id)
282	        if meta is None:
283	            # A verdict with no audit row is itself a fail-closed condition: we cannot
284	            # know the claim_text to redact, and a material non-VERIFIED claim might be
285	            # shipping. Only raise for non-VERIFIED verdicts (VERIFIED never redacts).
286	            if verdict != _VERDICT_VERIFIED:
287	                raise ReportRedactionError(
288	                    f"claim {claim_id} has verdict {verdict} but no audit_map row; "
289	                    "cannot locate its sentence to reconcile (fail-closed)."
290	                )
291	            continue
292	
293	        severity = str(meta.get("severity", ""))
294	        if verdict == _VERDICT_VERIFIED:
295	            # VERIFIED survives at ANY severity. Every non-VERIFIED verdict is redacted
296	            # regardless of severity (I-faith-003 #1174): S3 "observe-only" governs the
297	            # release LATCH (release_policy.py), never this redaction path.
298	            continue
299	
300	        sentence = str(meta.get("sentence", ""))
301	        stem_norm = _normalize(sentence)
302	        if not stem_norm:
303	            # Defensive: an empty/whitespace-only claim sentence cannot be matched. A
304	            # material non-VERIFIED claim with no usable text is a fail-closed condition.
305	            raise ReportRedactionError(
306	                f"claim {claim_id} ({verdict}/{severity}) has empty claim_text; "
307	                "cannot reconcile (fail-closed)."
308	            )
309	
310	        # MULTI-OCCURRENCE LOOP (I-redact-001 #1181, Codex iter-1 P1-1): a non-VERIFIED stem can
311	        # appear MORE THAN ONCE in the body — e.g. once cleanly (TIER 1 catches it) and once in a
312	        # boundary-under-split span (only TIER 2 catches it). The prior single-pass logic marked
313	        # the claim handled after the FIRST successful removal and `continue`d, leaving the second
314	        # occurrence in result.report_text — a leak. We now loop the tiered redaction until the
315	        # stem is ABSENT from the line-join-normalized whole-body projection (no occurrence left)
316	        # OR no tier can make further progress (-> fail-closed). The loop terminates because each
317	        # successful removal replaces the matched prose with ``_GAP_REPLACEMENT`` (which cannot
318	        # re-match the stem), so occurrences are consumed monotonically.
319	        redacted_any_for_claim = False
320	        section_withheld_for_claim = False  # I-wire-015 #1337 R5: a TIER-4 section withhold fired
321	        while _prose_present(working, stem_norm):
322	            # TIER 1 — precise single-span redaction (coverage floor protects VERIFIED neighbors).
323	            # One pass clears EVERY clean (pin-at-floor) occurrence across all lines at once.
324	            removed, working = _redact_sentence(working, stem_norm)
325	            if removed:
326	                redacted_any_for_claim = True
327	                continue
328	
329	            # TIER 1 made no progress this pass, but the prose IS still present (line-join
330	            # projection): it did not pin to one span at the coverage floor (a boundary
331	            # under-split merged it with a VERIFIED neighbor, OR it straddles spans). TIER 2 —
332	            # redact the minimal CONTAINING unit, over-redacting SAFELY rather than aborting a
333	            # present-but-hard-to-pin claim (#1181). TIER 2 removes ONE under-split unit per pass;
334	            # the loop re-checks presence and keeps going for any further occurrence.
335	            removed, working = _redact_minimal_containing_unit(working, stem_norm)
336	            if removed:
337	                redacted_any_for_claim = True
338	                continue
339	
340	            # TIER 4 (I-wire-015 #1337 R5) — SECTION-level bounded withhold. The stem is present
341	            # (line-join projection) but neither a span (TIER 1) nor a body-line / line-run (TIER 2)
342	            # could bound it without touching a forbidden heading/bib line. Rather than abort the
343	            # WHOLE report (the legacy fail-closed below — one un-boundable chrome fragment then
344	            # collapses an otherwise-good report, the #1337 reconfirm3 failure), withhold ONLY the
345	            # markdown SECTION whose body contains the stem. The unsupported claim is FULLY removed
346	            # (faithfulness preserved — it NEVER ships); only that one section's coverage is lost
347	            # (disclosed in gaps.json as redaction_scope="section"); every other section survives.
348	            # This is the next-coarser bounded unit ABOVE TIER-2's line-run, so it only fires when
349	            # TIER-2 already could not bound the stem. Default-ON; env-reversible to the legacy abort.
350	            if _section_level_fallback_enabled():
351	                removed, working = _redact_containing_section(working, stem_norm)
352	                if removed:
353	                    redacted_any_for_claim = True
354	                    section_withheld_for_claim = True
355	                    continue
356	
357	            # FAIL-CLOSED (true last resort): the prose registers as present in the line-join
358	            # projection but could not be bounded to a span, a body line/line-run, OR a section
359	            # (e.g. it exists ONLY inside a heading/bibliography line, or straddles a section
360	            # boundary). A real inconsistency — refuse to ship a partial report (#1174); never
361	            # present an unsupported claim as fact. Raising here (not after the loop) means we never
362	            # spin: no progress + still present == abort.
363	            raise ReportRedactionError(
364	                f"claim {claim_id} ({verdict}/{severity}) prose is present in report.md "
365	                "but could not be bounded to any redactable unit (span set, body line, or section); "
366	                "refusing to ship a partially-reconciled report (fail-closed). "
367	                f"prose_stem={stem_norm[:120]!r}"
368	            )
369	
370	        # Record the claim ONCE after the loop (Codex iter-1 P1-1: appending per removal would
371	        # double-count a claim that occurred twice and inflate redacted_count). If the loop body
372	        # never ran, the prose was genuinely ABSENT from the start — the SAFE state (TIER 3).
373	        if redacted_any_for_claim:
374	            result.redacted.append(
375	                RedactedClaim(
376	                    claim_id=claim_id,
377	                    severity=severity,
378	                    verdict=verdict,
379	                    claim_text=sentence,
380	                    redaction_scope=("section" if section_withheld_for_claim else "claim"),
381	                )
382	            )
383	        else:
384	            result.already_absent.append(claim_id)  # TIER 3 — genuinely absent, ship nothing
385	
386	    result.report_text = working
387	    return result
388	
389	
390	def _redact_sentence(report_text: str, stem_norm: str) -> tuple[bool, str]:
391	    """Replace each rendered sentence whose normalized prose contains ``stem_norm`` with the
392	    visible gap language. Returns (redacted_any, new_text).
393	
394	    Matching is done at the rendered-sentence granularity: each body line is split into
395	    sentence spans (each span KEEPS its own trailing citation markers), normalized (citation +
396	    whitespace insensitive), and a span is redacted iff its normalized prose CONTAINS the claim
397	    stem with sufficient coverage. Every NON-matching sentence — and its [N] markers — is
398	    preserved byte-for-byte, so redacting one sentence never strips citations off its VERIFIED
399	    neighbors (Codex iter-1 P1). Robust to the provenance-token -> [N] render shift; never
400	    matches across unrelated sentences.
401	    """
402	    redacted_any = False
403	    out_lines: list[str] = []
404	    for line in report_text.split("\n"):
405	        # Never touch headings, bibliography rows, or already-gap lines.
406	        stripped = line.lstrip()
407	        if stripped.startswith("#") or stripped.startswith("[") or not stripped:
408	            out_lines.append(line)
409	            continue
410	        new_line, hit = _redact_line(line, stem_norm)
411	        redacted_any = redacted_any or hit
412	        out_lines.append(new_line)
413	    return redacted_any, "\n".join(out_lines)
414	
415	
416	# A rendered-sentence boundary: a terminator [.!?], then any run of the sentence's OWN
417	# trailing citation markers ([N], optionally space-separated), then the inter-sentence
418	# whitespace, then the start of the next sentence. The trailing markers stay attached to the
419	# sentence they cite (the LEFT side) — they are NOT consumed as a separator.
420	#
421	# Codex iter-1 P1 fix: the prior `re.split(r"(?<=[.!?])(?:\[\d+\])?\s+...")` discarded the
422	# boundary marker (it was part of the matched, dropped separator), so redacting one sentence
423	# stripped the [8]/[4] citation markers off its VERIFIED neighbors on the same line and turned
424	# cited claims into uncited prose. The real drb_90 case: the VERIFIED 05-000 sentence renders
425	# "...crashes.[8]" immediately before the UNSUPPORTED 05-001 sentence — the [8] belongs to
426	# 05-000 and must survive redaction of 05-001.
427	#
428	# I-redact-001 #1181 — three ALTERNATION ARMS so a real boundary is not under-split (the
429	# under-split merged a non-VERIFIED sentence with a VERIFIED neighbor into one over-long span,
430	# dropping coverage below the floor and forcing a whole-report abort). Each arm still demands
431	# inter-sentence whitespace + a sentence-start, so decimals/abbreviations never become a split:
432	#   ARM 1 (terminator, sentence-start char): "...crashes.[8] The..." — the original behavior.
433	#   ARM 2 (terminator + >=1 marker, digit-start next): "...adherence.[16] 87.3% of..." — the
434	#          next sentence begins with a digit (defeating ARM 1's [A-Z"'(#] lookahead). REQUIRES
435	#          at least one [N] marker between the period and the digit, so a bare decimal
436	#          "0.90 (0.83 to 0.97) was found." (period, space, digit, NO marker) never matches.
437	#   ARM 3 (>=1 marker as terminator, NO preceding period): "...risk[1] Cereal...",
438	#          "...recovery[17][22] Legal..." — the renderer emitted the citation marker(s) with no
439	#          terminal period before them; the marker(s) ARE the boundary. The marker stays with
440	#          the LEFT (cited) sentence (it is m.start()-anchored, then rstrip-included in the span),
441	#          so a VERIFIED neighbor keeps its [N] byte-for-byte (the Codex iter-1 P1 invariant).
442	#          Codex iter-1 P2: ARM 3 is WORD-ANCHORED — the marker run must immediately follow a
443	#          word character ``(?<=\w)`` (NO whitespace between the sentence-final word and its
444	#          marker), so it fires only on a plausible sentence end where the renderer dropped the
445	#          terminal period ("risk[1]", "recovery[17][22]"). A MID-sentence inline citation —
446	#          "...as shown [5] In vivo..." or "the [5] Group reported..." — has WHITESPACE before
447	#          the bracket, so ``(?<=\w)`` rejects it and the inline-cited sentence stays intact (no
448	#          false split -> no over-redaction of a VERIFIED sentence). This only ever causes an
449	#          UNDER-split, which TIER 2 then bounds safely; it can never over-split a survivor.
450	# A decimal like "0.457" or "No. 157" or "U.S." is never a boundary: ARM 1/2 require whitespace
451	# before the next char and ARM 2 requires an intervening [N] marker; "U.S. products" splits only
452	# if "products" were uppercase (it is not), and "No. 157" has no marker so ARM 2 cannot fire.
453	# A4 (I-deepfix-001) boundary-safety (Codex P1): the inline attribution-origin caveat
454	# (``annotate_report_attribution_origin`` below) is appended AFTER a sentence's terminator +
455	# citation markers and carries NO terminator of its own. Without help the splitter could not find
456	# the boundary AFTER the caveat, so the caveated sentence and the NEXT grounded sentence would fuse
457	# into one span and a later D8 redaction could over-remove the grounded neighbor. Each arm therefore
458	# consumes an OPTIONAL trailing attribution caveat as attached material of the PRECEDING sentence, so
459	# the split lands AFTER the caveat and the next sentence stays a clean, separate span. Purely
460	# additive: the optional group matches empty for caveat-free text, so a report that carries no
461	# attribution caveat segments byte-identically to before.
462	_ATTRIBUTION_CAVEAT_TRAILER = r"(?:\s*\[attribution unverified[^\]]*\])?"
463	_SENTENCE_BOUNDARY_RE = re.compile(
464	    r"(?:"
465	    r"[.!?](?:\s*\[\d+\])*" + _ATTRIBUTION_CAVEAT_TRAILER + r"\s+(?=[A-Z\"'(#])"   # ARM 1: terminator -> sentence-start char
466	    r"|[.!?](?:\s*\[\d+\])+" + _ATTRIBUTION_CAVEAT_TRAILER + r"\s+(?=\d)"           # ARM 2: terminator + marker -> digit-start
467	    r"|(?<=\w)(?:\[\d+\])+" + _ATTRIBUTION_CAVEAT_TRAILER + r"\s+(?=[A-Z\"'(#0-9])"  # ARM 3: WORD-ATTACHED marker-as-terminator, no period
468	    r")"
469	)
470	
471	# The claim stem must cover at least this fraction of a rendered sentence to redact it. Guards
472	# against a short non-VERIFIED claim whose normalized prose is a substring of a LONGER VERIFIED
473	# sentence wrongly redacting that survivor (over-redaction). Internal matching-precision floor,
474	# named per §9.4 (no magic numbers) — not an operator-tunable parameter.
475	_MIN_REDACTION_COVERAGE = 0.6
476	
477	
478	def _sentence_spans(line: str) -> list[tuple[int, int]]:
479	    """(start, end) spans over ``line``, one per rendered sentence, each span INCLUDING the
480	    sentence's trailing citation markers. The gaps between spans are pure inter-sentence
481	    whitespace, which the caller preserves verbatim — so a redaction never disturbs a
482	    neighbor's text or markers.
483	    """
484	    spans: list[tuple[int, int]] = []
485	    start = 0
486	    for m in _SENTENCE_BOUNDARY_RE.finditer(line):
487	        # m.group() == terminator + markers + trailing whitespace; the sentence (with its
488	        # markers) ends where that trailing whitespace begins.
489	        sentence_end = m.start() + len(m.group().rstrip())
490	        spans.append((start, sentence_end))
491	        start = m.end()  # next sentence begins after the inter-sentence whitespace
492	    spans.append((start, len(line)))
493	    return spans
494	
495	
496	def _sentence_matches_stem(sentence: str, stem_norm: str) -> bool:
497	    """A rendered sentence IS the rejected claim iff its normalized prose contains the claim
498	    stem AND the stem covers >= _MIN_REDACTION_COVERAGE of it (no short-substring false hit)."""
499	    sent_norm = _normalize(sentence)
500	    if not sent_norm or stem_norm not in sent_norm:
501	        return False
502	    return len(stem_norm) >= _MIN_REDACTION_COVERAGE * len(sent_norm)
503	
504	
505	def _redact_line(line: str, stem_norm: str) -> tuple[str, bool]:
506	    """Redact matching rendered sentences within a single body line, byte-preserving EVERY
507	    non-matching sentence (its exact text + citation markers) and all inter-sentence
508	    whitespace. Only the matching sentence span (including its own trailing markers) is
509	    replaced with the gap language.
510	    """
511	    # Fast reject: if the normalized line does not contain the stem, nothing to do.
512	    if stem_norm not in _normalize(line):
513	        return line, False
514	
515	    out: list[str] = []
516	    cursor = 0
517	    hit = False
518	    for (start, end) in _sentence_spans(line):
519	        out.append(line[cursor:start])  # inter-sentence whitespace, verbatim
520	        sentence = line[start:end]
521	        if _sentence_matches_stem(sentence, stem_norm):
522	            out.append(_GAP_REPLACEMENT)
523	            hit = True
524	        else:
525	            out.append(sentence)  # byte-identical — markers intact
526	        cursor = end
527	    out.append(line[cursor:])
528	    return "".join(out), hit
529	
530	
531	def _is_redactable_body_line(line: str) -> bool:
532	    """A body line the redactor is allowed to rewrite: NOT a heading (#…), a bibliography /
533	    already-gap row ([…), or blank. Mirrors the skip in ``_redact_sentence`` so TIER-2 honors
534	    the exact same no-touch set (a claim that exists ONLY inside a heading stays a fail-closed
535	    inconsistency, not a TIER-2 redaction — preserving the present-but-unlocatable contract).
536	    """
537	    stripped = line.lstrip()
538	    if not stripped or stripped.startswith("#"):
539	        return False
540	    # I-wire-015 #1337: a genuine bibliography reference row starts with a SINGLE citation marker
541	    # then the reference ("[12] Autor, D. (2015). ...") and must NOT be redacted. But a BODY line can
542	    # begin with the WRAPPED trailing citations of the previous sentence ("[71][5][7][6] Further
543	    # research is needed..." — 2+ consecutive leading markers) — that is CONTENT, not a reference, and
544	    # MUST stay redactable, else an unsupported claim rendered on it cannot be bounded by ANY tier and
545	    # collapses the whole report (the #1337 reconfirm3 failure: the un-VERIFIED claim 05-041 rendered
546	    # on a "[71][5][7][6] ..." line). So only a SINGLE leading marker (a real reference row) is
547	    # protected; 2+ consecutive leading markers mark a redactable wrapped-body line.
548	    if stripped.startswith("["):
549	        return bool(re.match(r"(?:\[\d+\]){2,}", stripped))
550	    return True
551	
552	
553	def _prose_present(report_text: str, stem_norm: str) -> bool:
554	    """High-RECALL presence detector over a LINE-JOIN-NORMALIZED projection of the whole body
555	    (research practice: cross-line / table rendering is a blind spot — never silently treat a
556	    line-straddling claim as already-absent). Returns True iff the claim stem is normalized-
557	    present either within one body line OR across the join of consecutive redactable body lines.
558	
559	    Recall here only guards against a FALSE ``already_absent`` (a leak); the high-PRECISION
560	    decision of WHICH unit to remove stays in ``_redact_minimal_containing_unit`` (exact
561	    normalized containment + the coverage floor), so this projection never itself redacts.
562	    """
563	    if stem_norm in _normalize(report_text):
564	        return True
565	    # Cross-line projection: join only the redactable body lines (skip headings / bib / blanks),
566	    # normalized, and look for the stem straddling a soft line break.
567	    body = " ".join(
568	        line for line in report_text.split("\n") if _is_redactable_body_line(line)
569	    )
570	    return stem_norm in _normalize(body)
571	
572	
573	def _redact_minimal_containing_unit(report_text: str, stem_norm: str) -> tuple[bool, str]:
574	    """TIER-2 fallback (#1181): the stem is present but did NOT pin to one span at the coverage
575	    floor. Remove the SMALLEST containing unit, over-redacting SAFELY rather than aborting:
576	
577	      1. Within a single redactable body line, find the smallest set of CONSECUTIVE spans whose
578	         concatenation (normalized) contains the stem, and replace exactly that span set with one
579	         gap sentence — preserving every OTHER span (and its [N] markers) and all inter-span
580	         whitespace byte-for-byte. This handles both the boundary-under-split residue and a TRUE
581	         multi-span straddle (issue acceptance 1+2) with the same walk.
582	      2. If no within-line consecutive-span set bounds the stem (it straddles a soft line break),
583	         replace the smallest run of consecutive redactable body lines whose join contains the
584	         stem with one gap line — the coarsest safe unit.
585	
586	    Returns (redacted_any, new_text). Returns (False, unchanged) iff the stem cannot be bounded
587	    by any redactable unit (the caller then fails closed). NEVER touches a heading/bib line.
588	    """
589	    lines = report_text.split("\n")
590	
591	    # ---- 1) within-line minimal consecutive-span set -------------------------------------
592	    for idx, line in enumerate(lines):
593	        if not _is_redactable_body_line(line):
594	            continue
595	        if stem_norm not in _normalize(line):
596	            continue
597	        spans = _sentence_spans(line)
598	        span_set = _minimal_consecutive_span_set(line, spans, stem_norm)
599	        if span_set is not None:
600	            lo, hi = span_set  # inclusive span indices
601	            start = spans[lo][0]
602	            end = spans[hi][1]
603	            new_line = line[:start] + _GAP_REPLACEMENT + line[end:]
604	            lines[idx] = new_line
605	            return True, "\n".join(lines)
606	
607	    # ---- 2) cross-line: smallest consecutive redactable-body-line run --------------------
608	    redactable_idx = [i for i, ln in enumerate(lines) if _is_redactable_body_line(lines[i])]
609	    for span_len in range(1, len(redactable_idx) + 1):
610	        for offset in range(0, len(redactable_idx) - span_len + 1):
611	            window = redactable_idx[offset : offset + span_len]
612	            # The window must be CONTIGUOUS in the body-line sequence to be one rendered unit.
613	            if window[-1] - window[0] != span_len - 1:
614	                continue
615	            joined = " ".join(lines[i] for i in window)
616	            if stem_norm in _normalize(joined):
617	                first = window[0]
618	                # Replace the first line of the run with the gap; blank the remaining lines so the
619	                # leak prose is fully removed while preserving the line count (no structural churn).
620	                lines[first] = _GAP_REPLACEMENT
621	                for i in window[1:]:
622	                    lines[i] = ""
623	                return True, "\n".join(lines)
624	
625	    return False, report_text
626	
627	
628	def _minimal_consecutive_span_set(
629	    line: str, spans: list[tuple[int, int]], stem_norm: str
630	) -> tuple[int, int] | None:
631	    """Smallest (lo, hi) inclusive index window over ``spans`` whose concatenated source text is
632	    normalized-containing ``stem_norm``. Grows the window by length so the FIRST hit is minimal;
633	    prefers the smallest unit (precision: do not nuke a section when a span-pair suffices)."""
634	    n = len(spans)
635	    for window in range(1, n + 1):
636	        for lo in range(0, n - window + 1):
637	            hi = lo + window - 1
638	            seg = line[spans[lo][0] : spans[hi][1]]
639	            if stem_norm in _normalize(seg):
640	                return (lo, hi)
641	    return None
642	
643	
644	def _section_level_fallback_enabled() -> bool:
645	    """I-wire-015 #1337 R5: gate for the TIER-4 SECTION-level withhold (DEFAULT-ON). ON => an
646	    unsupported claim that neither TIER-1 (span) nor TIER-2 (line/line-run) can bound withholds only
647	    its markdown section instead of aborting the whole report. OFF => reverts to the legacy
648	    whole-report fail-closed abort. Faithfulness is IDENTICAL either way — the unsupported claim
649	    NEVER ships; the flag only chooses the blast radius (one section vs the entire report)."""
650	    return os.getenv("PG_REDACT_SECTION_LEVEL_FALLBACK", "1").strip().lower() not in (
651	        "", "0", "false", "off", "no",
652	    )
653	
654	
655	def _redact_containing_section(report_text: str, stem_norm: str) -> tuple[bool, str]:
656	    """TIER-4 (#1337 R5): the smallest BOUNDED unit above a body-line run — the markdown SECTION.
657	    Used ONLY after TIER-1 (span) and TIER-2 (line / line-run) both fail to bound a PRESENT
658	    unsupported claim (e.g. it straddles a heading the redactor must not touch, or non-adjacent
659	    units). Find the section — the run of lines from one ``#``-heading up to the next heading or
660	    EOF — whose redactable BODY lines' join contains the stem, and blank that section's body (its
661	    first redactable body line -> the gap sentence, the rest -> "" to preserve the line count),
662	    keeping the heading. The unsupported claim is FULLY removed (faithfulness preserved — it NEVER
663	    ships); only that one section's coverage is lost (disclosed). Returns (False, unchanged) iff no
664	    section BODY contains the stem (it lives only in a heading/bib line, or straddles a section
665	    boundary) — the caller then fails closed (the true last resort). NEVER touches a heading/bib
666	    line's text; NEVER ships the claim.
667	    """
668	    lines = report_text.split("\n")
669	    heading_positions = [i for i, ln in enumerate(lines) if ln.lstrip().startswith("#")]
670	    if not heading_positions:
671	        return False, report_text
672	    for k, h in enumerate(heading_positions):
673	        body_start = h + 1
674	        body_end = heading_positions[k + 1] if k + 1 < len(heading_positions) else len(lines)
675	        redactable_positions = [
676	            p for p in range(body_start, body_end) if _is_redactable_body_line(lines[p])
677	        ]
678	        if not redactable_positions:
679	            continue
680	        joined = " ".join(lines[p] for p in redactable_positions)
681	        if stem_norm in _normalize(joined):
682	            # Blank the whole section body: first redactable line -> the visible gap sentence; the
683	            # remaining redactable lines -> "" (preserve line count, no structural churn). Headings,
684	            # bibliography rows, and blank lines in the section are untouched.
685	            lines[redactable_positions[0]] = _GAP_REPLACEMENT
686	            for p in redactable_positions[1:]:
687	                lines[p] = ""
688	            return True, "\n".join(lines)
689	    return False, report_text
690

693	# I-perm-005 (#1199) — ANNOTATE (keep + label), the always-release sibling of REDACT
694	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
695	@dataclass
696	class AnnotatedClaim:
697	    """One non-VERIFIED claim KEPT in report.md with an appended confidence marker."""
698	
699	    claim_id: str
700	    severity: str

700	    severity: str
701	    verdict: str
702	    marker: str

705	@dataclass
706	class AnnotationResult:
707	    """Outcome of annotating report.md against the 4-role verdicts (keep + label)."""
708	
709	    report_text: str
710	    annotated: list["AnnotatedClaim"] = field(default_factory=list)
711	    already_absent: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
712	
713	    @property
714	    def annotated_count(self) -> int:
715	        return len(self.annotated)
