717	
718	def _claim_fully_pinnable(working: str, stem_norm: str) -> bool:
719	    """True iff EVERY occurrence of ``stem_norm`` is a clean TIER-1 single-sentence span the annotator
720	    can label — checked on the MARKER-STRIPPED text using the REDACTOR's own TIER-1 locate.
721	
722	    Completeness guard for the annotator (Codex slice-2 P0-1/P0-2 + iter-2): the appended confidence
723	    marker (no terminator) can ALTER ``_sentence_spans`` segmentation, so any post-marker scan is
724	    unreliable. Instead this strips ALL confidence markers FIRST (clean segmentation, immune to the
725	    marker even across claims; LOCAL strip — shared `_prose_stem` untouched), then runs the exact
726	    same TIER-1 removal the redactor uses (``_redact_sentence``): if removing every TIER-1-pinnable
727	    occurrence leaves the stem STILL present in the line-join projection, a non-TIER-1 occurrence
728	    (a boundary-straddle / under-split) remains that the per-line annotator CANNOT cleanly label ->
729	    fail closed. This is the SAME notion of "pinnable" the annotate pass acts on.
730	    """
731	    stripped = _CONFIDENCE_MARKER_RE.sub("", working)
732	    _removed, after = _redact_sentence(stripped, stem_norm)
733	    return not _prose_present(after, stem_norm)
734	
735	
736	def annotate_report_against_verdicts(
737	    report_text: str,
738	    final_verdicts: dict[str, str],
739	    audit_map: dict[str, dict],
740	    marker_by_claim: dict[str, str],
741	    *,
742	    span_text_by_claim: dict[str, str] | None = None,
743	) -> AnnotationResult:
744	    """KEEP every non-VERIFIED claim's sentence in ``report_text`` and APPEND its confidence marker.
745	
746	    The always-release (I-perm-001) counterpart of ``reconcile_report_against_verdicts``: under the
747	    reframe a non-VERIFIED claim is shown LABELED (user judges) instead of DELETED. Severity-
748	    independent, like the redactor. ``marker_by_claim[claim_id]`` is the inline marker the caller
749	    computed from ``claim_labeler`` (a non-VERIFIED claim is always low / no-source-found — never
750	    high). A non-VERIFIED claim with no marker falls back to a generic low marker (never silently
751	    unlabeled).
752	
753	    Single-pass per claim (appending a marker does not remove the stem, so there is no
754	    multi-occurrence loop): every matching sentence span across the body is labeled exactly once.
755	
756	    Fail-closed (same safety contract as the redactor): a material non-VERIFIED claim whose prose is
757	    PRESENT but cannot be pinned to a discrete rendered sentence raises ``ReportRedactionError`` — we
758	    never ship a non-VERIFIED claim as bare unlabeled prose.
759	
760	    WS-5 FIGURE-CONSISTENCY re-key (I-deepfix-001, default ON via ``PG_FIGURE_CONSISTENCY_ANNOTATE``):
761	    the low-confidence label used to key on ``claim_id`` alone, so a flagged claim's byte/figure-twin
762	    bound to a DIFFERENT (VERIFIED) claim_id — e.g. the Eloundou "46%" re-lifted verbatim into the
763	    Conclusion — shipped CLEAN with the caveat stripped. Two ADVISORY re-keys now make every twin
764	    inherit the caveat:
765	      * FIX-a: a VERIFIED claim that shares BOTH a span-identity tuple (evidence_id,start,end) AND a
766	        numeric figure with a flagged claim inherits that flagged sibling's marker.
767	      * FIX-b: when ``span_text_by_claim`` is supplied, a claim that re-lifts a span number while
768	        dropping the span's governing conditional/threshold antecedent (``effect_size_conditional_reason``)
769	        inherits a generic low marker.
770	    Advisory claims are BEST-EFFORT and NEVER fail-closed: labelling a twin only ADDS a caveat (§-1.3),
771	    so an absent / unpinnable twin is simply not labelled (its absence is not a leak — the twin is
772	    VERIFIED and already ships). ``PG_FIGURE_CONSISTENCY_ANNOTATE=0`` (or ``span_text_by_claim=None``
773	    for FIX-b) restores byte-identical claim_id-only behaviour. This never changes a verdict, never
774	    widens a span, never removes a caveat.
775	    """
776	    # IDEMPOTENCE (Codex slice-2 iter-4 P1): strip any PRE-EXISTING confidence markers up front so a
777	    # re-run over already-annotated output segments cleanly (the no-terminator marker would otherwise
778	    # collapse two same-line sentences into one span). Markers are deterministic, so stripping then
779	    # re-labeling reproduces them exactly; a fresh report has none, so this is a byte no-op there.
780	    report_text = _CONFIDENCE_MARKER_RE.sub("", report_text)
781	
782	    # Collect every non-VERIFIED claim (validate + pinnability), THEN label in a SINGLE pass over the
783	    # ORIGINAL report (Codex slice-2 iter-3 P0): sequentially mutating `working` per claim let claim
784	    # A's appended marker (no terminator) perturb claim B's same-line segmentation, leaking B
785	    # unlabeled. Computing all sentence spans ONCE off the unmutated text removes that coupling.
786	    claims: list[tuple[str, str, str, str, str]] = []  # (claim_id, verdict, severity, stem, marker)
787	    already_absent: list[str] = []
788	    for claim_id, verdict in sorted(final_verdicts.items()):
789	        if verdict == _VERDICT_VERIFIED:
790	            continue
791	        meta = audit_map.get(claim_id)
792	        if meta is None:
793	            raise ReportRedactionError(
794	                f"claim {claim_id} has verdict {verdict} but no audit_map row; "
795	                "cannot locate its sentence to annotate (fail-closed)."
796	            )
797	        severity = str(meta.get("severity", ""))
798	        stem_norm = _normalize(str(meta.get("sentence", "")))
799	        if not stem_norm:
800	            raise ReportRedactionError(
801	                f"claim {claim_id} ({verdict}/{severity}) has empty claim_text; "
802	                "cannot annotate (fail-closed)."
803	            )
804	        if not _prose_present(report_text, stem_norm):
805	            already_absent.append(claim_id)  # downstream dedup removed it; nothing to label
806	            continue
807	        # COMPLETENESS: every occurrence must be a clean TIER-1 single sentence (checked on the
808	        # marker-stripped text via the redactor's TIER-1 — immune to the marker). A straddle /
809	        # under-split occurrence the per-line pass cannot pin -> fail closed (never ship unlabeled).
810	        if not _claim_fully_pinnable(report_text, stem_norm):
811	            raise ReportRedactionError(
812	                f"claim {claim_id} ({verdict}/{severity}) prose is present in report.md but at least "
813	                "one occurrence could not be pinned to a discrete sentence to annotate (fail-closed)."
814	            )
815	        marker = marker_by_claim.get(claim_id) or _DEFAULT_LOW_MARKER
816	        claims.append((claim_id, verdict, severity, stem_norm, marker))
817	
818	    # WS-5 (I-deepfix-001): collect figure-consistency ADVISORY claims (default ON). These are
819	    # BEST-EFFORT twins of a flagged claim that inherit its caveat; unlike the mandatory
820	    # non-VERIFIED claims above they NEVER fail-closed (labelling a twin only ADDS a caveat, so an
821	    # absent / unpinnable twin is simply not labelled). Ordered AFTER the mandatory claims so a
822	    # sentence that IS a genuine non-VERIFIED claim keeps its own marker (first-match wins).
823	    advisory = _collect_figure_consistency_claims(
824	        report_text,
825	        final_verdicts,
826	        audit_map,
827	        marker_by_claim,
828	        span_text_by_claim,
829	        already_collected={c[0] for c in claims},
830	    )
831	
832	    # SINGLE labeling pass: spans come from the ORIGINAL line (never a marker-mutated one), so two
833	    # same-line claims each get their own marker. Each sentence is matched marker-stripped (idempotent)
834	    # and labeled once with the FIRST claim it matches. Mandatory claims precede advisory twins.
835	    label_targets = claims + advisory
836	    labeled: set[str] = set()
837	    new_lines: list[str] = []
838	    for line in report_text.split("\n"):
839	        if not _is_redactable_body_line(line):
840	            new_lines.append(line)
841	            continue
842	        out: list[str] = []
843	        cursor = 0
844	        for (start, end) in _sentence_spans(line):
845	            out.append(line[cursor:start])  # inter-sentence whitespace, verbatim
846	            sentence = line[start:end]
847	            stripped_sentence = _CONFIDENCE_MARKER_RE.sub("", sentence)
848	            chosen: tuple[str, str] | None = None
849	            for claim_id, _v, _s, stem_norm, marker in label_targets:
850	                if _sentence_matches_stem(stripped_sentence, stem_norm):
851	                    chosen = (claim_id, marker)
852	                    break
853	            if chosen is None:
854	                out.append(sentence)  # byte-identical — VERIFIED / unrelated sentence
855	            else:
856	                claim_id, marker = chosen
857	                labeled.add(claim_id)
858	                out.append(sentence if marker in sentence else f"{sentence} {marker}")
859	            cursor = end
860	        out.append(line[cursor:])
861	        new_lines.append("".join(out))
862	    working = "\n".join(new_lines)
863	
864	    # Defensive: a collected (present + pinnable) MANDATORY claim MUST have been labeled in the pass.
865	    annotated: list[AnnotatedClaim] = []
866	    for claim_id, verdict, severity, _stem, marker in claims:
867	        if claim_id not in labeled:
868	            raise ReportRedactionError(
869	                f"claim {claim_id} ({verdict}/{severity}) was pinnable but no sentence matched it in "
870	                "the labeling pass (fail-closed)."
871	            )
872	        annotated.append(
873	            AnnotatedClaim(claim_id=claim_id, severity=severity, verdict=verdict, marker=marker)
874	        )
875	    # Advisory twins are best-effort: record only the ones that actually got labelled; NEVER raise.
876	    for claim_id, verdict, severity, _stem, marker in advisory:
877	        if claim_id in labeled:
878	            annotated.append(
879	                AnnotatedClaim(claim_id=claim_id, severity=severity, verdict=verdict, marker=marker)
880	            )
881	
882	    return AnnotationResult(report_text=working, annotated=annotated, already_absent=already_absent)
883	
884	
885	def _collect_figure_consistency_claims(
886	    report_text: str,
887	    final_verdicts: dict[str, str],
888	    audit_map: dict[str, dict],
889	    marker_by_claim: dict[str, str],
890	    span_text_by_claim: dict[str, str] | None,
891	    already_collected: set[str],
892	) -> list[tuple[str, str, str, str, str]]:
893	    """WS-5 (I-deepfix-001): build the ADVISORY figure-consistency labelling claims.
894	
895	    Returns ``(claim_id, verdict, severity, stem_norm, marker)`` tuples for every claim NOT already
896	    collected as mandatory whose caveat must be inherited because it is a twin of a flagged claim:
897	      * FIX-a: it shares BOTH a span-identity tuple AND a numeric figure with a non-VERIFIED claim
898	        -> inherits that flagged sibling's marker.
899	      * FIX-b: ``span_text_by_claim`` names its cited span AND ``effect_size_conditional_reason``
900	        fires (the claim re-lifts a span number while dropping the governing conditional/threshold)
901	        -> inherits a generic low marker.
902	    Gated by ``PG_FIGURE_CONSISTENCY_ANNOTATE`` (default ON, via ``figure_consistency_annotate_enabled``);
903	    disabled -> returns ``[]`` (byte-identical claim_id-only behaviour). Never raises: a twin the
904	    per-line pass cannot pin is simply omitted (adding a caveat is a bonus, its absence is not a leak).
905	    """
906	    if not figure_consistency_annotate_enabled():
907	        return []
908	
909	    # FIX-a index: (span-identities, figures, marker) for each flagged (non-VERIFIED) claim that
910	    # names BOTH a span and a figure. A twin must intersect BOTH to inherit (surgical — same-span
911	    # prose that repeats no flagged figure is NOT caveated).
912	    flagged_index: list[tuple[set[tuple[str, int, int]], set[str], str]] = []
913	    for claim_id, verdict in final_verdicts.items():
914	        if verdict == _VERDICT_VERIFIED:
915	            continue
916	        sentence = str((audit_map.get(claim_id) or {}).get("sentence", ""))
917	        spans = _span_identities(sentence)
918	        figs = _figure_tokens(sentence)
919	        if spans and figs:
920	            marker = marker_by_claim.get(claim_id) or _DEFAULT_LOW_MARKER
921	            flagged_index.append((spans, figs, marker))
922	
923	    advisory: list[tuple[str, str, str, str, str]] = []
924	    seen = set(already_collected)
925	    for claim_id, verdict in sorted(final_verdicts.items()):
926	        if claim_id in seen:
927	            continue
928	        meta = audit_map.get(claim_id)
929	        if meta is None:
930	            continue
931	        sentence = str(meta.get("sentence", ""))
932	        stem_norm = _normalize(sentence)
933	        if not stem_norm or not _prose_present(report_text, stem_norm):
934	            continue  # nothing rendered to label
935	
936	        marker: str | None = None
937	        # FIX-a — flagged span-identity + shared figure.
938	        twin_spans = _span_identities(sentence)
939	        twin_figs = _figure_tokens(sentence)
940	        if twin_spans and twin_figs:
941	            for f_spans, f_figs, f_marker in flagged_index:
942	                if (twin_spans & f_spans) and (twin_figs & f_figs):
943	                    marker = f_marker
944	                    break
945	        # FIX-b — effect-size conditional/threshold stripped (needs the cited span text).
946	        if marker is None and span_text_by_claim:
947	            span_text = span_text_by_claim.get(claim_id, "")
948	            if span_text and effect_size_conditional_reason(sentence, span_text):
949	                marker = _DEFAULT_LOW_MARKER
950	
951	        if marker is None:
952	            continue
953	        advisory.append((claim_id, verdict, str(meta.get("severity", "")), stem_norm, marker))
954	        seen.add(claim_id)
955	    return advisory
956	
957	
958	# A generic low-confidence marker for a non-VERIFIED claim whose caller-supplied marker is missing
959	# (never leave an unverified claim unlabeled). Mirrors claim_labeler's low wording.
960	_DEFAULT_LOW_MARKER = "[confidence: low — NOT confirmed by the cited source; treat as unverified]"
961	
962	
963	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
964	# P0-2 (I-deepfix-001) — RENDER-SEAM FAITHFULNESS GATE (drop, don't caveat)
965	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
966	# §-1.3: the faithfulness engine is the ONE hard gate, so a settled 4-role verdict that is NOT
967	# VERIFIED reaching rendered prose is a LEAK. The always-release seam used to KEEP + caveat every
968	# non-VERIFIED settled verdict (annotate) — a claim the engine settled UNSUPPORTED / FABRICATED /
969	# UNREACHABLE / PARTIAL shipped as a "[confidence: low ...]" line in the section body. This gate
970	# DROPS such a claim from prose instead (reconcile: remove the sentence, keep VERIFIED neighbours,
971	# an emptied section falls to the existing TIER-4 section gap-stub). It is faithfulness-
972	# STRENGTHENING (drops MORE unfaithful text than the annotate path it replaces) and never relaxes
973	# a gate. PARTIAL is the ONLY configurable case: default DROP; the admit-partial flag keeps + labels
974	# PARTIAL only. This is NOT a breadth cap/filter — VERIFIED prose is untouched (§-1.3 WEIGHT-not-
975	# FILTER); it only removes claims the faithfulness engine already rejected.
976	_ENV_RENDER_VERDICT_GATE = "PG_RENDER_VERDICT_GATE"
977	_ENV_RENDER_VERDICT_GATE_ADMIT_PARTIAL = "PG_RENDER_VERDICT_GATE_ADMIT_PARTIAL"
978	_VERDICT_PARTIAL = "PARTIAL"
979	
980	
981	def render_verdict_gate_enabled() -> bool:
982	    """PG_RENDER_VERDICT_GATE — DEFAULT-ON. ON => a settled non-VERIFIED 4-role verdict is DROPPED
983	    from rendered prose (never caveated). OFF (explicit '0'/'false'/'off'/'no'/'') reverts the
984	    always-release seam to the legacy keep+label annotate path byte-for-byte (a documented kill-
985	    switch for offline-test isolation only; production leaves it ON)."""
986	    return os.getenv(_ENV_RENDER_VERDICT_GATE, "1").strip().lower() not in (
987	        "", "0", "false", "off", "no",
988	    )
989	
990	
991	def render_verdict_gate_admit_partial() -> bool:
992	    """PG_RENDER_VERDICT_GATE_ADMIT_PARTIAL — DEFAULT-OFF. OFF => a PARTIAL settled verdict is
993	    DROPPED like FABRICATED/UNSUPPORTED/UNREACHABLE. ON => a PARTIAL claim is KEPT + LABELED
994	    (caveated) while every OTHER non-VERIFIED verdict is still dropped. FABRICATED / UNSUPPORTED /
995	    UNREACHABLE are NEVER admitted — they always DROP (the render gate cannot be relaxed for them)."""
996	    return os.getenv(_ENV_RENDER_VERDICT_GATE_ADMIT_PARTIAL, "0").strip().lower() in (
997	        "1", "true", "yes", "on",
998	    )
999	
1000	
1001	def partition_verdicts_for_render(
1002	    final_verdicts: dict[str, str],
1003	    *,
1004	    drop_nonverified: bool = True,
1005	    admit_partial: bool = False,
1006	) -> tuple[dict[str, str], dict[str, str]]:
1007	    """Split settled 4-role verdicts into (DROP, LABEL) maps for the render seam (P0-2, §-1.3).
1008	
1009	    THE render-seam faithfulness rule, as a pure deterministic function so the runner and the unit
1010	    test drive the SAME code (the ``_route_seam_worker_exception`` "production IS the test subject"
1011	    precedent). VERIFIED always survives (absent from BOTH maps). Every other SETTLED verdict is:
1012	      * a DROP by default (removed from prose -> ``reconcile_report_against_verdicts``);
1013	      * a LABEL only for a PARTIAL verdict when ``admit_partial`` is True (kept + caveated ->
1014	        ``annotate_report_against_verdicts``). FABRICATED / UNSUPPORTED / UNREACHABLE (and any other
1015	        non-VERIFIED verdict) are NEVER admitted — they always DROP.
1016	    ``drop_nonverified=False`` is the kill-switch (PG_RENDER_VERDICT_GATE=0): EVERY non-VERIFIED
1017	    verdict routes to LABEL, reproducing the legacy always-release keep+label behaviour byte-for-byte.
1018	    """
1019	    drop: dict[str, str] = {}
1020	    label: dict[str, str] = {}
1021	    for claim_id, verdict in final_verdicts.items():
1022	        normalized = str(verdict or "").strip().upper()
1023	        if normalized == _VERDICT_VERIFIED:
1024	            continue  # VERIFIED survives untouched (never dropped, never caveated)
1025	        if not drop_nonverified:
1026	            label[claim_id] = verdict          # gate OFF -> legacy keep+label for all non-VERIFIED
1027	        elif admit_partial and normalized == _VERDICT_PARTIAL:
1028	            label[claim_id] = verdict          # PARTIAL admitted -> keep + caveat
1029	        else:
1030	            drop[claim_id] = verdict           # DROP (default for every non-VERIFIED verdict)
1031	    return drop, label
1032	
1033	
1034	def _refilter_after_redaction(report_text: str) -> str:
1035	    """Tidy the Key-Findings + Abstract/Conclusion blocks after a redaction (drop gap-stub bullets /
1036	    an emptied block), matching the runner's existing post-reconcile cleanup. Local imports keep the
1037	    generator dependency lazy (no import cycle at module load); no-op when nothing needs tidying."""
1038	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.key_findings import (  # noqa: PLC0415
1039	        refilter_key_findings_block,
1040	    )
1041	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.abstract_conclusion import (  # noqa: PLC0415
1042	        refilter_abstract_conclusion_block,
1043	    )
1044	    text = refilter_key_findings_block(report_text)
1045	    text = refilter_abstract_conclusion_block(text)
1046	    return text
1047	
1048	
1049	@dataclass
1050	class RenderGateResult:
1051	    """Outcome of the P0-2 render-seam faithfulness gate over one report body. Pure data — the
1052	    CALLER writes report.md / gaps.json / claim_confidence.json / manifest from these fields."""
1053	
1054	    report_text: str
1055	    redaction: "RedactionResult | None" = None      # the DROP pass (reconcile) result
1056	    annotation: "AnnotationResult | None" = None     # the LABEL pass (annotate) result, if it ran
1057	    outcome: str = "reconciled"                      # "reconciled" | "withhold_unpinnable"
1058	    error: str = ""
1059	
1060	    @property
1061	    def dropped_claim_ids(self) -> list[str]:
1062	        return [rc.claim_id for rc in self.redaction.redacted] if self.redaction else []
1063	
1064	    @property
1065	    def labeled_claim_ids(self) -> list[str]:
1066	        return [ac.claim_id for ac in self.annotation.annotated] if self.annotation else []
1067	
1068	
1069	def apply_render_verdict_gate(
1070	    report_text: str,
1071	    final_verdicts: dict[str, str],
1072	    audit_map: dict[str, dict],
1073	    marker_by_claim: dict[str, str],
1074	    *,
1075	    drop_nonverified: bool = True,
1076	    admit_partial: bool = False,
1077	    span_text_by_claim: dict[str, str] | None = None,
1078	) -> RenderGateResult:
1079	    """THE single deterministic render-seam faithfulness rule (P0-2, §-1.3): a settled 4-role verdict
1080	    that is NOT VERIFIED is DROPPED from rendered prose, never shown as a caveated low-confidence line.
1081	
1082	    Partitions ``final_verdicts`` (``partition_verdicts_for_render``), then:
1083	      1. DROP pass — ``reconcile_report_against_verdicts`` removes every DROP claim's verbatim
1084	         sentence (replaced with the visible gap language), KEEPING every VERIFIED neighbour byte-
1085	         for-byte; a section emptied by the drop falls to the existing TIER-4 section gap-stub
1086	         (never blanked when a VERIFIED claim remains).
1087	      2. LABEL pass — ``annotate_report_against_verdicts`` keeps + caveats every admitted claim (only
1088	         PARTIAL when ``admit_partial``; ALL non-VERIFIED when ``drop_nonverified`` is False — the
1089	         legacy keep+label kill-switch). The annotate input PRESERVES VERIFIED entries so the WS-5
1090	         figure-consistency twin re-key is byte-unchanged from the legacy annotate path.
1091	    FAITHFULNESS-STRENGTHENING: under the default (``drop_nonverified=True, admit_partial=False``)
1092	    the LABEL map is EMPTY, so NOTHING non-VERIFIED survives as prose. Pure (string ops only; no I/O)
1093	    — the CALLER writes report.md / gaps.json / manifest from the returned result.
1094	
1095	    Fail-safe: if the DROP reconcile cannot bound a PRESENT non-VERIFIED claim to any redactable unit
1096	    (``ReportRedactionError`` — e.g. it lives only inside a heading), the gate returns
1097	    ``outcome="withhold_unpinnable"`` with the ORIGINAL text so the caller withholds the body +
1098	    discloses the gap (never ships the leak). A LABEL-pass pin failure falls back to DROPPING the
1099	    label set (strictly safer than keep+label), matching the runner's legacy annotate->reconcile
1100	    quarantine fallback.
1101	    """
1102	    drop, label = partition_verdicts_for_render(
1103	        final_verdicts, drop_nonverified=drop_nonverified, admit_partial=admit_partial,
1104	    )
1105	
1106	    # ── DROP pass — remove every non-VERIFIED settled verdict from prose ──────────────────────────
1107	    try:
1108	        redaction = reconcile_report_against_verdicts(report_text, drop, audit_map)
1109	    except ReportRedactionError as drop_exc:
1110	        # A present non-VERIFIED claim could not be bounded to any redactable unit -> withhold-body.
1111	        return RenderGateResult(
1112	            report_text=report_text, outcome="withhold_unpinnable", error=str(drop_exc),
1113	        )
1114	    working = redaction.report_text
1115	    if redaction.redacted:
1116	        working = _refilter_after_redaction(working)
1117	
1118	    # ── LABEL pass — keep + caveat only the admitted (PARTIAL / kill-switch) claims ───────────────
1119	    # annotate sees VERIFIED (preserved so the WS-5 twin re-key is byte-unchanged) + the admitted
1120	    # label set, but NEVER a dropped claim (already removed above). Skipped when nothing is admitted.
1121	    annotate_input = {cid: v for cid, v in final_verdicts.items() if cid not in drop}
1122	    has_label = any(
1123	        str(v or "").strip().upper() != _VERDICT_VERIFIED for v in annotate_input.values()
1124	    )
1125	    annotation: "AnnotationResult | None" = None
1126	    if has_label:
1127	        try:
1128	            annotation = annotate_report_against_verdicts(
1129	                working,
1130	                annotate_input,
1131	                audit_map,
1132	                marker_by_claim,
1133	                span_text_by_claim=span_text_by_claim,
1134	            )
1135	            working = annotation.report_text
1136	        except ReportRedactionError:
1137	            # Legacy annotate->reconcile QUARANTINE fallback: a present-but-unpinnable label claim is
1138	            # DROPPED (strictly safer than keep+label) rather than shipped unlabeled.
1139	            try:
1140	                label_redaction = reconcile_report_against_verdicts(working, label, audit_map)
1141	            except ReportRedactionError as label_exc:
1142	                return RenderGateResult(
1143	                    report_text=report_text, outcome="withhold_unpinnable", error=str(label_exc),
1144	                )
1145	            working = label_redaction.report_text
1146	            if label_redaction.redacted:
1147	                working = _refilter_after_redaction(working)
1148	            # Fold the fallback drops into the DROP-pass record (single gaps.json / manifest source).
1149	            redaction.redacted.extend(label_redaction.redacted)
1150	            redaction.already_absent.extend(label_redaction.already_absent)
1151	            annotation = None
1152	
1153	    return RenderGateResult(
1154	        report_text=working, redaction=redaction, annotation=annotation, outcome="reconciled",
1155	    )
1156	
1157	
1158	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
1159	# A4 (I-deepfix-001) — ATTRIBUTION-ORIGIN visible-text caveat (render layer)
1160	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
1161	# The attribution-origin guard (``attribution_origin_guard.attribution_origin_reason``) flags a
1162	# claim that NAMES an organizational finder ("<ORG> found that Y") whose identity is NOT the
1163	# publisher of ANY cited source — a re-reporting SECONDARY citation ("correctness is not
1164	# faithfulness": the cited span supports the number, but the citation is not the faithful ORIGIN of
1165	# the assertion). During verification that fires as DISCLOSURE-ONLY telemetry; the VISIBLE sentence
1166	# still names the unsupported actor. This render pass APPENDS an inline caveat to that sentence so
1167	# the mis-attribution is corrected in the text the reader sees.
1168	#
1169	# Codex P1 fixes (this rewrites VERIFIED visible prose — highest risk, conservative by construction):
1170	#   * P1 #1 (boundary): the caveat marker is consumed as trailing material by
1171	#     ``_SENTENCE_BOUNDARY_RE`` (see ``_ATTRIBUTION_CAVEAT_TRAILER``), so it can NEVER fuse the
1172	#     caveated sentence with the next grounded sentence and cause a later D8 over-redaction. And
1173	#     ``_normalize`` strips the caveat, so a caveated sentence still matches its own stem at the
1174	#     redactor / annotator coverage floor.
1175	#   * P1 #2 (citation-SPECIFIC, not citation-insensitive): the decision is RE-DERIVED per rendered
1176	#     occurrence from that sentence's OWN resolved ``[N]`` citation domains via the guard — so a
1177	#     legitimately primary-cited occurrence of the same claim is NEVER caveated, and a deduped-away
1178	#     secondary occurrence cannot cause a sibling to be wrongly caveated.
1179	#   * P1 #3 (ALL rendered claim units): the pass scans the ASSEMBLED report.md body directly, so it
1180	#     covers EVERY rendered unit (CWF, quantified-analysis, every section) — not just
1181	#     ``multi.sections``.
1182	#
1183	# §-1.3 WITHHOLD-DISCLOSE (never a hard drop): it NEVER drops the sentence, NEVER removes the
1184	# grounded number, NEVER removes the ``[N]`` citation — it ONLY ADDS a caveat. HIGH-PRECISION +
1185	# FAIL-OPEN: a sentence with no citations, or whose ``[N]`` citations cannot be FULLY resolved to
1186	# URLs, is skipped (never caveated on incomplete evidence). The faithfulness engine (strict_verify /
1187	# NLI / 4-role D8 / provenance) is untouched — this pass runs entirely at the render layer on
1188	# already-verified prose.
1189	_ATTRIBUTION_SOFT_WARNING_PREFIX = "attribution_origin_unverified:"
1190	_ATTRIBUTION_ACTORS_RE = re.compile(r"actors=(.*?):cited_domains=")
1191	# A bare numbered citation marker ``[12]`` — used to read a rendered sentence's OWN visible
1192	# citations so the guard can be re-run against THAT occurrence's actual cited domains (P1 #2).
1193	_NUMBERED_CITATION_RE = re.compile(r"\[(\d+)\]")
1194	# A4 (I-deepfix-001) Codex P1: a rendered body line can BEGIN with wrapped trailing citations that
1195	# belong to the PREVIOUS sentence ("[71][5][7][6] Further analysis found …"). A LEADING run of bare
1196	# ``[N]`` markers (before any word character) is NOT this sentence's own citation — reading it would
1197	# resolve a neighbor's source domains and could caveat this sentence on evidence it does not carry.
1198	# Stripped before the sentence's OWN markers are read, so only citations attached to this sentence's
1199	# words / trailing its own terminator survive.
1200	_LEADING_WRAPPED_CITATIONS_RE = re.compile(r"^\s*(?:\[\d+\]\s*)+")
1201	
1202	
1203	def attribution_actor_from_soft_warning(soft_warning: str) -> str | None:
1204	    """Extract a human-readable actor label from an attribution-origin soft_warning.
1205	
1206	    Soft-warning form (``attribution_origin_guard.attribution_origin_reason``):
1207	      ``attribution_origin_unverified:actors=<run>|<run2>:cited_domains=<d1>,<d2>``
1208	    Returns the ``actors=`` payload with the internal ``|`` separator rendered as ``/`` (e.g.
1209	    "The International Labour Organization / Poland's National Research Institute"), or None when
1210	    the string is not an attribution-origin warning / carries no actors (defensive — never raises).
1211	    """
1212	    if not soft_warning or not soft_warning.startswith(_ATTRIBUTION_SOFT_WARNING_PREFIX):
1213	        return None
1214	    m = _ATTRIBUTION_ACTORS_RE.search(soft_warning)
1215	    if not m:
1216	        return None
1217	    actors = m.group(1).strip()
1218	    if not actors:
1219	        return None
1220	    return actors.replace("|", " / ")
1221	
1222	
1223	def _attribution_caveat_marker(actor: str) -> str:
1224	    """The inline caveat appended to a mis-attributed sentence (plain declarative English)."""
1225	    # Codex P1 (A4): NO semicolon — ``report_claim_extractor._default_atomizer`` splits atoms on
1226	    # ``;``, so a semicolon here would spawn a spurious "…supported by the cited source]" claim atom.
1227	    # An em-dash carries the same reading without creating a new atom.
1228	    return (
1229	        f"[attribution unverified — the cited source does not appear to be {actor} — "
1230	        "the finding itself is supported by the cited source]"
1231	    )
1232	
1233	
1234	@dataclass
1235	class AttributionCaveat:
1236	    """One sentence that received an inline attribution-origin caveat in the visible report."""
1237	
1238	    actor: str
1239	    sentence: str  # the matched rendered sentence (pre-caveat)
1240	
1241	
1242	@dataclass
1243	class AttributionCorrectionResult:
1244	    """Outcome of appending attribution-origin caveats to the rendered report."""
1245	
1246	    report_text: str
1247	    caveated: list[AttributionCaveat] = field(default_factory=list)
1248	    # Sentences the guard would flag but whose visible ``[N]`` citations could not be fully resolved
1249	    # to URLs (so we conservatively did NOT caveat) — recorded for the audit trail, never a leak.
1250	    skipped_unresolved: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
1251	
1252	    @property
1253	    def caveated_count(self) -> int:
1254	        return len(self.caveated)
1255	
1256	
1257	def _rendered_sentence_attribution_actor(
1258	    sentence: str,
1259	    citation_urls: "dict[str, str]",
1260	    attribution_origin_reason,
1261	) -> "tuple[str | None, bool]":
1262	    """Decide whether ONE rendered sentence is a mis-attribution, citation-SPECIFICally (P1 #2).
1263	
1264	    Returns ``(actor_label, resolved_ok)``:
1265	      * ``actor_label`` is the finder to name in the caveat, or None when the sentence is not a
1266	        mis-attribution (or cannot be judged).
1267	      * ``resolved_ok`` is False when the sentence NAMES cited markers we could not fully resolve to
1268	        URLs — the caller records that (audit trail) and conservatively skips it.
1269	
1270	    The decision RE-RUNS the attribution-origin guard against THIS occurrence's OWN visible ``[N]``
1271	    citations resolved to source URLs, so a legitimately primary-cited occurrence of the same claim
1272	    never fires. FAIL-OPEN: a sentence with no citations, or any unresolved citation, is not
1273	    caveated. Never raises.
1274	    """
1275	    # Idempotence: a sentence already carrying an attribution caveat is left alone (``_normalize``
1276	    # strips the caveat, so the redactor / annotator still match its stem).
1277	    if _ATTRIBUTION_CAVEAT_RE.search(sentence):
1278	        return None, True
1279	    # Codex P1 (A4): read only the citations this sentence OWNS. Strip a leading run of wrapped
1280	    # markers that belong to the previous sentence (report_redactor documents this shape) so we never
1281	    # resolve a neighbor's domains onto this sentence. If nothing remains after the strip, the
1282	    # sentence carries no citation of its own -> cannot judge origin -> skip (fail-open).
1283	    own = _LEADING_WRAPPED_CITATIONS_RE.sub("", sentence)
1284	    nums = _NUMBERED_CITATION_RE.findall(own)
1285	    if not nums:
1286	        return None, True  # no citation the sentence OWNS -> cannot judge origin -> skip (fail-open)
1287	    urls: list[str] = []
1288	    for n in nums:
1289	        url = citation_urls.get(str(n))
1290	        if not url:
1291	            # A cited marker we cannot resolve -> we cannot rule out a matching primary source ->
1292	            # do NOT caveat (never caveat on incomplete evidence).
1293	            return None, False
1294	        urls.append(url)
1295	    # Strip provenance tokens + numbered markers, keeping case + punctuation for the guard's
1296	    # proper-noun + attribution-verb matcher.
1297	    prose = _prose_stem(sentence).strip()
1298	    if not prose:
1299	        return None, True
1300	    reason = attribution_origin_reason(prose, urls)
1301	    if not reason:
1302	        return None, True  # guard inert -> attribution is fine (or ambiguous) -> no caveat
1303	    return attribution_actor_from_soft_warning(reason), True
1304	
1305	
1306	def annotate_report_attribution_origin(
1307	    report_text: str,
1308	    citation_urls: "dict[str, str]",
1309	) -> AttributionCorrectionResult:
1310	    """APPEND an inline attribution caveat to every mis-attributed body sentence in ``report_text``.
1311	
1312	    Args:
1313	      report_text: the assembled report.md content.
1314	      citation_urls: ``{citation_number(str): source_url}`` built from the rendered bibliography
1315	        (each row's ``num`` -> ``url``). Used to resolve a sentence's OWN visible ``[N]`` markers to
1316	        the domains the guard checks — this is what makes the caveat citation-SPECIFIC (P1 #2).
1317	
1318	    For each body sentence, the attribution-origin guard is RE-RUN against that occurrence's own
1319	    resolved citation domains (``_rendered_sentence_attribution_actor``); only a sentence that STILL
1320	    fires gets the caveat appended once. Covers EVERY rendered claim unit because it walks report.md
1321	    directly (P1 #3). IDEMPOTENT: a sentence already carrying an attribution caveat is byte-identical.
1322	    NEVER raises — a sentence with unresolved citations is recorded in ``skipped_unresolved`` and
1323	    left unchanged (FAIL-OPEN). Only body sentences are touched; headings and bibliography rows
1324	    (``_is_redactable_body_line``) are byte-preserved.
1325	    """
1326	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.attribution_origin_guard import (  # noqa: PLC0415
1327	        attribution_origin_reason,
1328	    )
1329	
1330	    caveated: list[AttributionCaveat] = []
1331	    skipped_unresolved: list[str] = []
1332	    new_lines: list[str] = []
1333	    for line in report_text.split("\n"):
1334	        if not _is_redactable_body_line(line):
1335	            new_lines.append(line)  # heading / bibliography / blank — byte-identical
1336	            continue
1337	        out: list[str] = []
1338	        cursor = 0
1339	        for (start, end) in _sentence_spans(line):
1340	            out.append(line[cursor:start])  # inter-sentence whitespace, verbatim
1341	            sentence = line[start:end]
1342	            actor, resolved_ok = _rendered_sentence_attribution_actor(
1343	                sentence, citation_urls, attribution_origin_reason
1344	            )
1345	            if not resolved_ok:
1346	                skipped_unresolved.append(sentence)
1347	            if actor is None:
1348	                out.append(sentence)  # byte-identical — not a mis-attribution / already caveated
1349	            else:
1350	                out.append(f"{sentence} {_attribution_caveat_marker(actor)}")
1351	                caveated.append(AttributionCaveat(actor=actor, sentence=sentence))
1352	            cursor = end
1353	        out.append(line[cursor:])
1354	        new_lines.append("".join(out))
1355	
1356	    return AttributionCorrectionResult(
1357	        report_text="\n".join(new_lines),
1358	        caveated=caveated,
1359	        skipped_unresolved=skipped_unresolved,

1	"""Per-claim 4-role orchestration — Mirror -> Sentinel -> Judge, fail-closed composition.
2	
3	I-meta-002 sub-PR-5. This is the COMPOSABLE orchestration layer that drives the three
4	sub-PR-4 role adapters (Mirror, Sentinel, Judge) over ONE injected `RoleTransport` and
5	produces a single `D8ClaimRow` (sub-PR-3) per claim, plus a complete per-call audit trail.
6	
7	There is no direct paid LLM call here; the injected transport performs the (already-paid)
8	call and this module threads its cost into the shared PG_MAX_COST_PER_RUN accumulator per
9	I-meta-008 (so generator + 4-role verifier spend are bounded by ONE cap). Tests inject a
10	mock transport. The runtime transport that wraps `openrouter_client` is wired in the sweep
11	surgery (sub-PR-6), NOT here.
12	
13	Two safety properties this module guarantees (both clinical-lethal if absent):
14	
15	1. RECORDING TRANSPORT (Codex iter-2 P1-4). `RecordingTransport` WRAPS the injected transport
16	   and appends one `RoleCallRecord` to `self.records` on EVERY `complete()` — BEFORE returning
17	   to the adapter, so the record exists even when the adapter later raises (Mirror fail-closed).
18	   The Path-B identity gate therefore has no blind spot: every served completion is captured,
19	   including the highest-risk fail-closed paths.
20	
21	2. FAIL-CLOSED FINAL VERDICT (Codex iter-2 P1-1, LOCKED rule). `run_claim_pipeline` computes a
22	   `final_verdict` and writes THAT into `D8ClaimRow.verdict`, while preserving the
23	   `raw_judge_verdict` separately on the result. The rule (documented inline below) makes it
24	   impossible for a hallucination — a Mirror with no grounded citation, or a Sentinel
25	   UNGROUNDED — to reach VERIFIED, while never UPGRADING a worse Judge verdict
26	   (FABRICATED/UNREACHABLE) to merely UNSUPPORTED.
27	
28	`claim_id` is a REQUIRED caller-supplied param (Codex iter-2 P1-2): it is used verbatim for
29	`D8ClaimRow.claim_id` and is NEVER synthesized from claim text (duplicate/edited claims would
30	otherwise collide and break rewrite/gap traceability).
31	"""
32	
33	from __future__ import annotations
34	
35	import os
36	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
37	from typing import Any
38	
39	# I-meta-008: thread the 4-role verifier spend into the SAME run-budget accumulator the
40	# generator already feeds. `openrouter_client` does NOT import the `roles` package (verified),
41	# so this top-level alias is circular-import-safe and mirrors entailment_judge.py's `_orc` use.
42	import src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client as _orc
43	
44	from src.polaris_graph.roles.judge_adapter import run_judge
45	from src.polaris_graph.roles.judge_contract import Verdict
46	from src.polaris_graph.roles.mirror_adapter import (
47	    MirrorBindingError,
48	    MirrorCitationError,
49	    MirrorParseError,
50	    run_mirror,
51	)
52	from src.polaris_graph.roles.mirror_contract import MirrorPass1, MirrorPass2
53	from src.polaris_graph.roles.openai_compatible_transport import RoleTransportError
54	from src.polaris_graph.roles.release_policy import D8ClaimRow
55	from src.polaris_graph.roles.role_transport import (
56	    EvidenceDocument,
57	    RoleCallRecord,
58	    RoleRequest,
59	    RoleResponse,
60	    RoleTransport,
61	)
62	from src.polaris_graph.roles.sentinel_adapter import run_sentinel
63	from src.polaris_graph.roles.sentinel_contract import (
64	    SentinelResult,
65	    SentinelVerdict,
66	    atom_coverage_complete,
67	    sentinel_atom_coverage_enabled,
68	)
69	
70	# --- canonical verdict tokens (Verdict is a Literal of plain strings, NOT an Enum) -------
71	# Mirror release_policy.py's _VERDICT_* string-constant pattern; never write `Verdict.X`.
72	_VERDICT_VERIFIED = "VERIFIED"
73	_VERDICT_PARTIAL = "PARTIAL"
74	_VERDICT_UNSUPPORTED = "UNSUPPORTED"
75	_VERDICT_FABRICATED = "FABRICATED"
76	_VERDICT_UNREACHABLE = "UNREACHABLE"
77	
78	# Judge verdicts that the Sentinel-override DOWNGRADES to UNSUPPORTED (an apparently-good
79	# verdict on an UNGROUNDED claim). FABRICATED/UNREACHABLE/UNSUPPORTED are NEVER upgraded.
80	_SENTINEL_OVERRIDE_DOWNGRADE_FROM = (_VERDICT_VERIFIED, _VERDICT_PARTIAL)
81	
82	# I-meta-008 floor: a degraded LIVE verifier call that yields no usable usage (no tokens, no
83	# api cost) must NOT bill $0 and slip the cap. Floor it at a conservative ~500 prompt / ~100
84	# completion shape priced through `_impute_cost_from_tokens` (Opus-tier default for unknown
85	# slugs => ~$0.003/call). This replicates entailment_judge.py:231-234 (the I-bug-100 P2 fix).
86	_FLOOR_PROMPT_TOKENS = 500
87	_FLOOR_COMPLETION_TOKENS = 100
88	
89	# I-beatboth-006 (#1283) Fix C — the SINGLE seam degrade switch. A force-closed Mirror OR Judge OR
90	# Sentinel `RoleTransportError` (the bounded transport's fail-closed output, §3.1/§1.3) maps to a
91	# PER-CLAIM fail-closed disclosed adjudication (D8 verdict UNSUPPORTED for THIS claim + a
92	# `<{role}_role_unavailable>` disclosure record) instead of tearing the whole seam down. Generalizes
93	# the precedent `PG_SENTINEL_TRANSPORT_DEGRADE` to one switch for the whole D8 seam (Mirror catch
94	# site C.1 + Judge adapter C.2 + Sentinel adapter). Default ON. `PG_SENTINEL_TRANSPORT_DEGRADE` is
95	# honored as a back-compat ALIAS for the Sentinel arm (read in sentinel_adapter.py). NOTE: because
96	# `BlankVerdictError` SUBCLASSES `RoleTransportError`, a blank-verdict exhaustion ALSO degrades to
97	# per-claim UNSUPPORTED here — tightening-only (UNSUPPORTED is never credited), consistent with the
98	# existing Sentinel arm. LAW VI: env-driven, read at CALL time so a slate override after import wins.
99	_ROLE_TRANSPORT_DEGRADE_ENV = "PG_ROLE_TRANSPORT_DEGRADE"
100	_SENTINEL_TRANSPORT_DEGRADE_ALIAS_ENV = "PG_SENTINEL_TRANSPORT_DEGRADE"
101	_DEGRADE_OFF_TOKENS = ("0", "false", "no", "off")
102	# The synthetic disclosure-record marker substring (in `raw_text`); the C.2-merge selectors key off
103	# `served_model is None` + this substring so ONLY the synthetic unavailable record is propagated,
104	# never a real served-call record (which carries a served_model and already transited RecordingTransport).
105	_ROLE_UNAVAILABLE_MARKER = "_role_unavailable>"
106	
107	
108	def role_transport_degrade_enabled() -> bool:
109	    """Whether the per-role transport-fault degrade (Fix C) is ON (default ON). LAW VI: read lazily.
110	
111	    OFF iff `PG_ROLE_TRANSPORT_DEGRADE` is explicitly off (-> re-raise the `RoleTransportError` so the
112	    seam HARD-HALTS loudly per §3.3 C.3); the legacy `PG_SENTINEL_TRANSPORT_DEGRADE` is honored as the
113	    SAME back-compat ALIAS the Judge + Sentinel arms read (consulted only when the new switch is unset),
114	    so a legacy-only `PG_SENTINEL_TRANSPORT_DEGRADE=0` config degrades/halts ALL THREE roles UNIFORMLY
115	    — the Mirror arm is never left out of step with Judge/Sentinel. Absent/other -> ON (the default-safe
116	    per-claim fail-closed disclosed adjudication). Mirrors judge_adapter._role_transport_degrade_enabled."""
117	    primary = os.getenv(_ROLE_TRANSPORT_DEGRADE_ENV)
118	    if primary is not None:
119	        return primary.strip().lower() not in _DEGRADE_OFF_TOKENS
120	    alias = os.getenv(_SENTINEL_TRANSPORT_DEGRADE_ALIAS_ENV)
121	    if alias is not None:
122	        return alias.strip().lower() not in _DEGRADE_OFF_TOKENS
123	    return True
124	
125	
126	def compute_role_call_cost(model_slug: str, usage: dict[str, Any] | None) -> float:
127	    """Cost (USD) for ONE verifier completion from its OpenRouter `usage` block.
128	
129	    Mirrors the GENERATOR extraction (`openrouter_client._finalize_*`, :1717-1751) exactly,
130	    because `RoleResponse.usage` is the verbatim OpenRouter usage block (same shape):
131	
132	    * prompt/completion tokens read directly; **reasoning tokens are NESTED** under
133	      `completion_tokens_details.reasoning_tokens` when the top-level field is 0 — this is
134	      BLOCKING for Mirror/Judge at xhigh reasoning (reasoning dominates their cost).
135	    * conservative `max(api_cost, imputed)`: OpenRouter omits `usage.cost` for some models, so
136	      the impute path is live; for a budget guard over-counting is the safe direction.
137	    * FLOOR (I-meta-008 P1 / Codex design-review): when the computed cost is 0.0 AND every
138	      extracted token (prompt + completion + reasoning) is zero AND there is no positive API
139	      cost, floor at the ~500/100 shape. The prior `cost == 0.0 and not usage` only caught
140	      None/{}; a NON-EMPTY all-zero usage block (e.g. `{"prompt_tokens": 0, ...}`) would still
141	      record $0 and slip the cap — this token-level guard closes that hole.
142	    """
143	    usage_block = usage or {}
144	    input_tokens = int(usage_block.get("prompt_tokens", 0) or 0)
145	    output_tokens = int(usage_block.get("completion_tokens", 0) or 0)
146	    reasoning_tokens = int(usage_block.get("reasoning_tokens", 0) or 0)
147	    if not reasoning_tokens:
148	        details = usage_block.get("completion_tokens_details") or {}
149	        reasoning_tokens = int(details.get("reasoning_tokens", 0) or 0)
150	
151	    imputed = _orc._impute_cost_from_tokens(
152	        model_slug, input_tokens, output_tokens, reasoning_tokens
153	    )
154	    api_cost = float(usage_block.get("cost", 0) or 0)
155	    cost = max(api_cost, imputed)
156	
157	    # I-meta-008 P1 zero-usage floor: token-level, NOT block-presence. A non-empty all-zero
158	    # usage block (or absent usage) with no positive api cost must be floored, never billed $0.
159	    if (
160	        cost == 0.0
161	        and input_tokens == 0
162	        and output_tokens == 0
163	        and reasoning_tokens == 0
164	        and api_cost <= 0.0
165	    ):
166	        cost = _orc._impute_cost_from_tokens(
167	            model_slug, _FLOOR_PROMPT_TOKENS, _FLOOR_COMPLETION_TOKENS, 0
168	        )
169	    return cost
170	
171	
172	class RecordingTransport:
173	    """Wrap an injected `RoleTransport`; record EVERY completion before returning it.
174	
175	    On each `complete(request)` the wrapper appends a `RoleCallRecord(role, model_slug,
176	    served_model, raw_text, parsed=None)` to `self.records` BEFORE returning the underlying
177	    response (Codex iter-2 P1-4). Because the record is appended before the adapter sees the
178	    response — and therefore before the adapter can parse or raise — the served-identity
179	    record survives even on the fail-closed paths (a Mirror pass-1 that then raises
180	    `MirrorCitationError`). `parsed` is left None here: it is the role contract that parses,
181	    and the parsed sub-results are carried separately on `ClaimPipelineResult`.
182	
183	    I-meta-008 — RUN-BUDGET ACCOUNTING. `complete()` is the SINGLE per-call chokepoint every
184	    verifier completion (Mirror pass-1, pass-2, Sentinel, Judge) flows through exactly once.
185	    After recording, it threads the just-served call's cost into the SHARED `PG_MAX_COST_PER_RUN`
186	    accumulator (`_orc._add_run_cost`) and calls `_orc.check_run_budget(0)`, which raises
187	    `BudgetExceededError` BEFORE the next paid verifier call once the generator + verifier total
188	    crosses the cap. The transport itself adds no paid call here — it ACCOUNTS for the
189	    already-served call.
190	    """
191	
192	    def __init__(self, transport: RoleTransport) -> None:
193	        self._transport = transport
194	        self.records: list[RoleCallRecord] = []
195	
196	    def complete(self, request: RoleRequest) -> RoleResponse:
197	        response = self._transport.complete(request)
198	        # Append BEFORE returning so a downstream adapter raise cannot drop the record. The
199	        # served-identity audit trail for the breaching call is preserved even when the budget
200	        # check below raises.
201	        self.records.append(
202	            RoleCallRecord(
203	                role=request.role,
204	                model_slug=request.model_slug,
205	                served_model=response.served_model,
206	                raw_text=response.raw_text,
207	                parsed=None,
208	                # I-meta-002-q1b (#939): carry the separated reasoning so the seam can persist it
209	                # to four_role_role_calls.jsonl apart from the verdict (None if the role had none).
210	                reasoning=response.reasoning,
211	            )
212	        )
213	        # I-meta-008: account THIS verifier call's cost into the SAME run-budget accumulator the
214	        # generator feeds, then enforce the cap. `_RUN_COST_CTX` is per-asyncio-Task; the seam
215	        # runs synchronously inside the same `run_one_query` task that holds the generator spend,
216	        # so the delta lands on the counter holding `generator_spend + verifier_spend`.
217	        _role_cost = compute_role_call_cost(request.model_slug, response.usage)
218	        _orc._add_run_cost(_role_cost)
219	        # FX-11 (I-ready-017 BUG-10b + Codex iter-1 P1): the four-role verifier calls
220	        # (mirror/sentinel/judge) wrote ZERO cost-ledger rows before this. Ledger THIS call through
221	        # the SINGLE canonical writer, which bumps the shared per-session accumulator and appends
222	        # the row atomically — so role-row cumulative is non-monotonic NEITHER across the parallel
223	        # claim workers (each reset their own _RUN_COST_CTX) NOR in file write order. Best-effort
224	        # inside the writer: a ledger failure never breaks the verifier call or the budget check.
225	        _ub = response.usage or {}
226	        _rt = int(_ub.get("reasoning_tokens", 0) or 0) or int(
227	            (_ub.get("completion_tokens_details") or {}).get("reasoning_tokens", 0) or 0
228	        )
229	        _orc.append_cost_ledger_row(
230	            session_id=_orc._CURRENT_RUN_ID_CTX.get() or "no_run_id",
231	            call_type=f"role:{request.role}",
232	            cost_usd=_role_cost,
233	            input_tokens=int(_ub.get("prompt_tokens", 0) or 0),
234	            output_tokens=int(_ub.get("completion_tokens", 0) or 0),
235	            reasoning_tokens=_rt,
236	        )
237	        _orc.check_run_budget(0)  # raises BudgetExceededError if the cap is now exceeded.
238	        return response
239	
240	
241	@dataclass
242	class ClaimPipelineResult:
243	    """The full per-claim orchestration result.
244	
245	    `d8_row` carries the POST-OVERRIDE `final_verdict` in `D8ClaimRow.verdict`.
246	    `raw_judge_verdict` preserves the Judge's pre-override verdict (None if the Judge never
247	    ran because Mirror failed closed). `records` is the complete served-identity audit trail
248	    from the `RecordingTransport` (complete even on fail-closed paths). The raw
249	    `mirror_result` / `sentinel_result` / `judge_result` are preserved for auditability and
250	    are None where that stage failed closed / did not run.
251	    """
252	
253	    d8_row: D8ClaimRow
254	    raw_judge_verdict: Verdict | None
255	    final_verdict: Verdict
256	    records: list[RoleCallRecord]
257	    mirror_result: MirrorPass2 | None
258	    sentinel_result: SentinelResult | None
259	    judge_result: Verdict | None
260	
261	
262	def _first_grounded_citation_id(mirror_records: list[RoleCallRecord]) -> str | None:
263	    """Extract a grounded citation doc_id from the Mirror pass-1 record, else None.
264	
265	    The grounded doc_ids live on the pass-1 `MirrorPass1.citation_spans` (the pass-2
266	    `MirrorPass2` carries only a classification + hash, NOT doc_ids). `run_mirror` returns
267	    its OWN record list whose pass-1 record's `parsed` is a `MirrorPass1`; the first grounded
268	    span's first doc_id is used for D8 gap reporting. Returns None if no grounded span exists.
269	    """
270	    for record in mirror_records:
271	        parsed = record.parsed
272	        if isinstance(parsed, MirrorPass1) and parsed.citation_spans:
273	            for span in parsed.citation_spans:
274	                if span.doc_ids:
275	                    return span.doc_ids[0]
276	    return None
277	
278	
279	def _compose_final_verdict(
280	    *,
281	    mirror_failed_closed: bool,
282	    sentinel_result: SentinelResult | None,
283	    raw_judge_verdict: Verdict | None,
284	) -> Verdict:
285	    """LOCKED fail-closed composition rule (Codex iter-2 P1-1).
286	
287	    A hallucination can NEVER reach VERIFIED; a worse Judge verdict is NEVER upgraded.
288	
289	      (1) Mirror raised MirrorCitationError/MirrorBindingError (no grounded/bound citation)
290	          -> UNSUPPORTED, regardless of Judge. A claim with no grounded citation can never be
291	          VERIFIED. (On this path the Judge never ran, so raw_judge_verdict is None.)
292	      (2) ELSE if Sentinel == UNGROUNDED OR Sentinel.parsed_ok is False
293	          -> if raw Judge verdict is VERIFIED/PARTIAL, OVERRIDE to UNSUPPORTED;
294	             if raw Judge verdict is FABRICATED/UNREACHABLE/UNSUPPORTED, PRESERVE it
295	             (never upgrade a worse verdict to merely UNSUPPORTED).
296	      (3) ELSE -> raw_judge_verdict (Sentinel grounded + parsed_ok; trust the arbiter).
297	    """
298	    # (1) Mirror fail-closed -> UNSUPPORTED.
299	    if mirror_failed_closed:
300	        return _VERDICT_UNSUPPORTED
301	
302	    # raw_judge_verdict is non-None here: the Judge ran (Mirror succeeded). It fails LOUD on a
303	    # non-enum token (JudgeEnumError propagates from run_judge), so it is a valid Verdict.
304	    assert raw_judge_verdict is not None  # invariant: Judge ran iff Mirror succeeded.
305	
306	    # (2) Sentinel UNGROUNDED or unparsed -> downgrade an apparently-good Judge verdict.
307	    sentinel_unsafe = sentinel_result is None or (
308	        sentinel_result.verdict == SentinelVerdict.UNGROUNDED
309	        or not sentinel_result.parsed_ok
310	    )
311	    if sentinel_unsafe:
312	        if raw_judge_verdict in _SENTINEL_OVERRIDE_DOWNGRADE_FROM:
313	            return _VERDICT_UNSUPPORTED
314	        return raw_judge_verdict  # FABRICATED / UNREACHABLE / UNSUPPORTED preserved.
315	
316	    # (3) Sentinel grounded + parsed_ok -> the Judge's verdict stands.
317	    return raw_judge_verdict
318	
319	
320	def run_claim_pipeline(
321	    transport: RoleTransport,
322	    *,
323	    claim_id: str,
324	    claim: str,
325	    evidence_documents: list[EvidenceDocument],
326	    severity: str,
327	    s0_categories: list[str],
328	    model_slugs: dict[str, str],
329	    timestamp: str,
330	) -> ClaimPipelineResult:
331	    """Run Mirror -> Sentinel -> Judge over the injected transport for ONE claim.
332	
333	    `model_slugs` maps role -> pinned slug (keys: "mirror", "sentinel", "judge"). `claim_id`
334	    is REQUIRED and flows verbatim into `D8ClaimRow.claim_id` (never synthesized). `timestamp`
335	    is passed through for the caller's audit record (this function does NOT call
336	    datetime.now()).
337	
338	    Composition is FAIL-CLOSED per `_compose_final_verdict`. On a Mirror fail-closed
339	    (MirrorCitationError/MirrorBindingError) the pipeline SHORT-CIRCUITS: Sentinel and Judge
340	    do not run, `final_verdict = UNSUPPORTED`, and the raw sub-results are None — but the
341	    RecordingTransport still holds the served-identity record for the Mirror call(s) that did
342	    fire, so the identity gate sees them.
343	    """
344	    recording = RecordingTransport(transport)
345	
346	    mirror_slug = model_slugs["mirror"]
347	    sentinel_slug = model_slugs["sentinel"]
348	    judge_slug = model_slugs["judge"]
349	
350	    mirror_result: MirrorPass2 | None = None
351	    sentinel_result: SentinelResult | None = None
352	    judge_result: Verdict | None = None
353	    raw_judge_verdict: Verdict | None = None
354	    citation_id: str | None = None
355	    mirror_failed_closed = False
356	
357	    # --- stage 14: Mirror (fail CLOSED) ----------------------------------------------
358	    # Catch the grounding/binding/parse errors EXPLICITLY (NOT `except: pass`) so they drive the
359	    # UNSUPPORTED override; any OTHER exception propagates (a transport fault is not a verdict).
360	    # MirrorParseError (#1028): a verifier that responded but returned un-classifiable JSON is a
361	    # VERDICT-level failure (fail closed for THIS claim), NOT a transport fault — so one malformed
362	    # pass-2 response degrades a single claim to UNSUPPORTED instead of crashing the whole run.
363	    try:
364	        mirror_result, mirror_records = run_mirror(
365	            recording, claim, evidence_documents, model_slug=mirror_slug
366	        )
367	        citation_id = _first_grounded_citation_id(mirror_records)
368	    except (MirrorCitationError, MirrorBindingError, MirrorParseError):
369	        mirror_failed_closed = True
370	    except RoleTransportError as exc:
371	        # I-beatboth-006 (#1283) Fix C.1: a force-closed Mirror `RoleTransportError` (the bounded
372	        # transport's fail-closed output, §3.1) must NOT propagate to the seam teardown. Compose
373	        # step-1 keys off the `mirror_failed_closed` BOOL (a returned tuple would not fire it, and
374	        # Sentinel+Judge would still run on a hung socket), so the Mirror fault MUST drive the bool
375	        # HERE — this is the ONLY site that sets it. Set `mirror_failed_closed=True` (compose returns
376	        # UNSUPPORTED + the `if not mirror_failed_closed:` short-circuit at :334 skips Sentinel+Judge —
377	        # fast-fail, no further transport calls on a hung socket) and append the synthetic
378	        # `<mirror_role_unavailable>` disclosure record DIRECTLY to `recording.records` (so the
379	        # served-identity / audit layer sees the Mirror was unavailable for THIS claim, and the record
380	        # reaches `ClaimPipelineResult.records`). FAIL-CLOSED, can only tighten — never a synthesized
381	        # PASS. Flag OFF (operator disabled the degrade) -> re-raise so the §3.3 C.3 seam hard-halt
382	        # branch (not a raw teardown) handles it. `_compose_final_verdict` (LOCKED) is UNTOUCHED.
383	        if not role_transport_degrade_enabled():
384	            raise
385	        mirror_failed_closed = True
386	        recording.records.append(
387	            RoleCallRecord(
388	                role="mirror",
389	                model_slug=mirror_slug,
390	                served_model=None,
391	                raw_text=(
392	                    f"<mirror_role_unavailable>{type(exc).__name__}: {exc}"
393	                    "</mirror_role_unavailable>"
394	                ),
395	                parsed=None,
396	            )
397	        )
398	
399	    # --- stage 15 + 16: Sentinel -> Judge (only if Mirror produced a grounded claim) -----
400	    if not mirror_failed_closed:
401	        # I-beatboth-006 (#1283) Fix C.2-merge-sentinel: the already-shipped Sentinel degrade
402	        # (sentinel_adapter.py, PG_SENTINEL_TRANSPORT_DEGRADE) builds its `<sentinel_role_unavailable>`
403	        # `RoleCallRecord` (served_model=None) and returns it, but this call site previously DISCARDED
404	        # the list into `_sentinel_records` while `ClaimPipelineResult.records` is sourced ONLY from
405	        # `recording.records` (RecordingTransport.complete appends ONLY after a SUCCESSFUL complete(),
406	        # never on the raising `RoleTransportError` path). So the disclosure record never reached
407	        # `ClaimPipelineResult.records`. Consume the list and append ONLY the synthetic marked record
408	        # (served_model is None + the `_role_unavailable>` marker) — selective, degrade-only,
409	        # exactly-once. The Sentinel `blank_records` carry a REAL served_model (each blank-200 already
410	        # transited RecordingTransport.complete -> already in recording.records), so the predicate
411	        # selects ONLY the synthetic unavailable record, never a blank-retry record (no double-count).
412	        sentinel_result, sentinel_records = run_sentinel(
413	            recording, claim, evidence_documents, model_slug=sentinel_slug
414	        )
415	        for _rec in sentinel_records:
416	            if _rec.served_model is None and _ROLE_UNAVAILABLE_MARKER in (_rec.raw_text or ""):
417	                recording.records.append(_rec)  # degrade-only, exactly-once
418	        # F06 (GH I-arch-004): atom-coverage completeness cross-check. The decomposition Sentinel's
419	        # GROUNDED verdict only gates on the model's OWN atom bookkeeping (non-empty atoms, zero
420	        # unsupported); it never checks that the atoms COVER every assertion in the claim. A half-
421	        # decomposed clinical sentence (efficacy clause atomized + supported, contraindication clause
422	        # silently dropped) therefore reaches GROUNDED. When the flag is ON and the Sentinel returned
423	        # a parsed_ok GROUNDED with an atom list (decomposition mode populates `atoms`; guardian /
424	        # noninverted modes leave it None and are SKIPPED), require every substantive clause to be
425	        # represented by an atom. An uncovered clause -> DOWNGRADE the Sentinel verdict to UNGROUNDED
426	        # (STRICTER, can only tighten). `parsed_ok` stays True — the output parsed cleanly; it is
427	        # genuinely ungrounded by coverage, mirroring the contract's own contradiction-veto. The
428	        # LOCKED `_compose_final_verdict` body is UNTOUCHED: its step-2 `sentinel_unsafe` branch then
429	        # downgrades a Judge VERIFIED/PARTIAL to UNSUPPORTED exactly as for any UNGROUNDED Sentinel.
430	        # Default OFF (F05 precedent) -> this whole block is a no-op and behavior is byte-identical.
431	        if (
432	            sentinel_atom_coverage_enabled()
433	            and sentinel_result is not None
434	            and sentinel_result.parsed_ok
435	            and sentinel_result.verdict == SentinelVerdict.GROUNDED
436	            and sentinel_result.atoms is not None
437	            and not atom_coverage_complete(claim, sentinel_result.atoms)
438	        ):
439	            sentinel_result = sentinel_result._replace(
440	                verdict=SentinelVerdict.UNGROUNDED
441	            )
442	        evidence_text = "\n\n".join(doc.text for doc in evidence_documents)
443	        # Judge fails LOUD by design: a non-enum token raises JudgeEnumError, which we do NOT
444	        # catch — a missing/garbage arbiter verdict must propagate, never coerce to a default.
445	        # F05 (GH #1254): thread the Sentinel's per-atom "why" detail into the Judge prompt (not
446	        # just the compressed `verdict.value` token). With PG_JUDGE_SENTINEL_ATOMS OFF (default)
447	        # this is a no-op — the request builder ignores `sentinel_atoms`. The Sentinel-override
448	        # block below is UNTOUCHED and still fires unconditionally on the UNGROUNDED/unparsed path;
449	        # the atom detail only enriches the GROUNDED-path prompt where the Judge is sole arbiter.
450	        # I-beatboth-006 (#1283) Fix C.2-merge: the Judge adapter (C.2) builds a fail-closed UNSUPPORTED
451	        # verdict + a `<judge_role_unavailable>` `RoleCallRecord` (served_model=None) on a force-close
452	        # `RoleTransportError` and returns them in `(UNSUPPORTED, [record])`. Its VERDICT flows straight
453	        # through compose (never None), but this call site previously DISCARDED the record-list into
454	        # `_judge_records` while `ClaimPipelineResult.records` comes ONLY from `recording.records`. On
455	        # the success path `run_judge`'s served call already transited RecordingTransport.complete() ->
456	        # its {parsed=None} record is ALREADY in recording.records (the adapter ALSO returns a
457	        # {parsed=verdict} record for the SAME call), so appending the WHOLE list would double-count.
458	        # Append ONLY the synthetic marked record (served_model is None + marker) — uniquely built in
459	        # the adapter's except arm, NEVER transited a successful complete() (the call raised), so this
460	        # yields it exactly once.
461	        raw_judge_verdict, judge_records = run_judge(
462	            recording,
463	            claim,
464	            evidence_text,
465	            mirror_verdict=str(mirror_result.classification),
466	            sentinel_verdict=sentinel_result.verdict.value,
467	            model_slug=judge_slug,
468	            sentinel_atoms=sentinel_result.atoms,
469	        )
470	        for _rec in judge_records:
471	            if _rec.served_model is None and _ROLE_UNAVAILABLE_MARKER in (_rec.raw_text or ""):
472	                recording.records.append(_rec)  # degrade-only, exactly-once
473	        judge_result = raw_judge_verdict
474	
475	    final_verdict = _compose_final_verdict(
476	        mirror_failed_closed=mirror_failed_closed,
477	        sentinel_result=sentinel_result,
478	        raw_judge_verdict=raw_judge_verdict,
479	    )
480	
481	    d8_row = D8ClaimRow(
482	        claim_id=claim_id,
483	        severity=severity,
484	        verdict=final_verdict,
485	        citation_id=citation_id,
486	        s0_categories=list(s0_categories),
487	    )
488	
489	    return ClaimPipelineResult(
490	        d8_row=d8_row,
491	        raw_judge_verdict=raw_judge_verdict,
492	        final_verdict=final_verdict,
493	        records=recording.records,
494	        mirror_result=mirror_result,
495	        sentinel_result=sentinel_result,
496	        judge_result=judge_result,
497	    )
498

1	"""Shared transport + result types for the POLARIS 4-role adapters (I-meta-002 sub-PR-4).
2	
3	Pure data contracts + a typing Protocol. There is NO network here and NO spend: the
4	real transport (the thing that actually performs an LLM completion) is sub-PR-5 and is
5	DEPENDENCY-INJECTED into each adapter. Tests inject a MOCK transport that returns canned
6	`RoleResponse`s. This module deliberately does NOT import `openrouter_client` (the real
7	transport that wraps it is sub-PR-5).
8	
9	Per-call identity capture (iter-3 fix, Codex P1-a): every adapter returns ONE
10	`RoleCallRecord` per transport completion so the Path-B identity gate (sub-PR-5) can verify
11	`served==pinned` for EVERY call — no completion may be hidden inside an adapter without its
12	own record. Single-call roles (Sentinel, Judge) emit a 1-element list; Mirror (two passes)
13	emits a 2-element list.
14	"""
15	
16	from __future__ import annotations
17	
18	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
19	from typing import Any, Protocol, runtime_checkable
20	
21	from src.polaris_graph.roles.mirror_contract import CitationSpan
22	
23	
24	@dataclass(frozen=True)
25	class EvidenceDocument:
26	    """One evidence document handed to a role, carrying a STABLE `doc_id`.
27	
28	    The `doc_id` is the citation identity the Mirror adapter binds against (iter-4): a
29	    citation span may only point at a `doc_id` that appears in the supplied documents, so
30	    the document set is the ground-truth identity pool, not a free-text payload.
31	    """
32	
33	    doc_id: str
34	    text: str
35	
36	
37	@dataclass
38	class RoleRequest:
39	    """A role-tagged request to the transport.
40	
41	    `role` and `model_slug` are the pinned identity the Path-B gate (sub-PR-5) checks the
42	    served identity against. The payload is carried as either a chat `messages` list OR a
43	    single `prompt` string (a role uses whichever its serving convention requires). `params`
44	    holds everything else the transport needs, including the structured-output spec
45	    (`structured_outputs`), the bounded `max_tokens`, and the `documents` payload (kept in
46	    `params["documents"]` so it is introspectable by the adequacy/identity checks and the
47	    tests).
48	    """
49	
50	    role: str
51	    model_slug: str
52	    messages: list[dict] | None = None
53	    prompt: str | None = None
54	    params: dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
55	
56	
57	@dataclass
58	class RoleResponse:
59	    """A transport completion.
60	
61	    `raw_text` is the model's raw output string (parsed by the role's sub-PR-2 contract).
62	    `served_model` is the identity the transport actually served (the Path-B gate compares
63	    it to the pinned `model_slug`). `citations` is the OPTIONAL structured-span path for the
64	    managed Mirror route (iter-3 P1-b): when the transport already normalized citations into
65	    spans, they arrive here; otherwise the adapter parses `<co>` spans out of `raw_text`.
66	    """
67	
68	    raw_text: str
69	    served_model: str | None = None
70	    usage: dict[str, Any] | None = None
71	    citations: list[CitationSpan] | None = None
72	    # I-meta-002-q1b (#939): the model's verifier REASONING, separated from `raw_text` at the
73	    # transport so the verdict parsers only ever see the bare verdict/body (no "soap") AND the
74	    # reasoning is captured for line-by-line review. None when the role emitted no reasoning.
75	    reasoning: str | None = None
76	    # I-deepfix-001 B6 FIX 2 (#1370): the ROUTING SLUG of the provider that actually served this
77	    # completion (the SAME `slug_for_provider(raw['provider'])` identity the transport's blank-200
78	    # rotation already uses for `provider.order`/`ignore`). It lets the Judge off-enum re-ask
79	    # (judge_adapter WS-1(b)) rotate OFF a provider whose load-balanced endpoint garbles the
80	    # reasoning channel. None when the transport did not report a served provider (e.g. a mock, the
81	    # self-host path, or a fault before any response). Defaulted last so every existing positional
82	    # + keyword construction stays byte-identical.
83	    served_provider: str | None = None
84	
85	
86	@dataclass
87	class RoleCallRecord:
88	    """One captured transport completion for the Path-B identity/capture gate (sub-PR-5).
89

97	    served_model: str | None

101	    # so `run_four_role_evaluation` can persist it to `four_role_role_calls.jsonl` SEPARATELY from
102	    # the verdict. None when the role emitted no reasoning.

1	"""Shared HARD total-deadline force-close helper for the role-transport POST (I-beatboth-006 #1283).
2	
3	Fix A lifts the PROVEN OpenRouter-side total-deadline wrapper into a self-contained, pure helper
4	so the SOVEREIGN `openai_compatible_transport.py` POST is bounded by the SAME force-close wall the
5	OpenRouter path uses — a trickle-hung sovereign socket on a D8 claim worker can no longer hang the
6	gate forever. The wrapper runs the blocking POST on a 1-worker executor, waits at most ``total_s``,
7	and on expiry FORCE-CLOSES the client so the orphaned worker's blocked C-level read errors out and
8	the thread exits. On exhaustion the caller raises the UNCHANGED fail-closed ``RoleTransportError``
9	(consumed per-claim by Fix C). FAITHFULNESS-NEUTRAL: this is transport client lifecycle + a wall-
10	deadline only — no verdict logic, no gate threshold.
11	
12	Scope note (I-beatboth-006 #1283, design §3.1.1 + §5/§6 reconciliation): the OpenRouter transport
13	(`openrouter_role_transport.py`) RETAINS its proven IN-PLACE copy of ``_post_with_total_deadline`` /
14	``_role_transport_total_s`` / ``_default_role_http_client`` this PR — it is the battle-tested path
15	(F3 / #1264 / #1226 / #1173) and is listed clean/no-change in the design's §6, so it is NOT touched
16	here. Only the sovereign transport imports this module. Migrating OpenRouter to import from here is a
17	DISCLOSED follow-up (a 2-line import swap), kept out of this reliability PR so the proven path stays
18	byte-identical. The two copies are KEPT IN SYNC — same pattern the local convention already uses
19	between these two transports (cf. ``_SENTINEL_DECOMPOSITION_MIN_MAX_TOKENS`` "Kept in sync with
20	openrouter_role_transport...").
21	
22	LAW VI: every knob is env-driven (``PG_ROLE_TRANSPORT_TOTAL_S``), read at CALL time so a slate env
23	override after import wins. No magic numbers.
24	"""
25	
26	from __future__ import annotations
27	
28	import concurrent.futures
29	import os
30	
31	import httpx
32	
33	# The per-call total wall-deadline default. Mirrors openrouter_role_transport._role_transport_total_s
34	# (kept in sync): 900s comfortably exceeds a HEALTHY seconds-to-minutes role call (so the healthy path
35	# is byte-identical) while bounding a trickle hang. LAW VI: env-driven, read at CALL time.
36	_ROLE_TRANSPORT_TOTAL_S_ENV = "PG_ROLE_TRANSPORT_TOTAL_S"
37	_ROLE_TRANSPORT_TOTAL_S_DEFAULT = "900"
38	
39	
40	def role_transport_total_s() -> float:
41	    """The per-call total wall-deadline (seconds), read at CALL time so a slate env override wins.
42	
43	    Mirror of ``openrouter_role_transport._role_transport_total_s`` (kept in sync). A non-numeric /
44	    unparseable override falls back to the generous 900s default (never aborts every call at 0s).
45	    """
46	    try:
47	        return float(os.getenv(_ROLE_TRANSPORT_TOTAL_S_ENV, _ROLE_TRANSPORT_TOTAL_S_DEFAULT))
48	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
49	        return float(_ROLE_TRANSPORT_TOTAL_S_DEFAULT)
50	
51	
52	def default_role_http_client(timeout_seconds: int) -> httpx.Client:
53	    """Rebuild a fresh role-transport client after a total-deadline FORCE-CLOSE (prod path only).
54	
55	    Only ever invoked on a genuine trickle-hang timeout — NEVER in tests, where the injected
56	    MockTransport returns instantly so the deadline never fires. Role headers are per-request and the
57	    endpoint URL is absolute, so a timeout-configured client is sufficient for ``/chat/completions``.
58	    Mirrors ``openrouter_role_transport._default_role_http_client`` (kept in sync).
59	    """
60	    return httpx.Client(timeout=timeout_seconds)
61	
62	
63	def post_with_total_deadline(client, url, *, json_body, headers, timeout, total_s):
64	    """Run the blocking role POST under a HARD total wall-deadline; force-close the client on expiry.
65	
66	    Returns the ``httpx.Response`` on success; re-raises ``concurrent.futures.TimeoutError`` (after
67	    force-closing the hung client) so the caller's bounded retry can rebuild + retry, then fail closed.
68	    Byte-identical in shape to ``openrouter_role_transport._post_with_total_deadline`` (kept in sync).
69	    """
70	    ex = concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=1)
71	    fut = ex.submit(client.post, url, json=json_body, headers=headers, timeout=timeout)
72	    try:
73	        return fut.result(timeout=total_s)
74	    except concurrent.futures.TimeoutError:
75	        try:
76	            client.close()  # force the hung socket closed -> the worker's blocked read unblocks + exits
77	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
78	            pass
79	        raise
80	    finally:
81	        # Deterministic executor teardown on EVERY exit path; wait=False so an unsticking worker never blocks us.
82	        ex.shutdown(wait=False)
83

1	"""Sentinel adapter — request builder + FAIL-CLOSED caller (3 groundedness modes).
2	
3	THREE (prompt, parser) contracts, selected by mode (env- + model-aware, LAW VI):
4	  - "guardian"      (sovereign granite-Guardian): assistant claim turn + a FINAL user
5	    `<guardian>` groundedness block + `documents`; the model emits `<score>yes|no</score>`
6	    (parsed by `parse_sentinel_score`, yes=UNGROUNDED).
7	  - "noninverted"   (general granite): the DIRECT one-word GROUNDED/UNGROUNDED block
8	    (parsed by `parse_sentinel_grounded_token`).
9	  - "decomposition" (CERTIFIED MiniMax-M2, I-run11-004): a SINGLE user message with the certified
10	    claim-DECOMPOSITION + span-coverage prompt (span+claim inline), `response_format` json_object;
11	    the model emits JSON {verdict, unsupported_atoms, atoms} (parsed by
12	    `parse_sentinel_decomposition`, "supported"=GROUNDED / "unsupported"=UNGROUNDED). The
13	    production decomposition call REPLICATES the certified call (verbatim `_DECOMPOSITION_PROMPT`,
14	    reasoning ON via the transport, max_tokens>=3000) so the 0-false-accept certification transfers.
15	
16	FAIL CLOSED (lethal-inversion guard, ALL modes): a malformed/empty output OR a transport error
17	yields `SentinelResult(UNGROUNDED, parsed_ok=False)`. There is NO path that returns GROUNDED on
18	bad or missing output. Polarity/mapping lives in the contract, never re-derived here.
19	"""

21	from __future__ import annotations
22	
23	import os
24	
25	# I-meta-008: the Sentinel fail-closed catch must NOT swallow a budget-cap breach. The hook in
26	# RecordingTransport.complete raises BudgetExceededError from inside transport.complete(); a
27	# typed re-raise guard ahead of the broad `except` keeps the cap a HARD ABORT (never a
28	# fail-closed UNGROUNDED verdict). Mirrors entailment_judge.py:255-258.
29	import src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client as _orc
30	
31	from src.polaris_graph.roles.openai_compatible_transport import RoleTransportError
32	from src.polaris_graph.roles.role_transport import (
33	    EvidenceDocument,
34	    RoleCallRecord,
35	    RoleRequest,
36	    RoleResponse,
37	    RoleTransport,

39	from src.polaris_graph.roles.sentinel_contract import (
40	    SentinelResult,
41	    SentinelVerdict,
42	    parse_sentinel_decomposition,
43	    parse_sentinel_grounded_token,
44	    parse_sentinel_score,
45	)

47	_ROLE = "sentinel"
