49	# === GROUNDEDNESS MODE selection (I-run11-002 L1, LAW VI) ====================================
50	# Two distinct (prompt, parser) contracts, selected by `PG_SENTINEL_GROUNDEDNESS_MODE`:
51	#   - "noninverted" (DEFAULT for the OpenRouter/benchmark Sentinel): a DIRECT one-word
52	#     GROUNDED/UNGROUNDED prompt, parsed by `parse_sentinel_grounded_token`. Run 11 proved the
53	#     general `ibm-granite/granite-4.1-8b` ignores the inverted Guardian instruction and answers
54	#     naturally, so the inverted contract mislabeled every grounded claim as UNGROUNDED. The
55	#     non-inverted prompt discriminates cleanly on the same general granite model
56	#     (outputs/audits/I-run11-002/l1_groundedness_probe.md).
57	#   - "guardian" (the SOVEREIGN self-host Sentinel): the INVERTED `<guardian>` block + the strict
58	#     `<score>yes|no</score>` parser (yes=risk=UNGROUNDED). KEPT BYTE-FOR-BYTE because the
59	#     task-trained self-host `granite-guardian-4.1-8b` is trained on the yes=risk polarity.
60	#
61	# DEFAULT WIRING (the runtime-desync guard): when `PG_SENTINEL_GROUNDEDNESS_MODE` is unset, the
62	# mode is DERIVED from `PG_FOUR_ROLE_TRANSPORT` so the prompt can never silently desync from the
63	# served model — "self_host" (the sovereign granite-Guardian route) defaults to "guardian";
64	# "openrouter"/unset (the benchmark general-granite route) defaults to "noninverted". An explicit
65	# `PG_SENTINEL_GROUNDEDNESS_MODE` always overrides. Both read lazily (per call) so a post-import
66	# override is honored (mirrors `role_reasoning_enabled` / `four_role_transport_mode`).
67	_GROUNDEDNESS_MODE_ENV = "PG_SENTINEL_GROUNDEDNESS_MODE"
68	_MODE_NONINVERTED = "noninverted"
69	_MODE_GUARDIAN = "guardian"
70	# I-run11-004: the CERTIFIED MiniMax-M2 claim-decomposition + span-coverage mode (the new lock +
71	# benchmark Sentinel). Single-user-message span+claim prompt, JSON verdict parsed by
72	# `parse_sentinel_decomposition`. Valid for PG_SENTINEL_GROUNDEDNESS_MODE=decomposition.
73	_MODE_DECOMPOSITION = "decomposition"
74	_VALID_MODES = (_MODE_NONINVERTED, _MODE_GUARDIAN, _MODE_DECOMPOSITION)
75	# The transport env the default derives from (literals kept in sync with run_gate_b.py's
76	# `_FOUR_ROLE_TRANSPORT_ENV` / `_TRANSPORT_SELF_HOST`; NOT imported, to avoid a scripts->src cycle).

80	# I-run11-004: model-aware default mode selection. When PG_SENTINEL_GROUNDEDNESS_MODE is UNSET, the
81	# default mode depends on the CONFIGURED Sentinel slug so the prompt+parser can never silently
82	# desync from the served model:
83	#   - a granite-guardian model -> "guardian"  (the inverted <score>yes|no</score> contract);
84	#   - a minimax model          -> "decomposition" (the certified MiniMax-M2 detector);
85	#   - else                     -> the transport-derived default (self_host->guardian, else noninverted).
86	# Substring tokens (matched case-insensitively against the lock/PG_SENTINEL_MODEL slug). Kept in
87	# sync with openrouter_client._FAMILY_PREFIXES (granite / minimax) WITHOUT importing it, so the
88	# adapter has no import-time dependency on the family registry.
89	_SENTINEL_GUARDIAN_SLUG_TOKEN = "granite-guardian"
90	_SENTINEL_DECOMPOSITION_SLUG_TOKENS = ("minimax/", "minimax-")

92	# FIX 2 (A3 sentinel transport-blank retry, I-arch-007). A flaky socket can return
93	# a success HTTP-200 with an EMPTY raw_text; today that empty body falls straight
94	# into the parser, which fail-closes to SentinelResult(UNGROUNDED, parsed_ok=False)
95	# (_FAIL_CLOSED). That collapses a TRANSPORT BLANK ("the verifier returned nothing")
96	# with a GENUINE UNGROUNDED downstream (role_pipeline._compose_final_verdict), and
97	# downgrades a Judge VERIFIED/PARTIAL to UNSUPPORTED on a transient empty 200.
98	#
99	# This flag enables up to N EXTRA same-request transport.complete calls — and ONLY
100	# on the RAW-TEXT-BLANK discriminator `not raw_text or not raw_text.strip()`, NEVER
101	# on parsed_ok==False (which also covers a NON-EMPTY-but-unparseable output and a
102	# GENUINE clean UNGROUNDED — both must still downgrade exactly as today, no retry).
103	#
104	# DEFAULT 0 => byte-identical: no extra transport call, the empty 200 falls into the
105	# parser exactly as before, and the RoleCallRecord list stays 1 element. On retry
106	# EXHAUSTION the result STAYS SentinelResult(UNGROUNDED, parsed_ok=False) — the retry
107	# NEVER converts a blank into GROUNDED. Each attempt is recorded so the blank is
108	# auditable. A BudgetExceededError (cap breach) is a HARD ABORT, never retried; a
109	# RoleTransportError (persistent transport fault) still PROPAGATES and HOLDS the
110	# claim. Read lazily per call (mirrors the mode helpers above).
111	_SENTINEL_BLANK_RETRIES_ENV = "PG_SENTINEL_BLANK_RETRIES"
112	
113	
114	def _sentinel_blank_retries() -> int:
115	    """FIX 2: max EXTRA Sentinel transport calls on a RAW-TEXT-BLANK empty-200.
116	
117	    DEFAULT 0 (DISABLED) => byte-identical (no extra call). A non-integer or
118	    negative value clamps to 0 (fail-safe to legacy, never unbounded). Read at
119	    CALL time so a post-import override / test toggle is honored (LAW VI)."""
120	    raw = os.getenv(_SENTINEL_BLANK_RETRIES_ENV, "0").strip()
121	    try:
122	        value = int(raw)
123	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
124	        return 0
125	    return value if value > 0 else 0

127	
128	# I-beatboth-006 (#1283) Fix C: the UNIFIED seam degrade switch (default ON). The Sentinel arm reads
129	# PG_ROLE_TRANSPORT_DEGRADE as the primary switch (one switch for Mirror+Judge+Sentinel) and honors
130	# the legacy PG_SENTINEL_TRANSPORT_DEGRADE as a back-compat ALIAS, so an operator who set only the
131	# old switch still degrades/halts the Sentinel arm identically. Mirrors judge_adapter's helper.
132	_ROLE_TRANSPORT_DEGRADE_ENV = "PG_ROLE_TRANSPORT_DEGRADE"
133	_SENTINEL_TRANSPORT_DEGRADE_ALIAS_ENV = "PG_SENTINEL_TRANSPORT_DEGRADE"
134	_DEGRADE_OFF_TOKENS = ("0", "false", "no", "off")

140	    is the back-compat ALIAS (consulted only when the new switch is unset). LAW VI: read lazily."""
141	    primary = os.getenv(_ROLE_TRANSPORT_DEGRADE_ENV)

168	def _model_aware_default_mode(slug_override: str | None = None) -> str:
169	    """Default Sentinel mode when PG_SENTINEL_GROUNDEDNESS_MODE is UNSET (I-run11-004).
170	
171	    MODEL-AWARE first, so the lock Sentinel (MiniMax-M2) gets the DECOMPOSITION prompt+parser and a
172	    granite-guardian Sentinel still gets the inverted guardian contract — no silent desync between
173	    the served model and the prompt:
174	      - configured slug is a granite-guardian model -> "guardian";
175	      - configured slug is a minimax model          -> "decomposition";
176	      - otherwise -> the prior transport-derived default ("self_host" -> "guardian", else
177	        "noninverted" — the benchmark general-granite route, preserved for back-compat).

181	    by `role_endpoint`. Deriving the mode from the real served slug (not the `PG_SENTINEL_MODEL` env)
182	    closes the desync where a stale `PG_SENTINEL_MODEL=granite-guardian` would build the Guardian

197	def sentinel_groundedness_mode(slug_override: str | None = None) -> str:
198	    """Resolve the active Sentinel groundedness mode (LAW VI).
199	
200	    Returns "noninverted" | "guardian" | "decomposition".
201	
202	    Precedence: an explicit `PG_SENTINEL_GROUNDEDNESS_MODE` ("noninverted" | "guardian" |
203	    "decomposition") ALWAYS wins. An explicit but UNRECOGNIZED value raises ValueError (Codex
204	    diff-gate P2, no-silent-fallback: a mode typo must not silently desync the prompt+parser from
205	    the served model). When the env is UNSET, the default is MODEL-AWARE (I-run11-004): a
206	    granite-guardian slug -> "guardian"; a minimax slug -> "decomposition"; otherwise the
207	    transport-derived default ("self_host" -> "guardian", else "noninverted").
208	    """
209	    override = os.getenv(_GROUNDEDNESS_MODE_ENV)
210	    if override is not None and override.strip():
211	        token = override.strip().lower()
212	        if token in _VALID_MODES:
213	            return token
214	        # Fail LOUD on an explicit unrecognized mode (LAW II no-silent-fallback).
215	        raise ValueError(
216	            f"{_GROUNDEDNESS_MODE_ENV}={override!r} is invalid; "
217	            f"expected one of {_VALID_MODES!r}."
218	        )
219	    # Unset/blank: model-aware default (then transport-derived for an unrecognized model). The
220	    # served slug (Codex diff-gate iter-3 P1) is threaded so the mode matches the ACTUAL model.

224	def _resolve_mode(mode: str | None, model_slug: str | None = None) -> str:
225	    """Resolve + VALIDATE the active mode (Codex diff-gate P2, no-silent-fallback). An explicit
226	    but unrecognized `mode` argument must NOT silently select a parser — it raises ValueError,
227	    so a caller typo cannot desync the prompt from the parser. None -> env-gated resolution keyed
228	    on the ACTUAL `model_slug` being served (iter-3 P1: no PG_SENTINEL_MODEL-vs-lock desync)."""
229	    resolved = mode if mode is not None else sentinel_groundedness_mode(model_slug)
230	    if resolved not in _VALID_MODES:
231	        raise ValueError(
232	            f"sentinel groundedness mode {resolved!r} is invalid; "
233	            f"expected one of {_VALID_MODES!r}."
234	        )
235	    return resolved

238	# The prescribed groundedness instruction wrapped in the final user `<guardian>` block.

240	# NOT inlined here, so the calling convention stays auditable. SOVEREIGN ("guardian") mode ONLY.
241	_GUARDIAN_BLOCK = (
242	    "<guardian>groundedness</guardian>\n"
243	    "Assess whether the assistant's claim is grounded in the provided documents. "
244	    "Respond with exactly <score>yes</score> if the claim is NOT grounded (risk present) "
245	    "or <score>no</score> if it IS grounded (no risk). Output only the score element."
246	)

248	# The NON-INVERTED groundedness instruction (BENCHMARK / "noninverted" mode). VALIDATED VERBATIM
249	# by the L1 probe (scripts/diagnostics/sentinel_groundedness_probe.py `_NONINVERTED_BLOCK`):
250	# `ibm-granite/granite-4.1-8b` returned GROUNDED on the verbatim-quoted (grounded) fixture and
251	# UNGROUNDED on the on-topic-fabricated fixture under this exact wording. Asks for ONE word so
252	# `parse_sentinel_grounded_token` (word-boundary GROUNDED|UNGROUNDED) can read it. Documents are
253	# rendered model-visible by `_normalize_messages` (the leading evidence message), identical to the
254	# guardian path; only the FINAL user instruction differs.
255	_NONINVERTED_BLOCK = (
256	    "You are a strict groundedness checker. Decide whether the assistant's CLAIM is fully "
257	    "supported by (entailed by) the EVIDENCE DOCUMENTS shown above. A claim is GROUNDED only if "
258	    "every factual assertion in it — including any number, percentage, or named entity — is "
259	    "directly stated in or directly entailed by the documents. If the claim adds any fact, number, "
260	    "or detail not present in the documents, it is UNGROUNDED.\n"
261	    "Answer with EXACTLY one word: GROUNDED or UNGROUNDED. Output only that single word."
262	)
263	
264	# === CERTIFIED DECOMPOSITION prompt (MiniMax-M2, I-run11-004) =================================
265	# COPIED VERBATIM from the CERTIFIED harness scripts/diagnostics/sentinel_bakeoff.py `GLM_PROMPT`
266	# so the certification (0 false-accepts on 28 fabrications, over-flag 0.107 on the 56-item fixture)
267	# TRANSFERS to production. It has `{span}` and `{claim}` `.format` fields and `{{...}}` escaped
268	# JSON braces. The atomic-decomposition + grammatical-voice attribution rules are the heart of the
269	# detector — DO NOT paraphrase or trim. The production decomposition call inlines span+claim in a
270	# SINGLE user message (NOT the guardian documents-channel layout) so the served call matches the
271	# certified one exactly.
272	_DECOMPOSITION_PROMPT = """You are a strict faithfulness checker for a clinical-grade research pipeline. You are given a SPAN of source text and a CLAIM that cites ONLY that span. Your job: decide whether EVERY factual assertion in the CLAIM is supported by the SPAN alone.
273	
274	STEP 1 — Decompose the CLAIM into atomic sub-assertions. Separate them into:
275	  - mechanism/fact atoms (what happens, numbers, findings),
276	  - attribution atoms (WHO said / did / authored / found something — any named person, group, or framework),
277	  - relation atoms (causal or "offsets / counterbalances / compensates" links between two things).
278	List every atom; do not merge two assertions into one.
279	
280	STEP 2 — Check EACH atom against the SPAN ONLY. An atom is:
281	  - "supported" if the SPAN states it (conservative paraphrase allowed), OR
282	  - "unsupported" if the SPAN does not state it.
283	
284	Rules that decide hard cases:
285	  - SCOPE / OFFSET: if the CLAIM says one thing "offsets / counterbalances / compensates for / cancels" another, the SPAN must actually state that offsetting relation. The SPAN merely listing both things separately (e.g. "raises output" AND "displaces labor") does NOT support an "offset" relation atom — that atom is unsupported.
286	  - ATTRIBUTION by grammatical voice:
287	      * If the SPAN attributes a result with FIRST PERSON ("We present...", "We show...", "Our framework..."), then a CLAIM atom that names the cited source's own authors as the source IS supported (the source is speaking about itself).
288	      * If the SPAN attributes a result with a THIRD-PERSON pronoun that has NO proper-noun antecedent inside the SPAN ("He applies...", "She finds...", "They argue..."), then a CLAIM atom that names a SPECIFIC PERSON as the source is UNSUPPORTED — that named identity is not present in the SPAN.
289	      * If the SPAN names the person explicitly, an attribution atom naming that same person is supported.
290	  - SPECIFICITY: if the CLAIM names a specific entity/number/mechanism the SPAN does not contain, that atom is unsupported.
291	
292	STEP 3 — Verdict: "unsupported" if ANY atom is unsupported; otherwise "supported".
293	
294	Return STRICT JSON only, no prose outside it:
295	{{"atoms": [{{"atom": "<text>", "type": "mechanism|attribution|relation", "status": "supported|unsupported", "why": "<short>"}}], "unsupported_atoms": <int>, "verdict": "supported" | "unsupported"}}
296	
297	SPAN:
298	{span}
299	
300	CLAIM:
301	{claim}
302	
303	JSON:"""
304	
305	# The fail-closed result returned on transport error / malformed output. NEVER GROUNDED.
306	_FAIL_CLOSED = SentinelResult(SentinelVerdict.UNGROUNDED, parsed_ok=False)
307	
308	
309	def build_sentinel_request(
310	    claim: str,
311	    evidence_documents: list[EvidenceDocument],
312	    *,
313	    model_slug: str,
314	    mode: str | None = None,
315	) -> RoleRequest:
316	    """Build a Sentinel groundedness request for the active mode (I-run11-002 L1 / I-run11-004).
317	
318	    `mode` selects BOTH the message LAYOUT and the FINAL user instruction:
319	      - "guardian"    -> the INVERTED `<guardian>` block (sovereign self-host granite-Guardian).
320	      - "noninverted" -> the DIRECT one-word GROUNDED/UNGROUNDED block (general granite).
321	      - "decomposition" -> the CERTIFIED MiniMax-M2 single-user-message span+claim prompt.
322	
323	    GUARDIAN / NONINVERTED layout (F3): assistant turn = the claim under check; final user turn =
324	    the groundedness instruction; documents ride in `params["documents"]` (rendered model-visible by
325	    the transport). NO structured-output spec — these emit free text the contract parses.
326	
327	    DECOMPOSITION layout (I-run11-004) REPLICATES the certified call so the certification transfers:
328	    a SINGLE user message carrying `_DECOMPOSITION_PROMPT.format(span=<all evidence .text joined>,
329	    claim=claim)` (NOT the guardian documents-channel layout), with `response_format`
330	    {"type":"json_object"} requested (the transport forwards it; the robust parser also handles
331	    non-JSON-mode output). Decomposition carries NO documents in params (the SPAN is inlined into the
332	    single prompt message, exactly as certified) so the transport prepends no separate evidence
333	    message — the live body is the certified ONE user message (Codex diff-gate iter-2 P1-2).
334	
335	    When `mode` is None it resolves from `sentinel_groundedness_mode()` (env- + model-aware, LAW VI).
336	    """
337	    resolved_mode = _resolve_mode(mode, model_slug=model_slug)
338	    documents = [{"doc_id": doc.doc_id, "text": doc.text} for doc in evidence_documents]
339	
340	    if resolved_mode == _MODE_DECOMPOSITION:
341	        # Certified single-user-message layout: span = all evidence document texts joined (the
342	        # certified harness passed one `cited_evidence_text` span; multi-doc evidence is joined so
343	        # the whole cited pool is in-span). response_format requests JSON; the parser is robust to
344	        # non-JSON output too.
345	        span = "\n\n".join(doc.text for doc in evidence_documents)
346	        user_content = _DECOMPOSITION_PROMPT.format(span=span, claim=claim)
347	        messages = [{"role": "user", "content": user_content}]
348	        # NO documents in decomposition mode (Codex diff-gate iter-2 P1-2): the SPAN is already
349	        # inlined into `user_content`, exactly as the certification ran. If documents were carried
350	        # here, the transport's _normalize_messages would PREPEND a separate evidence user message,
351	        # making the live body TWO user messages instead of the certified ONE — diverging from the
352	        # certified call and invalidating the 0-false-accept guarantee. Pass an empty list so the
353	        # transport prepends nothing; the model reads the span from the single prompt message.
354	        params = {
355	            "documents": [],
356	            "response_format": {"type": "json_object"},
357	        }
358	        return RoleRequest(
359	            role=_ROLE,
360	            model_slug=model_slug,
361	            messages=messages,
362	            params=params,
363	        )
364	
365	    instruction = _GUARDIAN_BLOCK if resolved_mode == _MODE_GUARDIAN else _NONINVERTED_BLOCK
366	    messages = [
367	        {"role": "assistant", "content": claim},
368	        {"role": "user", "content": instruction},
369	    ]
370	    params = {"documents": documents}
371	    return RoleRequest(
372	        role=_ROLE,
373	        model_slug=model_slug,
374	        messages=messages,
375	        params=params,
376	    )
377	
378	
379	def run_sentinel(
380	    transport: RoleTransport,
381	    claim: str,
382	    evidence_documents: list[EvidenceDocument],
383	    *,
384	    model_slug: str,
385	    mode: str | None = None,
386	) -> tuple[SentinelResult, list[RoleCallRecord]]:
387	    """Call the transport once and parse the groundedness output, FAIL CLOSED.
388	
389	    Returns the `SentinelResult` and a 1-element `RoleCallRecord` list (one record per
390	    completion, iter-3 P1-a). A transport error OR a malformed/empty `raw_text` both yield
391	    `SentinelResult(UNGROUNDED, parsed_ok=False)` — never GROUNDED, in EITHER mode.
392	
393	    `mode` (None -> `sentinel_groundedness_mode()`, env- + model-aware, LAW VI) selects BOTH the
394	    prompt (via `build_sentinel_request`) AND the parser, so they always pair correctly:
395	      - "guardian"      -> inverted `<score>yes|no</score>` parser (`parse_sentinel_score`).
396	      - "noninverted"   -> direct GROUNDED/UNGROUNDED parser (`parse_sentinel_grounded_token`).
397	      - "decomposition" -> certified JSON-verdict parser (`parse_sentinel_decomposition`).
398	    """
399	    resolved_mode = _resolve_mode(mode, model_slug=model_slug)
400	    if resolved_mode == _MODE_GUARDIAN:
401	        parser = parse_sentinel_score
402	    elif resolved_mode == _MODE_DECOMPOSITION:
403	        parser = parse_sentinel_decomposition
404	    else:
405	        parser = parse_sentinel_grounded_token
406	    request = build_sentinel_request(
407	        claim, evidence_documents, model_slug=model_slug, mode=resolved_mode
408	    )
409	    # FIX 2: bounded retry budget for a RAW-TEXT-BLANK empty-200 only (default 0 =>
410	    # exactly one attempt, byte-identical). Each attempt appends a RoleCallRecord so
411	    # the blank is auditable; at default the list stays a 1-element list.
412	    max_blank_retries = _sentinel_blank_retries()
413	    blank_records: list[RoleCallRecord] = []
414	    try:
415	        attempt = 0
416	        while True:
417	            response: RoleResponse = transport.complete(request)
418	            # ONLY a raw-text-blank empty-200 is retried. A non-empty-unparseable
419	            # output and a genuine clean UNGROUNDED both go straight to the parser
420	            # below (no retry) — the predicate is the raw-text blank, NOT parsed_ok.
421	            if (
422	                _is_blank_raw_text(response.raw_text)
423	                and attempt < max_blank_retries
424	            ):
425	                # Record the blank attempt so retries are visible, then re-issue the
426	                # SAME request on a fresh socket. On exhaustion we fall through to the
427	                # parser with the (still blank) raw_text -> _FAIL_CLOSED UNGROUNDED.
428	                blank_records.append(RoleCallRecord(
429	                    role=_ROLE,
430	                    model_slug=model_slug,
431	                    served_model=response.served_model,
432	                    raw_text=response.raw_text or "",
433	                    parsed=_FAIL_CLOSED,
434	                ))
435	                attempt += 1
436	                continue
437	            break
438	        result = parser(response.raw_text)
439	        served_model = response.served_model
440	        raw_text = response.raw_text
441	    except _orc.BudgetExceededError:
442	        # I-meta-008: a budget-cap breach (raised by the RecordingTransport cost hook inside
443	        # transport.complete) is a HARD ABORT, never a fail-closed UNGROUNDED verdict. Re-raise
444	        # BEFORE the broad except below so the cap actually bites on the Sentinel call. The
445	        # FIX 2 blank-retry loop is INSIDE this try, so a cap breach on a retry call also aborts
446	        # hard here (never retried past the cap).
447	        raise
448	    except RoleTransportError as exc:
449	        # B5/B7 (#1257, "nothing shall hold the report") supersedes I-run11-010 (#1056, D4):
450	        # a Sentinel transport fault that SURVIVED the bounded transport retries (effort ladder +
451	        # provider failover, openrouter_role_transport #1053) marks ONLY the sentinel role unavailable
452	        # for THIS claim (fail-closed UNGROUNDED, parsed_ok=False — NEVER GROUNDED, so
453	        # _compose_final_verdict downgrades the claim, never upgrades it) and the D8 seam CONTINUES
454	        # adjudicating every other claim. #1056's propagate-to-HOLD was right for a per-claim hold,
455	        # but in the PARALLEL seam path it does NOT hold one claim — the propagated error reaches
456	        # sweep_integration's future.result(), which cancel_futures + tears the WHOLE seam down and
457	        # hardcodes coverage_fraction=0.0, discarding ~177 successfully-adjudicated claims and (legacy)
458	        # holding the entire report. The recall cost of ONE transport-faulted claim (already
459	        # bounded-retried + provider-failed-over) is the correct trade vs nuking the real adjudication
460	        # of every other claim. Mirror/Judge verdicts on this and all other claims are untouched.
461	        # Default-ON under the always-release regime; OFF = byte-identical legacy propagate->HOLD
462	        # (which sweep_integration C.3 now converts to a DISCLOSED hard halt, not a raw teardown).
463	        # Genuine VERDICT-level faults (parse/contract — the model responded un-classifiably) still
464	        # fail-closed UNGROUNDED via the broad except below. A BudgetExceededError is already re-raised
465	        # hard ABOVE this branch. I-beatboth-006 (#1283) Fix C: the switch is the UNIFIED seam degrade
466	        # flag PG_ROLE_TRANSPORT_DEGRADE (one switch for Mirror+Judge+Sentinel); the legacy
467	        # PG_SENTINEL_TRANSPORT_DEGRADE is honored as a back-compat ALIAS so an operator who set only
468	        # the old switch still degrades/halts the Sentinel arm identically.
469	        if not _role_transport_degrade_enabled():
470	            raise
471	        record = RoleCallRecord(
472	            role=_ROLE,
473	            model_slug=model_slug,
474	            served_model=None,
475	            raw_text=f"<sentinel_role_unavailable>{type(exc).__name__}: {exc}</sentinel_role_unavailable>",
476	            parsed=_FAIL_CLOSED,
477	        )
478	        return _FAIL_CLOSED, [*blank_records, record]
479	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — deliberate fail-closed; see comment below.
480	        # FAIL CLOSED: a VERDICT-level fault (parse / contract failure — the model responded but the
481	        # output was un-classifiable) must not be read as GROUNDED. We capture a record with the safe
482	        # (UNGROUNDED, parsed_ok=False) result and the error text as the raw payload so sub-PR-5 can
483	        # surface the fault rather than silently masking it.
484	        record = RoleCallRecord(
485	            role=_ROLE,
486	            model_slug=model_slug,
487	            served_model=None,
488	            raw_text=f"<transport_error>{exc}</transport_error>",
489	            parsed=_FAIL_CLOSED,
490	        )
491	        return _FAIL_CLOSED, [*blank_records, record]
492	
493	    record = RoleCallRecord(
494	        role=_ROLE,
495	        model_slug=model_slug,
496	        served_model=served_model,
497	        raw_text=raw_text,
498	        parsed=result,
499	    )
500	    return result, [*blank_records, record]
501

1	"""Sentinel (IBM Granite Guardian 4.1) groundedness contract — FAIL CLOSED.
2	
3	Granite Guardian groundedness polarity (primary source: ibm.com/granite/docs/models/guardian,
4	confirmed in I-meta-002 iter-2 verdict F3):
5	    output is `<score>yes|no</score>` (NOT JSON), where
6	      yes = risk present       -> the answer is UNGROUNDED
7	      no  = risk not present    -> the answer is GROUNDED
8	
9	This is the LETHAL inversion guard. The two failure modes that hurt patients are:
10	  (1) silently reading `yes` as GROUNDED (polarity inversion), and
11	  (2) silently defaulting an unparseable/missing/ambiguous output to GROUNDED.
12	Both are prevented here: the polarity is hard-coded yes->UNGROUNDED, and EVERY
13	non-clean parse falls to the safe side (UNGROUNDED) with parsed_ok=False so the
14	caller can escalate. There is NO code path that returns GROUNDED on bad input.
15	
16	Contrast with `entailment_judge.py`, which fails OPEN on transient error; Sentinel
17	deliberately fails CLOSED, so it is a SEPARATE contract, not a reuse.
18	"""
19	
20	from __future__ import annotations
21	
22	import json
23	import os
24	import re
25	from enum import Enum
26	from typing import Any, NamedTuple
27	
28	
29	class SentinelVerdict(Enum):
30	    """Groundedness verdict for one Sentinel score."""
31	
32	    GROUNDED = "grounded"
33	    UNGROUNDED = "ungrounded"
34	
35	
36	class SentinelResult(NamedTuple):
37	    """Result of parsing one Sentinel `<score>` output.
38	
39	    Returned on EVERY path. `parsed_ok` is True only on a clean, unambiguous parse;
40	    on any malformed/missing/ambiguous input it is False and `verdict` is forced to the
41	    safe (UNGROUNDED) side so the caller can escalate rather than trust a bad score.
42	
43	    `atoms` (F05, GH #1254) carries the per-atom DECOMPOSITION detail — the model's own
44	    `[{atom, type, status, why}, ...]` list — when the decomposition parser produced one,
45	    else None. It is an APPENDED OPTIONAL field with a `None` default so the two non-
46	    decomposition parsers and EVERY positional `SentinelResult(verdict, parsed_ok)` call
47	    site stay byte-identical. It is READ-ONLY metadata threaded into the Judge prompt so the
48	    terminal arbiter sees the Sentinel's per-atom "why" (not just the compressed
49	    grounded/ungrounded token) and cannot rubber-stamp a doc-level "unsupported" without a
50	    per-atom span-grounded rebuttal. It NEVER feeds a verdict decision — `verdict`/`parsed_ok`
51	    are computed exactly as before, so no composition or override behaviour changes.
52	    """
53	
54	    verdict: SentinelVerdict
55	    parsed_ok: bool
56	    atoms: list[dict[str, Any]] | None = None
57	
58	
59	# The canonical Granite Guardian score tokens (LOCKED polarity, F3).
60	_SCORE_TOKEN_YES = "yes"
61	_SCORE_TOKEN_NO = "no"
62	
63	# Map the canonical score token to its groundedness verdict. yes=risk=UNGROUNDED.
64	_SCORE_TOKEN_TO_VERDICT = {
65	    _SCORE_TOKEN_YES: SentinelVerdict.UNGROUNDED,
66	    _SCORE_TOKEN_NO: SentinelVerdict.GROUNDED,
67	}
68	
69	# STRICT ENVELOPE: the WHOLE output must be exactly one `<score>yes|no</score>` element,
70	# with only surrounding whitespace allowed (and inner whitespace around the token). We
71	# `fullmatch` the entire raw string, so ANY surrounding prose, a second (even malformed)
72	# score tag, stray markup, or an off-enum body fails the match and falls to the safe side.
73	# This single anchored rule subsumes the weaker "count the matches/tags" guards (which let
74	# `<score>no</score><score>maybe` and `prefix <score>no</score> suffix` slip through as a
75	# silent GROUNDED — Codex sub-PR-2 diff P0 iter-1/iter-2 + P1 iter-3). The token group is
76	# constrained to yes|no so a clean match can never carry an off-enum body.
77	#
78	# NOTE for PR4 (adapter): if the SERVED Granite Guardian build emits an additional token
79	# (e.g. a trailing `<confidence>...` line) alongside the score, this contract must be
80	# EXTENDED with that exact verified format — never loosened to "tag present anywhere".
81	# `re.ASCII` is REQUIRED alongside IGNORECASE: without it, Unicode case-folding accepts
82	# homoglyphs like U+017F LONG S ('ſ') as 's', so `<ſcore>no</ſcore>` would match and return
83	# a silent GROUNDED (Codex sub-PR-2 diff P1 iter-4). ASCII mode folds only a-z/A-Z and limits
84	# `\s` to ASCII whitespace, so the envelope must be the verified ASCII `<score>yes|no</score>`.
85	_STRICT_SCORE_RE = re.compile(
86	    r"\s*<score>\s*(yes|no)\s*</score>\s*", re.IGNORECASE | re.ASCII
87	)
88	
89	
90	def parse_sentinel_score(raw: str) -> SentinelResult:
91	    """Parse a raw Granite Guardian groundedness output into a SentinelResult.
92	
93	    Mapping (LOCKED, F3): `<score>yes</score>` -> UNGROUNDED, `<score>no</score>` -> GROUNDED.
94	    Case- and whitespace-tolerant.
95	
96	    FAIL CLOSED: any output that is not EXACTLY one `<score>yes|no</score>` element
97	    (surrounding whitespace aside) -> UNGROUNDED with parsed_ok=False. NEVER returns
98	    GROUNDED on bad input. Surrounding prose, a second/partial score tag, off-enum body,
99	    missing tag, or non-string are ALL treated as failed parses, not best-effort guesses.
100	    parsed_ok=True is reserved for a single clean score envelope.
101	
102	    This is the INVERTED (yes=risk) Guardian contract — it stays the SOVEREIGN self-host
103	    path's parser (the task-trained `granite-guardian-4.1-8b` honors yes=risk). The benchmark
104	    OpenRouter path uses `parse_sentinel_grounded_token` instead (I-run11-002 L1).
105	    """
106	    if not isinstance(raw, str):
107	        return SentinelResult(SentinelVerdict.UNGROUNDED, parsed_ok=False)
108	
109	    # Anchor on the WHOLE output. fullmatch fails on any prefix/suffix/extra/partial markup,
110	    # so the only parsed_ok=True paths are a lone `<score>yes</score>` or `<score>no</score>`.
111	    match = _STRICT_SCORE_RE.fullmatch(raw)
112	    if match is None:
113	        return SentinelResult(SentinelVerdict.UNGROUNDED, parsed_ok=False)
114	
115	    token = match.group(1).lower()
116	    verdict = _SCORE_TOKEN_TO_VERDICT.get(token)
117	    if verdict is None:
118	        # Defensive: the regex constrains the group to yes|no, so this is unreachable;
119	        # keep the fail-closed branch so a future regex change cannot leak GROUNDED.
120	        return SentinelResult(SentinelVerdict.UNGROUNDED, parsed_ok=False)
121	
122	    return SentinelResult(verdict, parsed_ok=True)
123	
124	
125	# === NON-INVERTED (benchmark) groundedness parser (I-run11-002 L1) ===========================
126	# WHY a SECOND parser: run 11 proved the general `ibm-granite/granite-4.1-8b` (the OpenRouter
127	# benchmark Sentinel) IGNORES the inverted Guardian instruction and answers the NATURAL question,
128	# so the yes=risk contract above mislabels every grounded claim as UNGROUNDED (the run-11 wipeout,
129	# outputs/audits/I-run11-002/l1_groundedness_probe.md). The non-inverted prompt asks the model to
130	# emit one word — GROUNDED or UNGROUNDED — directly. This parser reads THAT.
131	#
132	# It is NOT a polarity flip of the inverted parser (a flip would false-accept fabricated claims —
133	# §-1.1 clinically lethal). It is a SEPARATE contract over a DIFFERENT prompt's output. The
134	# polarity is direct (GROUNDED -> GROUNDED, UNGROUNDED -> UNGROUNDED), and EVERY ambiguous output
135	# (both tokens, neither token, a repeated token, a non-string) fails CLOSED to UNGROUNDED with
136	# parsed_ok=False — never a silent GROUNDED, identical to the inverted parser's safety property.
137	#
138	# Word-boundary count (NOT substring): `\bgrounded\b` does NOT match inside "ungrounded" (no left
139	# word boundary before the 'g'), so a clean `UNGROUNDED` yields grounded_count=0, ungrounded_count=1
140	# -> UNGROUNDED. `re.ASCII` reuses the inverted parser's homoglyph defense (Unicode case-folding
141	# under bare IGNORECASE would accept homoglyphs like U+017F LONG S).
142	#
143	# STRICT WHOLE-OUTPUT grammar (Codex diff-gate P1, no-false-accept): the ENTIRE response, after
144	# stripping surrounding whitespace, must be EXACTLY `GROUNDED` or `UNGROUNDED` with at most a single
145	# trailing period. A word-boundary COUNT was REJECTED because it false-accepts negated prose — e.g.
146	# "not grounded" / "The claim is not grounded." would count one standalone `grounded`, zero
147	# `ungrounded` -> a WRONG GROUNDED parsed_ok=True (a genuinely-ungrounded claim laundered to
148	# VERIFIED, §-1.1 clinically lethal). With the anchored full match, "not grounded", "grounded: no",
149	# "not fully grounded", and any prose all FAIL the match and fall CLOSED to UNGROUNDED (also the
150	# correct verdict for a "not grounded" answer). Trailing-period tolerance keeps the common
151	# "GROUNDED." case clean. `^\s*grounded\s*\.?\s*$` does NOT match "ungrounded" (the `un` prefix
152	# breaks the start anchor), so the substring trap cannot mis-fire.
153	_GROUNDED_FULL_RE = re.compile(r"^\s*grounded\s*\.?\s*$", re.IGNORECASE | re.ASCII)
154	_UNGROUNDED_FULL_RE = re.compile(r"^\s*ungrounded\s*\.?\s*$", re.IGNORECASE | re.ASCII)
155	
156	
157	def parse_sentinel_grounded_token(raw: str) -> SentinelResult:
158	    """Parse a NON-INVERTED groundedness output (one word: GROUNDED | UNGROUNDED) -> SentinelResult.
159	
160	    Direct polarity (I-run11-002 L1, validated on `ibm-granite/granite-4.1-8b`), STRICT whole-output:
161	        whole output == `UNGROUNDED` (optional trailing `.`) -> UNGROUNDED, parsed_ok=True
162	        whole output == `GROUNDED`   (optional trailing `.`) -> GROUNDED,  parsed_ok=True
163	
164	    FAIL CLOSED to UNGROUNDED (never a silent GROUNDED) on ANYTHING else — negated/prose output
165	    ("not grounded", "grounded: no", "not fully grounded"), extra tokens, both/neither token, a
166	    repeated token, or non-string input.
167	    """
168	    if not isinstance(raw, str):
169	        return SentinelResult(SentinelVerdict.UNGROUNDED, parsed_ok=False)
170	
171	    # UNGROUNDED checked first (mutually exclusive under full anchoring; order-safe regardless).
172	    if _UNGROUNDED_FULL_RE.match(raw):
173	        return SentinelResult(SentinelVerdict.UNGROUNDED, parsed_ok=True)
174	    if _GROUNDED_FULL_RE.match(raw):
175	        return SentinelResult(SentinelVerdict.GROUNDED, parsed_ok=True)
176	    # Negated prose, extra tokens, both/neither, repeats, non-clean output -> fail CLOSED.
177	    return SentinelResult(SentinelVerdict.UNGROUNDED, parsed_ok=False)
178	
179	
180	# === DECOMPOSITION (certified MiniMax-M2) groundedness parser (I-run11-004) ===================
181	# WHY a THIRD parser: the broken Granite Guardian Sentinel was replaced with the CERTIFIED
182	# MiniMax-M2 claim-DECOMPOSITION detector. On the 56-item fixture (28 grounded + 28 fabricated
183	# across NUMBER_SWAP / ENTITY_SWAP / NEGATION / FABRICATED_ATTRIBUTION / SCOPE_INFLATION) the
184	# certified prompt+parse scored 0 false-accepts on all 28 fabrications and over-flag 0.107. The
185	# certified call returns STRICT JSON {verdict: "supported"|"unsupported", unsupported_atoms, atoms}
186	# (scripts/diagnostics/sentinel_bakeoff.py: GLM_PROMPT + _strip_json + run_glm_decomposition).
187	#
188	# This parser PORTS the certified `_strip_json` robust extraction (strip ```json fences, json.loads,
189	# largest {...} span, trailing-comma repair) and maps the certified verdict to the SentinelResult:
190	#     "supported"   -> GROUNDED,   parsed_ok=True
191	#     "unsupported" -> UNGROUNDED,  parsed_ok=True
192	# It preserves the LETHAL fail-closed property identical to the other two parsers: ANY parse
193	# failure, a missing verdict, an off-enum verdict, or a non-string input -> UNGROUNDED,
194	# parsed_ok=False. There is NO code path that returns GROUNDED on bad input — an unverifiable
195	# claim is HELD, never released (§-1.1 clinical-safety).
196	
197	
198	def _strip_json(text: str) -> dict:
199	    """Robust JSON extraction from a frontier-LLM response (ported VERBATIM-in-behavior from
200	    scripts/diagnostics/sentinel_bakeoff.py `_strip_json`, the CERTIFIED harness).
201	
202	    Frontier models wrap JSON in markdown fences, prepend reasoning text, or emit trailing commas.
203	    A brittle `json.loads` crashes on one such reply. This handles: fenced ```json blocks, reasoning
204	    prefixes/suffixes (largest {...} span), and trailing commas. Raises ValueError when NO parseable
205	    JSON object is present (the caller maps that to a fail-closed UNGROUNDED).
206	    """
207	    if not isinstance(text, str):
208	        raise ValueError(f"non-string response: {type(text).__name__}")
209	    s = text.strip()
210	    fence = re.search(r"```(?:json)?\s*(.*?)```", s, re.DOTALL | re.IGNORECASE)
211	    if fence:
212	        s = fence.group(1).strip()
213	    try:
214	        return json.loads(s)
215	    except json.JSONDecodeError:
216	        pass
217	    start, end = s.find("{"), s.rfind("}")
218	    if start != -1 and end != -1 and end > start:
219	        block = s[start:end + 1]
220	        for attempt in (block, re.sub(r",(\s*[}\]])", r"\1", block)):
221	            try:
222	                return json.loads(attempt)
223	            except json.JSONDecodeError:
224	                continue
225	    raise ValueError(f"no parseable JSON object in response: {text[:200]!r}")
226	
227	
228	# The two valid decomposition verdict tokens and their groundedness mapping (LOCKED, certified).
229	_DECOMPOSITION_VERDICT_SUPPORTED = "supported"
230	_DECOMPOSITION_VERDICT_UNSUPPORTED = "unsupported"
231	_DECOMPOSITION_VERDICT_TO_VERDICT = {
232	    _DECOMPOSITION_VERDICT_SUPPORTED: SentinelVerdict.GROUNDED,
233	    _DECOMPOSITION_VERDICT_UNSUPPORTED: SentinelVerdict.UNGROUNDED,
234	}
235	
236	
237	def parse_sentinel_decomposition(raw: str) -> SentinelResult:
238	    """Parse a CERTIFIED MiniMax-M2 DECOMPOSITION output (JSON) into a SentinelResult (I-run11-004).
239	
240	    The certified output is STRICT JSON {"verdict": "supported"|"unsupported", "unsupported_atoms":
241	    <int>, "atoms": [...]}. Mapping (LOCKED): "supported" -> GROUNDED, "unsupported" -> UNGROUNDED.
242	
243	    FAIL CLOSED (lethal-inversion guard, identical safety to the other two parsers): ANY of —
244	      - non-string input,
245	      - unparseable JSON (after the robust `_strip_json` fence/prefix/trailing-comma handling),
246	      - a missing `verdict` key,
247	      - an off-enum verdict (anything other than the exact tokens "supported"/"unsupported"),
248	      - a "supported" verdict that OMITS the decomposition contract — no non-empty `atoms` list
249	        (with >=1 atom object) OR no `unsupported_atoms` field (Codex brief-gate P1: a bare/
250	        truncated/non-atomized "supported" did no per-atom work and must not release),
251	    yields UNGROUNDED with parsed_ok=False. There is NO path that returns GROUNDED on bad input.
252	    `parsed_ok=True` is reserved for a clean JSON object carrying a recognized verdict AND, for
253	    "supported", the full decomposition contract (non-empty atoms + unsupported_atoms count == 0,
254	    every atom supported).
255	    """
256	    if not isinstance(raw, str):
257	        return SentinelResult(SentinelVerdict.UNGROUNDED, parsed_ok=False)
258	    try:
259	        parsed = _strip_json(raw)
260	    except ValueError:
261	        # Unparseable / no JSON object -> fail CLOSED (clinical-safe: hold, never release).
262	        return SentinelResult(SentinelVerdict.UNGROUNDED, parsed_ok=False)
263	    if not isinstance(parsed, dict):
264	        # A bare JSON array / scalar carries no verdict -> fail CLOSED.
265	        return SentinelResult(SentinelVerdict.UNGROUNDED, parsed_ok=False)
266	    verdict_token = parsed.get("verdict")
267	    if not isinstance(verdict_token, str):
268	        return SentinelResult(SentinelVerdict.UNGROUNDED, parsed_ok=False)
269	    verdict = _DECOMPOSITION_VERDICT_TO_VERDICT.get(verdict_token.strip().lower())
270	    if verdict is None:
271	        # Missing/odd/off-enum verdict -> fail CLOSED (never a silent GROUNDED).
272	        return SentinelResult(SentinelVerdict.UNGROUNDED, parsed_ok=False)
273	    # F05 (GH #1254): carry the model's per-atom decomposition list through to the result so the
274	    # Judge prompt can show the per-atom "why" (NOT just the compressed grounded/ungrounded token).
275	    # This is READ-ONLY metadata — it does NOT participate in the verdict/parsed_ok decision below,
276	    # which is computed byte-for-byte as before. `_atom_list` is the model's `atoms` list iff it is a
277	    # list of atom dicts, else None (so a malformed/absent atoms field never threads garbage). Every
278	    # `parsed_ok=True` return below carries it; the contract-fail (`parsed_ok=False`) returns above
279	    # already returned and carry None (a failed parse has no trustworthy atom detail to surface).
280	    _raw_atoms = parsed.get("atoms")
281	    _atom_list: list[dict[str, Any]] | None = (
282	        [a for a in _raw_atoms if isinstance(a, dict)]
283	        if isinstance(_raw_atoms, list) and any(isinstance(a, dict) for a in _raw_atoms)
284	        else None
285	    )
286	    # SAFETY cross-check (Codex diff-gate iter-3 P1): a top-level "supported" verdict that
287	    # SIMULTANEOUSLY reports unsupported atoms is an INTERNALLY CONTRADICTORY output. Trusting the
288	    # top-level "supported" there is a fail-OPEN path — a fabricated claim laundered to VERIFIED.
289	    # When the verdict is GROUNDED but the model's OWN atom analysis flags any unsupported
290	    # sub-assertion (unsupported_atoms > 0, or an atom whose status is "unsupported"), VETO to
291	    # UNGROUNDED — the clinical-safe side. The atom analysis OVERRIDES an over-confident top verdict.
292	    if verdict is SentinelVerdict.GROUNDED:
293	        # CONTRACT GATE (Codex BRIEF-gate P1 fail-open fix): the certified decomposition output is
294	        # STRICT JSON {verdict, unsupported_atoms, atoms}. A top-level "supported" verdict that OMITS
295	        # the decomposition — no non-empty `atoms` list (with >=1 atom object), or no
296	        # `unsupported_atoms` field — is a non-atomized / truncated / lazy answer that did NOT do the
297	        # per-atom span-coverage work. Trusting it as GROUNDED is a fail-OPEN: a bare
298	        # {"verdict":"supported"} could release a fabricated claim if the Judge verifies. FAIL CLOSED
299	        # (parsed_ok=False: the output did not meet the decomposition contract). Validated against the
300	        # certification cache: all 25 real "supported" outputs carry BOTH a non-empty atoms list AND
301	        # unsupported_atoms, so this contract gate has ZERO false-drops on the real model.
302	        atoms = parsed.get("atoms")
303	        has_atoms = isinstance(atoms, list) and any(isinstance(a, dict) for a in atoms)
304	        if not has_atoms or "unsupported_atoms" not in parsed:
305	            return SentinelResult(SentinelVerdict.UNGROUNDED, parsed_ok=False)
306	        # Robustly coerce unsupported_atoms (Codex diff-gate iter-4 P1 + iter-5 P1). A JSON-mode model
307	        # can QUOTE the number ("unsupported_atoms": "1"), emit a bool (true/false), null, or a list —
308	        # a bare numeric check would FAIL OPEN. The field is REQUIRED (contract gate above); a value is
309	        # released ONLY if it coerces to a CLEAN ZERO; anything else (>0, fractional, bool, null,
310	        # non-numeric) VETOES to UNGROUNDED.
311	        raw_count = parsed["unsupported_atoms"]
312	        count: float | None
313	        if isinstance(raw_count, bool):
314	            count = None  # a JSON bool is not a count -> present-but-invalid
315	        elif isinstance(raw_count, (int, float)):
316	            count = raw_count
317	        elif isinstance(raw_count, str):
318	            token = raw_count.strip()
319	            try:
320	                count = int(token)
321	            except ValueError:
322	                try:
323	                    count = float(token)
324	                except ValueError:
325	                    count = None
326	        else:
327	            count = None  # null (None), list, dict, ... -> present-but-invalid
328	        if count is None or count != 0:
329	            return SentinelResult(
330	                SentinelVerdict.UNGROUNDED, parsed_ok=True, atoms=_atom_list
331	            )
332	        # atoms is a non-empty list with >=1 atom object (contract gate): veto if ANY atom is unsupported.
333	        for atom in atoms:
334	            if not isinstance(atom, dict):
335	                continue
336	            status = atom.get("status") or atom.get("verdict")
337	            if isinstance(status, str) and status.strip().lower() == "unsupported":
338	                return SentinelResult(
339	                    SentinelVerdict.UNGROUNDED, parsed_ok=True, atoms=_atom_list
340	                )
341	            if atom.get("supported") is False:
342	                return SentinelResult(
343	                    SentinelVerdict.UNGROUNDED, parsed_ok=True, atoms=_atom_list
344	                )
345	    return SentinelResult(verdict, parsed_ok=True, atoms=_atom_list)
346

348	# === F06 (P1, GH I-arch-004) ATOM-COVERAGE COMPLETENESS CROSS-CHECK ============================
349	# WHY this exists: `parse_sentinel_decomposition` (above) gates a GROUNDED ("supported") verdict on
350	# the model's OWN atom bookkeeping — a non-empty `atoms` list, `unsupported_atoms == 0`, and no
351	# per-atom "unsupported" status. But it NEVER verifies that the atom set actually COVERS every
352	# assertion in the cited sentence. A half-decomposed clinical claim — e.g. an efficacy clause that
353	# IS atomized + supported, with a SECOND contraindication/safety clause the model silently dropped
354	# (never atomized, never checked) — therefore passes as GROUNDED. The dropped clause carries the
355	# clinical risk (a missed contraindication is §-1.1 lethal), yet no atom ever examined it.
356	#
357	# This is an INDEPENDENT cross-check: the clauses are derived FROM THE CITED SENTENCE (not from the
358	# model's verdict or its atom list), then each substantive clause is required to be REPRESENTED by
359	# at least one atom. If a substantive clause has no covering atom, the decomposition is INCOMPLETE
360	# and the claim is treated as UNGROUNDED (STRICTER — it can only DOWNGRADE a GROUNDED to UNGROUNDED,
361	# never the reverse). It STRENGTHENS the faithfulness gate; it can never relax it.
362	#
363	# FLAG (F05 precedent, judge_adapter._sentinel_atoms_enabled): default OFF -> byte-identical (the
364	# caller never runs this), enabled by the Gate-B / cert run slate so the strengthened behavior is
365	# the cert-slate default without churning the locked offline suite. Read at call time so one process
366	# can exercise both states in tests (mirrors the rubric/atoms flags).
367	_ATOM_COVERAGE_FLAG = "PG_SENTINEL_ATOM_COVERAGE"
368	
369	# Coverage tuning (LAW VI / §9.4: named constants, never inline magic numbers):
370	#  - _ATOM_COVERAGE_MAX_DISTINCTIVE_MISSES: the incidental-word tolerance for a MULTI-WORD clause.
371	#    Coverage checks each clause's DISTINCTIVE content words (its own words minus the claim's common
372	#    backbone — see `_distinctive_words`); a MULTI-word clause is covered iff at most this many of its
373	#    distinctive words are absent from the union of all atom texts. The tolerance (>0) is REQUIRED so a
374	#    faithful atom that drops ONE incidental word ("...in the trial") does not false-drop a legit
375	#    multi-word clause. It applies ONLY to multi-word clauses (see `_allowed_misses`): a SINGLE-
376	#    distinctive-word clause gets ZERO tolerance, so its lone assertion ("contraindicated") must be
377	#    covered — Codex diff-gate iter-2 P1: a flat tolerance of 1 waived any size-1 distinctive clause,
378	#    re-opening the dropped one-word-contraindication risk.
379	#  - _MIN_CLAUSE_CONTENT_WORDS: a clause must carry at least this many content words to be checkable.
380	#    Set to 1 (Codex iter-2 P1): a one-content-word safety predicate ("is contraindicated") is a real
381	#    assertion that must be covered, not skipped. Only a 0-content-word fragment (bare numeral, pure
382	#    function words) is non-substantive and waived.
383	#  - _BACKBONE_MIN_CLAUSE_COUNT: a content word is "backbone" (the shared drug/population subject) iff
384	#    it appears in at least this many clauses; backbone words are discounted from a clause's
385	#    distinctive set so a repeated subject term cannot launder a dropped predicate.
386	_ATOM_COVERAGE_MAX_DISTINCTIVE_MISSES = 1
387	_MIN_CLAUSE_CONTENT_WORDS = 1
388	_BACKBONE_MIN_CLAUSE_COUNT = 2
389	# A single-distinctive-word clause carries its whole assertion in that one word -> ZERO tolerance.
390	_SINGLE_DISTINCTIVE_WORD = 1
391	
392	# Content-word tokenizer for the cross-check. Kept LOCAL (self-contained, no clinical_generator /
393	# strict_verify import from the roles package) but the stoplist + ">=3 chars, drop stopwords" rule
394	# mirror strict_verify._content_words so coverage counts the SAME substantive vocabulary the rest of
395	# the faithfulness engine does. The connective/contrastive words that START a new clause are folded
396	# into the stoplist so they never count as the lone "shared" word.
397	_COVERAGE_WORD_RE = re.compile(r"[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9_\-]+")
398	_COVERAGE_STOPWORDS: frozenset[str] = frozenset({
399	    "a", "an", "the", "and", "or", "but", "if", "of", "in", "on", "at",
400	    "to", "for", "with", "as", "by", "from", "into", "through", "during",
401	    "before", "after", "above", "below", "between", "is", "are", "was",
402	    "were", "be", "been", "being", "have", "has", "had", "having", "do",
403	    "does", "did", "doing", "will", "would", "should", "could", "may",
404	    "might", "must", "can", "this", "that", "these", "those", "it",
405	    "its", "their", "there", "they", "them", "we", "us", "our", "you",
406	    "your", "i", "me", "my", "he", "she", "his", "her",
407	    # contrastive / subordinating connectives that introduce a SEPARATE clause (split markers below):
408	    "however", "although", "though", "whereas", "while", "despite", "except", "yet", "not",
409	})
410	
411	# Intra-sentence CONTRASTIVE/SUBORDINATING clause markers. The decimal-aware SENTENCE boundary
412	# (lazy-imported from claim_atom_extractor, the read-only cross-check tool) splits on `. ; ! ?` /
413	# newlines without cutting decimals; these connectives additionally split a single sentence into its
414	# constituent clauses so a "X works, BUT it is contraindicated in Y" claim yields TWO clauses to
415	# cover — the contraindication clause cannot hide inside the efficacy sentence. Matched
416	# case-insensitively as whole words.
417	_CLAUSE_CONNECTIVE_RE = re.compile(
418	    r"\b(?:however|although|though|whereas|while|despite|except|but|yet)\b",
419	    re.IGNORECASE,
420	)
421	
422	# Codex diff-gate iter-1 P1.2: coordinating "and"/"or" must split COORDINATED PREDICATES so
423	# "Drug reduced HbA1c and is contraindicated in pancreatitis" yields two clauses — otherwise the
424	# dropped contraindication conjunct hides in one clause and the efficacy atom covers it. But naive
425	# and/or splitting RE-BREAKS numeric lists ("5 mg, 10 mg, and 15 mg") and noun coordinations
426	# ("HbA1c and body weight"). CONSTRAINT: split at " and "/" or " ONLY when the following conjunct
427	# opens with a finite PREDICATE verb (is/are/was/were/has/have/had/showed/reduced/increased/...). A
428	# noun/numeric conjunct ("15 mg in the trial") carries no leading finite verb and is NOT split. This
429	# discriminates clausal-and (split) from noun/numeric-and (keep) without a comma split.
430	_PREDICATE_AND_OR_RE = re.compile(
431	    r"\s+(?:and|or)\s+(?="
432	    r"(?:is|are|was|were|has|have|had|"
433	    r"shows?|showed|demonstrat\w*|reduc\w*|increas\w*|decreas\w*|lower\w*|rais\w*|"
434	    r"caus\w*|le[ad]\w*|result\w*|associat\w*|improv\w*|worsen\w*|"
435	    r"remain\w*|appear\w*|prevent\w*|induc\w*|inhibit\w*|"
436	    r"is\s+contraindicat\w*|contraindicat\w*|"
437	    r"may|can|should|must|might|will|would)"
438	    r"\b)",
439	    re.IGNORECASE,
440	)
441	
442	
443	def sentinel_atom_coverage_enabled() -> bool:
444	    """True iff the F06 atom-coverage completeness cross-check is enabled (read at call time).
445	
446	    Default OFF: the locked decomposition behavior stays byte-identical until the cert run slate
447	    turns it on (F05 precedent). Read here (not at import) so a single process can exercise both
448	    flag states in tests.
449	    """
450	    return os.getenv(_ATOM_COVERAGE_FLAG, "0").strip().lower() in ("1", "true", "yes", "on")
451	
452	
453	def _coverage_content_words(text: str) -> set[str]:
454	    """Lowercase content words (>=3 chars, not stopwords/connectives) from `text` (F06).
455	
456	    Mirrors strict_verify._content_words so the cross-check counts the SAME substantive vocabulary
457	    the faithfulness engine uses. Non-string / empty -> empty set (the caller treats a clause with
458	    too few content words as non-substantive and skips it)."""
459	    if not isinstance(text, str):
460	        return set()
461	    return {
462	        m.group(0).lower()
463	        for m in _COVERAGE_WORD_RE.finditer(text)
464	        if len(m.group(0)) >= 3 and m.group(0).lower() not in _COVERAGE_STOPWORDS
465	    }
466	
467	
468	def _split_claim_clauses(claim: str) -> list[str]:
469	    """Split a claim sentence into its constituent clauses (F06, independent of the model).
470	
471	    Three-stage split, decimal-safe: (1) the decimal-aware SENTENCE boundary regex lazy-imported from
472	    `claim_atom_extractor` (the spec's read-only cross-check tool) cuts on `. ; ! ?` / newlines
473	    WITHOUT splitting decimals like `8.21`; (2) each sentence is further split on the contrastive /
474	    subordinating CONNECTIVES (`_CLAUSE_CONNECTIVE_RE`) so intra-sentence clauses (the "...BUT
475	    contraindicated in..." tail) become their own clause; (3) each piece is split on COORDINATING
476	    "and"/"or" that introduce a PREDICATE conjunct (`_PREDICATE_AND_OR_RE`) so "X reduced Y and is
477	    contraindicated in Z" yields two clauses — but a noun/numeric "and" ("5 mg, 10 mg, and 15 mg")
478	    is left intact. Returns the non-empty trimmed clause strings; an empty/non-string claim yields []."""
479	    if not isinstance(claim, str) or not claim.strip():
480	        return []
481	    # Lazy import (anti-coupling, evidence_distiller.py:511-514 precedent): the roles package does not
482	    # import the generator package at module load. The regex is READ-ONLY (used only to .split()).
483	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.claim_atom_extractor import _SENTENCE_BOUNDARY_RE
484	
485	    clauses: list[str] = []
486	    for sentence in _SENTENCE_BOUNDARY_RE.split(claim):
487	        if not sentence or not sentence.strip():
488	            continue
489	        for connective_piece in _CLAUSE_CONNECTIVE_RE.split(sentence):
490	            if not connective_piece or not connective_piece.strip():
491	                continue
492	            # Split coordinating predicate-bearing and/or (Codex iter-1 P1.2); numeric/noun and/or
493	            # (no leading finite verb) is NOT matched and stays one clause.
494	            for clause in _PREDICATE_AND_OR_RE.split(connective_piece):
495	                if clause and clause.strip():
496	                    clauses.append(clause.strip())
497	    return clauses
498	
499	
500	def _distinctive_words(clause_words: set[str], all_clause_word_sets: list[set[str]]) -> set[str]:
501	    """The clause's DISTINCTIVE content words: its own words minus the claim's BACKBONE (Codex iter-1
502	    P1.1). A word is backbone iff it appears in >= `_BACKBONE_MIN_CLAUSE_COUNT` clauses — that is the
503	    shared drug/population SUBJECT ("tirzepatide", "patients") every clause repeats. Discounting the
504	    backbone means a dropped clause that merely echoes the subject cannot be laundered as covered by
505	    another clause's atom; only the clause's OWN predicate content ("contraindicated", "pancreatitis")
506	    can satisfy coverage. If a clause is ALL backbone (empty distinctive set), it carries no unique
507	    assertion and is treated as covered by the caller."""
508	    backbone = {
509	        word
510	        for word in clause_words
511	        if sum(1 for other in all_clause_word_sets if word in other)
512	        >= _BACKBONE_MIN_CLAUSE_COUNT
513	    }
514	    return clause_words - backbone
515	
516	
517	def atom_coverage_complete(claim: str, atoms: list[dict[str, Any]] | None) -> bool:
518	    """Does the Sentinel `atoms` set COVER every substantive assertion in `claim`? (F06 cross-check.)
519	
520	    INDEPENDENT of the model's verdict: clauses are derived from the CITED SENTENCE (sentence +
521	    contrastive + predicate-and/or split), then each substantive clause's DISTINCTIVE content words
522	    (its own words minus the shared drug/population backbone) must appear in the UNION of all atom
523	    texts. A MULTI-word distinctive clause allows at most `_ATOM_COVERAGE_MAX_DISTINCTIVE_MISSES`
524	    incidental misses; a SINGLE-distinctive-word clause ("...is contraindicated") allows ZERO — its
525	    lone assertion must be covered (Codex iter-2 P1). Returns:
526	      - True  -> every substantive clause is covered (decomposition is complete; do NOT downgrade).
527	      - False -> at least one substantive clause's distinctive assertion is NOT represented by any
528	                 atom (a dropped/un-atomized clause; the caller treats this as UNGROUNDED — STRICTER).
529	
530	    Distinctive-word coverage (Codex iter-1 P1.1) discounts the shared subject so a dropped clause
531	    cannot pass merely by repeating the drug/population terms of a covered clause. The miss tolerance
532	    keeps a faithful atom that drops one incidental word ("...in the trial") from false-dropping.
533	
534	    FAIL-CLOSED on a missing/empty atom set: `atoms` None or carrying no usable atom text, against a
535	    claim that DOES have a substantive distinctive clause, returns False (no atom did the per-clause
536	    work). A claim with no substantive distinctive assertion at all (a bare numeral, or all-backbone)
537	    returns True (nothing unique to cover -> the coverage check adds no constraint; the model's own
538	    per-atom gate above still applies)."""
539	    clauses = _split_claim_clauses(claim)
540	    # Per-clause content-word sets; keep only clauses that carry a checkable assertion.
541	    clause_word_sets: list[set[str]] = [
542	        words
543	        for clause in clauses
544	        if len(words := _coverage_content_words(clause)) >= _MIN_CLAUSE_CONTENT_WORDS
545	    ]
546	    if not clause_word_sets:
547	        # No checkable assertion in the claim text -> coverage imposes no extra constraint.
548	        return True
549	
550	    # Union of all atom texts' content words (join each atom's own contract fields). A model atom that
551	    # speaks to a clause should contribute that clause's distinctive vocabulary into this union.
552	    atom_word_union: set[str] = set()
553	    for atom in atoms or []:
554	        if not isinstance(atom, dict):
555	            continue
556	        atom_text = " ".join(
557	            str(atom.get(field, "") or "") for field in ("atom", "why", "type")
558	        )
559	        atom_word_union |= _coverage_content_words(atom_text)
560	
561	    for clause_words in clause_word_sets:
562	        distinctive = _distinctive_words(clause_words, clause_word_sets)
563	        if not distinctive:
564	            # All-backbone clause: no unique assertion to cover (redundant subject restatement).
565	            continue
566	        # A SINGLE-distinctive-word clause ("...is contraindicated") gets ZERO tolerance: its whole
567	        # assertion is that one word and must be covered. The incidental-word tolerance applies ONLY
568	        # to multi-word clauses, where it forgives one dropped incidental word ("...in the trial").
569	        # Codex diff-gate iter-2 P1: a flat tolerance waived size-1 distinctive clauses.
570	        allowed_misses = (
571	            0
572	            if len(distinctive) == _SINGLE_DISTINCTIVE_WORD
573	            else _ATOM_COVERAGE_MAX_DISTINCTIVE_MISSES
574	        )
575	        misses = len(distinctive - atom_word_union)
576	        if misses > allowed_misses:
577	            # The clause's distinctive assertion is NOT represented -> incomplete decomposition.
578	            return False
579	    return True
580

1	"""Sweep-side 4-role evaluation orchestration (I-meta-002 sub-PR-6).
2	
3	This module is the THIN seam between `scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py` and the per-claim
4	4-role pipeline (`role_pipeline.run_claim_pipeline`, sub-PR-5). It exists so the big sweep
5	file's edit stays minimal: the sweep supplies an INJECTED `RoleTransport` plus the already-
6	extracted per-claim inputs, and this module drives the Mirror -> Sentinel -> Judge pipeline
7	for each claim, feeds the resulting `D8ClaimRow`s through the SINGLE binding gate
8	(`apply_d8_release_policy`, sub-PR-3), persists the snowball KG (sub-PR-5), and returns a
9	flat result the sweep maps onto the manifest + `VerifiedSentence.evaluator_agrees`.
10	
11	The seam itself adds no paid LLM call; the dependency-injected transport performs the
12	(already-paid) verifier calls, and the per-call run-budget guard lives in
13	`role_pipeline.RecordingTransport` (I-meta-008 — verifier spend is threaded into the shared
14	PG_MAX_COST_PER_RUN accumulator there, so a cap breach raises `BudgetExceededError` out of
15	this seam). Tests inject a mock transport. There is NO `datetime.now()`: the caller supplies
16	the audit `timestamp` (LAW VI). The KG opens a SQLite file under the caller-supplied `run_dir`
17	only — no other I/O except the D8 config YAML read (a pure file read).
18	
19	Fail-closed contract (Codex P2 directives, binding):
20	
21	  * D8 IS THE SINGLE BINDING GATE. `apply_d8_release_policy` produces the headline
22	    `release_allowed`. The legacy `evaluator_gate` may be emitted by the sweep as ADVISORY
23	    metadata only; this module never reads or writes it.
24	  * NO synthesized claim_ids. Each claim carries its existing `claim_id` (from the verified
25	    sentence / section). A blank id fails LOUD (`ValueError`) — duplicate/edited claims must
26	    not collide and break rewrite/gap traceability.
27	  * NO empty / vacuous D8 pass. An empty claim set OR an empty canonical required-element set
28	    (the coverage denominator) fails LOUD before any pipeline call — a `fraction()==1.0` over
29	    an empty denominator must never be allowed to release nothing as "all good".
30	  * Coverage denominator comes from the CANONICAL required elements (`required_element_ids`,
31	    caller-supplied), NOT from which claims happened to survive. `covered_element_ids` is
32	    populated ONLY from VERIFIED final verdicts via the caller-supplied claim->element map,
33	    so dropping/refusing a claim lowers coverage rather than dodging the gate.
34	"""

77	class RoleTransportExhaustedError(RuntimeError):
78	    """A force-closed role `RoleTransportError` propagated to the D8 seam with the degrade flag OFF.
79	
80	    Carries the disclosed manifest `status` (`abort_role_transport_exhausted`) and the path to the
81	    `state/halt_<utc>_role_transport_exhausted.md` artifact the seam wrote BEFORE re-raising, so the
82	    outcome is a DISCLOSED hard halt — never a bare coverage=0 teardown. The default-ON path never
83	    raises this (a force-close is consumed per-claim); it fires only when an operator explicitly set
84	    `PG_ROLE_TRANSPORT_DEGRADE=0` and a role transport-faulted."""
85	
86	    def __init__(self, message: str, *, status: str, halt_artifact: Path | None) -> None:
87	        super().__init__(message)
88	        self.status = status
89	        self.halt_artifact = halt_artifact

92	def _write_role_transport_halt_artifact(exc: RoleTransportError) -> Path | None:
93	    """Write the disclosed `state/halt_<utc>_role_transport_exhausted.md` halt marker (Fix C.3).
94	
95	    Records the faulted role's exception type + message so the operator sees WHY the D8 seam hard-
96	    halted with the degrade flag OFF. Best-effort: a write failure must not mask the original fault
97	    (we still re-raise the typed error), so it is logged loudly and returns None. The `state/` dir is
98	    the repository-standard halt-marker location (§3.0); created if absent."""
99	    try:
100	        ts = datetime.datetime.now(datetime.timezone.utc).strftime("%Y%m%dT%H%M%SZ")
101	        halt_dir = Path("state")
102	        halt_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
103	        artifact = halt_dir / f"halt_{ts}_role_transport_exhausted.md"
104	        artifact.write_text(
105	            "# HALT: role_transport_exhausted (I-beatboth-006 #1283 Fix C.3)\n\n"
106	            f"- utc: {ts}\n"
107	            f"- status: {_ROLE_TRANSPORT_EXHAUSTED_STATUS}\n"
108	            f"- reason: a role `RoleTransportError` propagated to the D8 seam with "
109	            f"`PG_ROLE_TRANSPORT_DEGRADE` OFF.\n"
110	            f"- faulted_role_error: {type(exc).__name__}: {exc}\n\n"
111	            "The per-role transport degrade (default ON) was explicitly disabled, so a force-closed "
112	            "Mirror/Judge/Sentinel transport HARD-HALTS the run loudly with this disclosed status "
113	            "instead of degrading per-claim to UNSUPPORTED. Re-enable `PG_ROLE_TRANSPORT_DEGRADE` "
114	            "(default) for the per-claim fail-closed disclosed adjudication.\n",
115	            encoding="utf-8",
116	        )
117	        return artifact
118	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — a halt-artifact write failure must not mask the original fault.
119	        logger.warning(
120	            "[polaris graph] #1283: failed to write role_transport_exhausted halt artifact "
121	            "(the typed RoleTransportExhaustedError is still re-raised).",
122	            exc_info=True,
123	        )
124	        return None

152	# I-run11-001 (#1042): bounded per-claim COMPUTE parallelism for the 4-role seam. The per-claim
153	# Mirror->Sentinel->Judge pipeline is independent across claims (each `run_claim_pipeline` builds
154	# its OWN `RecordingTransport`), so the COMPUTE half can run in a small thread pool while ALL
155	# reduction + persistence (D8 policy, coverage credit, KG write, run-budget cap) stays on the
156	# PARENT thread in ORIGINAL claim order (Codex Path-B SAFE design, .codex/I-run11-seam). At the
157	# benchmark stage (xhigh reasoning, minutes/claim) this is what makes the seam finish in time;
158	# run 10 died on the sequential operational failure mode. LAW VI: worker count from env only.
159	# `1` (or a single claim) preserves the EXACT sequential behaviour, including the live per-call
160	# budget enforcement inside `RecordingTransport.complete()`.
161	#
162	# I-wire-007 (#1321): when the AIMD adaptive controller is ON (default), the per-role AIMD gates
163	# (judge + sentinel, in openrouter_role_transport) are the BINDING concurrency throttle — they probe
164	# UP to and ride the live ceiling. The claim-worker pool is then sized to the AIMD per-role MAX as a
165	# CEILING (so enough worker threads exist for the controller to ramp into) rather than the fixed-6
166	# static cap; the controller — not this number — decides how many POSTs actually run at once. When
167	# adaptive is OFF, the static `PG_FOUR_ROLE_CLAIM_WORKERS` (default 6) is used — byte-identical to
168	# pre-#1321. The pool is a thread-count ceiling only; the AIMD gate inside each role's `complete()`
169	# enforces the real in-flight limit. FAITHFULNESS-NEUTRAL: a larger pool only lets the controller
170	# explore concurrency; the per-claim fail-closed verdict logic is untouched.
171	def _resolve_claim_workers() -> int:
172	    """The 4-role COMPUTE thread-pool size.
173	
174	    An explicit `PG_FOUR_ROLE_CLAIM_WORKERS` ALWAYS wins (full operator override). Otherwise, with the
175	    AIMD controller ON (default), size the pool to the larger of the static default and the AIMD
176	    per-role MAX ceiling so the controller has worker threads to ramp into; with adaptive OFF, the
177	    static default 6 is used (byte-identical to pre-#1321).
178	    """
179	    explicit = os.getenv("PG_FOUR_ROLE_CLAIM_WORKERS")
180	    if explicit is not None:
181	        try:
182	            return max(1, int(explicit))
183	        except (TypeError, ValueError):
184	            return 6
185	    static_default = 6
186	    try:
187	        from src.polaris_graph.roles.adaptive_concurrency import (  # noqa: PLC0415
188	            adaptive_concurrency_enabled as _aimd_on,
189	            build_role_controller as _build_ctrl,
190	        )
191	
192	        if _aimd_on():
193	            # Ceiling = the largest AIMD per-role MAX across the throttled roles, floored at the
194	            # static default so the pool never SHRINKS below baseline.
195	            _maxes = [
196	                _build_ctrl(role).bounds[1] for role in ("judge", "sentinel")
197	            ]
198	            return max(static_default, *_maxes)
199	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — a resolver hiccup must never block the seam; fall to default.
200	        pass
201	    return static_default
202

215	# claim's final verdict (and its covered required-element ids) AS IT SETTLES, so on a seam timeout the

230	# I-arch-004 F22 (#1255, h4): HARD cap under parallel 4-role via ATOMIC budget RESERVATION.
231	#
232	# ROOT CAUSE: the prior parallel path submitted ALL claim futures up front and only re-added each
233	# worker's verifier spend to the parent run counter AFTER its future completed (Codex iter-2 P1.1).
234	# That BOUNDS the overshoot to ~(workers in flight) but is NOT a hard cap — up to `_CLAIM_WORKERS`
235	# claims each run (and spend) before any of their deltas reconcile into `current_run_cost()` and the
236	# cap fires. So a near-cap run could overshoot `PG_MAX_COST_PER_RUN` by ~workers * per-claim spend.
237	#
238	# FIX (parent-thread admission control, no new global / no lock / `RecordingTransport` untouched):
239	# the PARENT reserves a conservative per-claim ANTICIPATED cost BEFORE submitting a claim's worker,
240	# and admits a claim ONLY while
241	#     current_run_cost() (settled) + reserved (in-flight) + anticipated(claim) <= cap.
242	# A worker SETTLES on completion: the parent re-adds the worker's ACTUAL verifier delta and releases
243	# that claim's reservation. Because anticipated >= actual for every claim, even if EVERY in-flight
244	# claim bills its FULL reservation the total can never exceed the cap — a HARD cap. When a claim
245	# cannot fit AND nothing is in flight to free headroom, the seam raises `BudgetExceededError`
246	# pre-spend (fail LOUD, no silent thin — §-1.3). The SEQUENTIAL path (workers==1 / single claim) is
247	# UNTOUCHED and byte-identical: it keeps the live per-call `RecordingTransport` budget (soft-by-one,
248	# exactly as today). Flag-gated by PG_FOUR_ROLE_BUDGET_RESERVE (default ON); OFF reverts the parallel
249	# path to the pre-F22 submit-all + reconcile-on-completion behaviour (still bounded, not hard).
250	_BUDGET_RESERVE_ENABLED = os.getenv("PG_FOUR_ROLE_BUDGET_RESERVE", "1") != "0"
251	
252	# I-arch-004 F22 (#1255, h4): a TRUE per-call token upper bound for the reservation, covering BOTH the
253	# prompt (input) and the completion+reasoning (output) sides. For any one OpenRouter call the provider
254	# bills `prompt + completion + reasoning` tokens, and the SUM is bounded by the model's CONTEXT WINDOW
255	# (the provider rejects a request whose prompt+max_completion exceeds context_length). So
256	# `context_tokens` is a hard ceiling on a call's TOTAL billable tokens — pricing that whole budget at
257	# the model's OUTPUT rate (>= its input rate for every verifier model in the price table) makes the
258	# per-call estimate >= the real `_impute_cost_from_tokens` for ANY in-spec call, INCLUDING a maximal
259	# prompt (Codex F22 iter-1 P1: the prior bound only allowed `max_tokens` worth of input, which a long
260	# prompt could exceed). Default 400_000 covers the largest pinned context window (deepseek-v4-pro
261	# 384_000 per §9.1.8 / OpenRouter `/api/v1/models`); LAW VI override for a re-pin to a larger-context
262	# verifier. This is a CAP on the reservation, not a token starvation — it never lowers any max_tokens.
263	_RESERVE_CONTEXT_TOKENS = max(1, int(os.getenv("PG_FOUR_ROLE_RESERVE_CONTEXT_TOKENS", "400000")))
264	
265	
266	def _anticipated_claim_cost(model_slugs: dict[str, str]) -> float:
267	    """A conservative UPPER BOUND on one claim's 4-role verifier spend, for budget RESERVATION.
268	
269	    A claim runs Mirror (pass-1 + pass-2 == 2 calls), Sentinel (1) and Judge (1). For ONE call the
270	    provider bills `prompt + completion + reasoning` tokens, and that SUM is bounded by the model's
271	    CONTEXT WINDOW (`_RESERVE_CONTEXT_TOKENS`, default 400_000 — >= the largest pinned verifier context).
272	    Pricing the WHOLE context budget at the model's OUTPUT rate (>= its INPUT rate for every verifier
273	    in the price table) makes the per-call estimate >= the real `_impute_cost_from_tokens` for ANY
274	    in-spec call, including a maximal prompt: actual `(prompt + completion + reasoning) <= context`, and
275	    every one of those tokens bills at <= output_rate, so `actual_call_cost <= context * output_rate ==
276	    anticipated_call_cost`. Therefore anticipated >= actual per call, which is what makes the
277	    reservation a HARD ceiling. Uses the SAME price table / impute function the budget guard bills with
278	    (genuineness: the bound is not a test-known constant — LAW II). Reasoning bills at the output rate,
279	    so `_impute_cost_from_tokens(slug, 0, context, 0)` == context * output_rate (Opus-tier default for an
280	    unpriced slug — the safe, conservative direction for a budget ceiling).
281	    """
282	    # (call_count, model_slug) per role the pipeline invokes (Mirror is two calls: pass-1 + pass-2).
283	    legs = (
284	        (2, model_slugs.get("mirror", "")),
285	        (1, model_slugs.get("sentinel", "")),
286	        (1, model_slugs.get("judge", "")),
287	    )
288	    total = 0.0
289	    for call_count, slug in legs:
290	        # Price the ENTIRE context-window token budget at the slug's OUTPUT rate (the highest of its
291	        # input/output rates). Passing it as `output_tokens` bills it at the output rate via the impute
292	        # function — a single term that upper-bounds the prompt + completion + reasoning of any one call.
293	        per_call = _impute_cost_from_tokens(slug, 0, _RESERVE_CONTEXT_TOKENS, 0)
294	        total += call_count * per_call
295	    return total
