360	
361	
362	def evaluator_agrees_from_verdict(final_verdict: str) -> bool:
363	    """Map a composed final verdict to the two-family `evaluator_agrees` boolean.
364	
365	    VERIFIED -> True (the evaluator stack confirms the claim); every other verdict
366	    (PARTIAL / UNSUPPORTED / FABRICATED / UNREACHABLE) -> False. The sweep applies this ONLY
367	    to kept sentences (`verifier_pass=True`), so the `VerifiedSentence` validator that forbids
368	    `evaluator_agrees=True` on a dropped sentence is never tripped.
369	    """
370	    return final_verdict == _VERDICT_VERIFIED
371	
372	
373	def build_evaluator_agrees_map(
374	    final_verdicts: dict[str, str],
375	    kept_claim_ids: set[str] | None = None,
376	) -> dict[str, bool]:
377	    """Build the per-claim `evaluator_agrees` MAP for the sweep manifest (I-meta-002 PR-9/M5).
378	
379	    Maps each `claim_id` in `final_verdicts` to the §-1.1 fail-safe boolean:
380	
381	        evaluator_agrees = (claim is kept) AND evaluator_agrees_from_verdict(final_verdict)
382	
383	    where `evaluator_agrees_from_verdict` is the SINGLE source of the verdict rule
384	    (`final_verdict == "VERIFIED"`). Every other verdict — PARTIAL / UNSUPPORTED / FABRICATED /
385	    UNREACHABLE / any unknown string — maps to False. The value is NEVER True before a VERIFIED
386	    final verdict exists AND the claim is kept (a non-VERIFIED verdict must never read as
387	    "evaluator agreed" in clinical context).
388	
389	    `kept_claim_ids` is the set of claim_ids that survived as kept/verified sentences
390	    (`verifier_pass=True`). When it is None, ALL claim_ids in `final_verdicts` are treated as
391	    kept — INVARIANT: on the sweep path the `FourRoleClaim` set is built (by the M3a builder)
392	    from KEPT (is_verified) sentences only, so every claim_id in `final_verdicts` is already a
393	    kept claim and passing None is correct there. When a set IS supplied, a claim_id absent from
394	    it maps to False even if its verdict is VERIFIED (defensive: a not-kept claim can never agree).
395	
396	    Keys are EXACTLY `final_verdicts.keys()` (so the map is joinable to
397	    `four_role_claim_audit.json` from M3b); `kept_claim_ids` affects only the boolean value and
398	    never adds or removes keys. An empty `final_verdicts` yields `{}` (no error here; the sweep
399	    branch's own fail-closed guards already handle empty claim sets upstream).
400	    """
401	    agrees_map: dict[str, bool] = {}
402	    for claim_id, final_verdict in final_verdicts.items():
403	        is_kept = kept_claim_ids is None or claim_id in kept_claim_ids
404	        agrees_map[claim_id] = is_kept and evaluator_agrees_from_verdict(final_verdict)
405	    return agrees_map
406	
407	
408	def _append_settled_verdict(
409	    settled_path: Path, *, claim_id: str, verdict: str, covered_element_ids
410	) -> None:
411	    """I-wire-007 (#1321): APPEND one settled-verdict line to the seam-preserve sidecar.
412	
413	    Used by BOTH the parallel `_settle` drain and the sequential per-claim loop so a later outer-seam
414	    TIMEOUT can RECOVER the partials already computed. Best-effort: a sidecar I/O failure is logged but
415	    NEVER raised (FAITHFULNESS-NEUTRAL — the sidecar is a recovery aid, never a verdict gate).
416	    """
417	    try:
418	        with settled_path.open("a", encoding="utf-8") as handle:
419	            handle.write(
420	                json.dumps(
421	                    {
422	                        "claim_id": claim_id,
423	                        "verdict": verdict,
424	                        "covered_element_ids": list(covered_element_ids or []),
425	                    },
426	                    ensure_ascii=False,
427	                    sort_keys=True,
428	                )
429	                + "\n"
430	            )
431	    except OSError as exc:
432	        logger.warning(
433	            "[polaris graph] I-wire-007: settled-verdicts sidecar append failed at %s: %s",
434	            settled_path, exc,
435	        )
436	
437	
438	def _write_role_call_log(path: Path, role_call_log: list[dict]) -> None:
439	    """Write the per-role-call reasoning log as one sorted-key JSON object per line.
440	
441	    I-run11-001 (#1042): factored out of `run_four_role_evaluation` so the SAME serialization is
442	    used by both the INCREMENTAL per-claim write (mid-run monitorability) and the final idempotent
443	    write. Byte-identical to the prior inline `write_text` (`ensure_ascii=False, sort_keys=True`),
444	    so a partial file rewritten on the next claim is just a longer prefix of the same content.
445	    """
446	    path.write_text(
447	        "".join(
448	            json.dumps(entry, ensure_ascii=False, sort_keys=True) + "\n"
449	            for entry in role_call_log
450	        ),
451	        encoding="utf-8",
452	    )
453	
454	
455	def _compute_claim_results(
456	    transport: RoleTransport,
457	    *,
458	    claims: list[FourRoleClaim],
459	    model_slugs: dict[str, str],
460	    timestamp: str,
461	    run_dir: Path,
462	    heartbeat_claims_cb=None,
463	) -> list[tuple[ClaimPipelineResult, float | None]]:
464	    """COMPUTE the per-claim 4-role pipeline for every claim, returning results BY INPUT INDEX.
465	
466	    I-run11-001 (#1042), Codex Path-B SAFE design (.codex/I-run11-seam). This is the ONLY half
467	    that runs the (already-paid) verifier calls; it makes NO reduction decision — the parent
468	    reduces in input order. Each element is `(ClaimPipelineResult, cost_delta)` for `claims[idx]`,
469	    where:
470	
471	      * SEQUENTIAL (`_CLAIM_WORKERS == 1` or a single claim): `run_claim_pipeline` is called
472	        DIRECTLY on the parent thread (no copied context, no `reset_run_cost`), so
473	        `RecordingTransport.complete()` enforces the LIVE per-call run budget on the parent
474	        `_RUN_COST_CTX` EXACTLY as today. `cost_delta is None` signals the parent "already
475	        accounted — do not re-add" (byte-equivalence with the pre-#1042 behaviour).
476	
477	      * PARALLEL: each claim runs inside its OWN `contextvars.copy_context()` snapshot that is taken
478	        ON THE PARENT THREAD BEFORE submit (Codex iter-2 P1.2 — copying inside the worker would
479	        snapshot the worker's EMPTY default context, so the parent Path-B capture sink
480	        `pathB_capture._SINK` / `_ROLE` registered by `pathB_runner` on the PARENT would be ABSENT
481	        and verifier capture would no-op, failing post-run completeness when workers>1). Each claim
482	        gets its OWN copy (never one shared copy across concurrent workers — a copy carries the
483	        worker's isolated `_RUN_COST_CTX` AND the parent's `_SINK` reference). The worker
484	        `reset_run_cost()`s its copy, runs the pipeline, and returns `current_run_cost()` as
485	        `cost_delta`; the PARENT re-adds it to the single run counter and enforces the cap DURING
486	        compute (Codex iter-2 P1.1) so overspend is bounded to the workers in-flight at breach
487	        (~workers-1), not all claims. The pathB capture `_SINK` is shared BY REFERENCE through the
488	        copied context (atomic `list.append`), so verifier captures land at the PARENT sink for the
489	        M4 gate — it is NOT isolated. A worker exception PROPAGATES (fail closed; no `except: pass`).
490	    """
491	    n = len(claims)
492	
493	    # I-ready-017 FX-08b (#1113): CLAIM-LEVEL DEDUP (BUG-04 determinism half).
494	    # 4+ byte-identical claims were SPLIT VERIFIED/UNSUPPORTED in the same run
495	    # (a non-deterministic release gate). Two claims whose ENTIRE pipeline INPUT
496	    # is identical MUST get the same verdict — so run the pipeline ONCE per
497	    # distinct input and fan the verdict out. The key is the FULL input the
498	    # pipeline consumes (claim_text + evidence doc_id+text + severity +
499	    # s0_categories), NOT just (sentence+ids+spans): anything that could
500	    # legitimately change the verdict is in the key, so two genuinely-different
501	    # claims can NEVER collapse to one verdict (faithfulness-safe; identical
502	    # input => identical output is the only justification for sharing a verdict).
503	    # The evidence doc `text` is the FX-03 cited WINDOW, so it encodes the span.
504	    # Fan-out is HONEST on the audit trail + cost: a duplicate gets its OWN
505	    # claim_id (dataclasses.replace on d8_row), the SHARED verdict + sub-results,
506	    # EMPTY records (no new model calls were made for it, so all_records /
507	    # role_call_log are not inflated), and zero cost (cost_delta None). Coverage
508	    # credit still flows per-claim (each dup's covered_element_ids on its VERIFIED
509	    # verdict). Flag-gated (PG_FOUR_ROLE_CLAIM_DEDUP, default ON); OFF reverts to
510	    # the per-claim run. Recursion on representatives is one level deep (reps have
511	    # all-distinct keys -> this branch is skipped on the inner call).
512	    _dedup_enabled = os.getenv("PG_FOUR_ROLE_CLAIM_DEDUP", "1") != "0"
513	
514	    def _dedup_key(c: FourRoleClaim) -> tuple:
515	        # I-deepfix-001 tail-B1 (#1344, finding #10): normalize a leaked contract-field label prefix
516	        # OUT of the dedup key so two claims that differ ONLY by an echoed "Effect estimate with
517	        # uncertainty:" label (same (fact, source, span) triple) collapse to ONE representative and
518	        # receive ONE consistent verdict — belt-and-braces alongside the strip at claim construction.
519	        return (
520	            " ".join(strip_contract_field_prefix(c.claim_text or "").split()),
521	            tuple(sorted(
522	                (d.doc_id, d.text) for d in (c.evidence_documents or [])
523	            )),
524	            c.severity,
525	            tuple(sorted(c.s0_categories or [])),
526	        )
527	
528	    if _dedup_enabled and n > 1:
529	        rep_idx_by_key: dict[tuple, int] = {}
530	        rep_indices: list[int] = []
531	        for idx, claim in enumerate(claims):
532	            k = _dedup_key(claim)
533	            if k not in rep_idx_by_key:
534	                rep_idx_by_key[k] = idx
535	                rep_indices.append(idx)
536	        if len(rep_indices) < n:
537	            # At least one duplicate — run the pipeline only on representatives.
538	            rep_results = _compute_claim_results(
539	                transport,
540	                claims=[claims[i] for i in rep_indices],
541	                model_slugs=model_slugs,
542	                timestamp=timestamp,
543	                run_dir=run_dir,
544	                heartbeat_claims_cb=heartbeat_claims_cb,  # Codex AC1-gate P2-1: dedup path too
545	            )
546	            rep_result_by_idx = {
547	                rep_indices[j]: rep_results[j] for j in range(len(rep_indices))
548	            }
549	            fanned: list[tuple[ClaimPipelineResult, float | None]] = []
550	            for idx, claim in enumerate(claims):
551	                rep_idx = rep_idx_by_key[_dedup_key(claim)]
552	                rep_res, rep_cost = rep_result_by_idx[rep_idx]
553	                if idx == rep_idx:
554	                    fanned.append((rep_res, rep_cost))
555	                else:
556	                    # Fan the verdict out to this duplicate under ITS own
557	                    # claim_id; no new model calls (records=[]) and no new spend
558	                    # (cost None) — the representative already paid + recorded.
559	                    dup_row = replace(rep_res.d8_row, claim_id=claim.claim_id)
560	                    dup_res = replace(rep_res, d8_row=dup_row, records=[])
561	                    fanned.append((dup_res, None))
562	            return fanned
563	
564	    def _compute_one(
565	        idx: int, claim: FourRoleClaim, worker_ctx: contextvars.Context
566	    ) -> tuple[int, ClaimPipelineResult, float]:
567	        def _run() -> tuple[ClaimPipelineResult, float]:
568	            # Isolate THIS claim's verifier spend in the copied context so the parent can re-add a
569	            # clean per-claim delta and enforce the cap deterministically at the claim boundary.
570	            # `reset_run_cost()` zeroes ONLY this copy's `_RUN_COST_CTX`; the parent's `_SINK` /
571	            # `_ROLE` references carried by the same copy are untouched, so verifier capture lands
572	            # at the parent sink.
573	            reset_run_cost()
574	            res = run_claim_pipeline(
575	                transport,
576	                claim_id=claim.claim_id,
577	                claim=claim.claim_text,
578	                evidence_documents=claim.evidence_documents,
579	                severity=claim.severity,
580	                s0_categories=claim.s0_categories,
581	                model_slugs=model_slugs,
582	                timestamp=timestamp,
583	            )
584	            return res, current_run_cost()
585	
586	        # Run inside the PARENT-captured context snapshot (P1.2): the worker executes `_run` under
587	        # the parent's Path-B capture state, not the worker thread's empty default context.
588	        result, delta = worker_ctx.run(_run)
589	        return idx, result, delta
590	
591	    # SEQUENTIAL fast path: byte-equivalent to the pre-#1042 loop (live per-call budget preserved,
592	    # cost_delta None so the parent does not re-account). A single claim also takes this path (a
593	    # thread pool for one item is pure overhead).
594	    if _CLAIM_WORKERS == 1 or n <= 1:
595	        out: list[tuple[ClaimPipelineResult, float | None]] = []
596	        # I-wire-007 (#1321): seam-preserve sidecar on the SEQUENTIAL path too (a long sequential
597	        # evaluation can also trip the outer seam wall). Only when run_dir already exists — the
598	        # sequential fast path deliberately does NOT mkdir (callers may pass a not-yet-created dir),
599	        # so this never re-introduces the FileNotFoundError the parallel-path comment documents; the
600	        # append helper is best-effort (logs, never raises). Truncate first so a recovery never reads
601	        # stale lines from a prior run sharing the dir.
602	        _seq_settled_path = run_dir / FOUR_ROLE_SETTLED_VERDICTS_FILENAME
603	        _seq_settled_writable = run_dir.exists()
604	        if _seq_settled_writable:
605	            try:
606	                _seq_settled_path.write_text("", encoding="utf-8")
607	            except OSError:
608	                _seq_settled_writable = False
609	        try:
610	            for claim in claims:
611	                result = run_claim_pipeline(
612	                    transport,
613	                    claim_id=claim.claim_id,
614	                    claim=claim.claim_text,
615	                    evidence_documents=claim.evidence_documents,
616	                    severity=claim.severity,
617	                    s0_categories=claim.s0_categories,
618	                    model_slugs=model_slugs,
619	                    timestamp=timestamp,
620	                )
621	                out.append((result, None))
622	                if _seq_settled_writable:
623	                    _append_settled_verdict(
624	                        _seq_settled_path,
625	                        claim_id=result.d8_row.claim_id,
626	                        verdict=result.final_verdict,
627	                        covered_element_ids=claim.covered_element_ids,
628	                    )
629	        except RoleTransportError as _rte:
630	            # I-beatboth-006 (#1283) Fix C.3 — SAME disclosed hard-halt on the SEQUENTIAL path (flag
631	            # OFF / unexpected propagated RTE): write the halt artifact + re-raise the TYPED error,
632	            # never a raw propagation. Flag-ON (default) consumes it per-claim upstream, so this fires
633	            # only when PG_ROLE_TRANSPORT_DEGRADE is OFF — consistent with the parallel branch below.
634	            _halt_artifact = _write_role_transport_halt_artifact(_rte)
635	            raise RoleTransportExhaustedError(
636	                f"D8 seam role transport exhausted ({type(_rte).__name__}: {_rte}); "
637	                f"PG_ROLE_TRANSPORT_DEGRADE is OFF -> disclosed hard halt "
638	                f"(status={_ROLE_TRANSPORT_EXHAUSTED_STATUS}).",
639	                status=_ROLE_TRANSPORT_EXHAUSTED_STATUS,
640	                halt_artifact=_halt_artifact,
641	            ) from _rte
642	        return out
643	
644	    # PARALLEL path (Codex iter-2 P1.1 + P1.2; I-arch-004 F22 HARD cap): each claim is submitted with
645	    # its OWN parent-captured context snapshot so the worker inherits the Path-B capture sink/role
646	    # registered on the parent. The PARENT is the single coordinator: it submits, drains completed
647	    # futures via `as_completed`, re-adds each worker's per-claim verifier delta to the SINGLE parent
648	    # run counter, writes the progress marker, and forwards the heartbeat — ALL on the parent thread in
649	    # claim order. The pool is managed MANUALLY (not via `with`, whose `__exit__` waits for ALL pending
650	    # futures) so on a breach / worker exception we `shutdown(wait=False, cancel_futures=True)` to
651	    # cancel still-queued claims, then re-raise (P2.2 cancel-on-fail).
652	    #
653	    # F22 (#1255, h4) — HARD cap via ATOMIC budget RESERVATION (default ON, PG_FOUR_ROLE_BUDGET_RESERVE):
654	    # the PARENT reserves a conservative `_anticipated_claim_cost(...)` for a claim BEFORE submitting its
655	    # worker, and admits a claim ONLY while
656	    #     current_run_cost() (settled) + reserved (in-flight) + anticipated <= cap.
657	    # On settle the parent re-adds the worker's ACTUAL delta and RELEASES that claim's reservation
658	    # (net change to settled+reserved is actual-anticipated <= 0, so headroom only grows). Because
659	    # anticipated >= actual, the cap holds HARD even if every in-flight claim bills its full reservation.
660	    # When the next claim cannot fit AND nothing is in flight to free headroom, we raise
661	    # `BudgetExceededError` PRE-SPEND (fail loud, no silent thin — §-1.3). With the flag OFF the parent
662	    # submits ALL futures up front and reconciles cost on completion (the pre-F22 bounded-overshoot
663	    # behaviour); the cap then bounds overspend to ~(workers in flight), NOT a hard ceiling.
664	    #
665	    # P2.1 (aborted-run cost under-accounting — documented, not over-engineered): on a worker
666	    # exception the worker's partial paid spend lives in the worker's isolated copied context and is
667	    # NOT reconciled into the parent counter. This is the SAME accepted tradeoff the seam-timeout
668	    # wrapper documents (run_honest_sweep_r3.py ~L4587-4596: in-flight verifier cost on the
669	    # held/aborted path is not reconciled) — prompt fail-closed termination outranks exact accounting
670	    # on an already-aborted run, and the operator authorized the spend.
671	    computed: list[tuple[ClaimPipelineResult, float | None] | None] = [None] * n
672	    # Codex iter-2 P1 (REGRESSION FIX): the progress write below targets `run_dir`, but
673	    # `_compute_claim_results` runs BEFORE `VerifiedClaimGraphStore(run_dir=...)` (which is what
674	    # historically created `run_dir`). Existing callers (scripts/dr_benchmark/offline_e2e.py,
675	    # tests/dr_benchmark/test_offline_e2e.py) pass a `run_dir` that does NOT yet exist, so the
676	    # parallel path's progress write raised FileNotFoundError. Ensure the dir exists exactly once
677	    # at the TOP of the parallel branch (idempotent; the later kg_store reuses the same dir —
678	    # harmless). The SEQUENTIAL fast path does NOT mkdir (it writes no progress file) so it stays
679	    # byte-equivalent to the pre-#1042 behaviour.
680	    run_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
681	    progress_path = run_dir / FOUR_ROLE_COMPUTE_PROGRESS_FILENAME
682	    # I-wire-007 (#1321): SEAM-PRESERVE sidecar — truncate at the start of this compute so a recovery
683	    # never reads a STALE prior run's lines, then append one settled-verdict line per claim below.
684	    settled_path = run_dir / FOUR_ROLE_SETTLED_VERDICTS_FILENAME
685	    try:
686	        settled_path.write_text("", encoding="utf-8")
687	    except OSError as exc:
688	        logger.warning(
689	            "[polaris graph] I-wire-007: could not initialize the settled-verdicts sidecar at %s: %s",
690	            settled_path, exc,
691	        )
692	    done = 0
693	
694	    def _settle(future) -> int:
695	        """Drain ONE completed future on the parent thread: record the result, write the progress
696	        marker, re-add the worker's ACTUAL verifier delta to the single parent counter, re-check the
697	        cap, and forward the heartbeat. Returns the settled claim index so the reservation caller can
698	        release the matching reservation WITHOUT a second `future.result()`. `nonlocal done` so the
699	        marker counts across calls."""
700	        nonlocal done

700	        nonlocal done
701	        # `future.result()` re-raises any worker exception (fail closed) — handled by the caller.
702	        idx, result, delta = future.result()
703	        computed[idx] = (result, delta)
704	        # P2.3 + Codex iter-2 P2 (observability): write the tiny on-disk progress marker BEFORE the
705	        # budget enforcement below, so the just-completed claim — INCLUDING a cap-breaching one — is
706	        # reflected in four_role_compute_progress.json even when the very next check_run_budget(0)
707	        # raises. Parent-only write — never inside a worker.
708	        done += 1
709	        progress_path.write_text(
710	            json.dumps({"done": done, "total": n}, sort_keys=True) + "\n",
711	            encoding="utf-8",
712	        )
713	        # I-wire-007 (#1321): APPEND this settled claim's final verdict + the required-element ids it
714	        # covers to the seam-preserve sidecar, so a later outer-seam TIMEOUT can RECOVER the partials
715	        # already computed instead of discarding them. Parent-only append (this drain runs on the
716	        # parent thread). The verdict is the UNCHANGED `_compose_final_verdict` output on `result`;
717	        # `covered_element_ids` comes from the ORIGINAL claim (the coverage NUMERATOR the recovery
718	        # credits ONLY on a VERIFIED verdict).
719	        _append_settled_verdict(
720	            settled_path,
721	            claim_id=result.d8_row.claim_id,
722	            verdict=result.final_verdict,
723	            covered_element_ids=claims[idx].covered_element_ids,
724	        )
725	        # Re-add this claim's verifier spend to the SINGLE parent run counter and re-check the cap.
726	        # With reservation ON this is the SETTLE step (the reservation for this claim is released by
727	        # the caller AFTER this returns); the check is a belt-and-suspenders guard — admission already
728	        # bounded the spend, but actual could differ and a generator pre-seed change between admit and
729	        # settle would still be caught.
730	        if delta is not None:
731	            _add_run_cost(delta)
732	            check_run_budget(0)  # raises BudgetExceededError if the cap is now exceeded.
733	        # I-obs-001 #1141 AC1 (Codex AC1-gate P2): forward done/total to the run-status heartbeat AFTER
734	        # _add_run_cost so running_cost_usd reflects the just-completed claim. Opaque callback — the
735	        # seam stays ignorant of the heartbeat schema; additive, never raises.
736	        if heartbeat_claims_cb is not None:
737	            try:
738	                heartbeat_claims_cb(done, n)
739	            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
740	                pass
741	        return idx
742	
743	    pool = concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=_CLAIM_WORKERS)
744	    try:
745	        if not _BUDGET_RESERVE_ENABLED:
746	            # Legacy (flag OFF): submit ALL futures up front, reconcile cost on completion. Bounded
747	            # overspend (~workers in flight), NOT a hard cap. Byte-equivalent to the pre-F22 path.
748	            futures = [
749	                pool.submit(_compute_one, idx, claim, contextvars.copy_context())
750	                for idx, claim in enumerate(claims)
751	            ]
752	            for future in concurrent.futures.as_completed(futures):
753	                _settle(future)
754	        else:
755	            # HARD cap (flag ON): parent-thread admission control with budget RESERVATION + bounded
756	            # in-flight submission. `reserved` is the sum of anticipated costs for admitted-but-not-yet-
757	            # settled claims (parent-local; never touched off the parent thread). A claim is admitted
758	            # only while settled + reserved + anticipated <= cap AND in-flight < _CLAIM_WORKERS.
759	            cap = _orc.get_max_cost_per_run()
760	            reserved = 0.0
761	            # claim index -> its reserved anticipated cost (so settle releases the exact amount).
762	            reservation_by_idx: dict[int, float] = {}
763	            inflight: set = set()  # Futures submitted but not yet settled.
764	            next_idx = 0
765	            while next_idx < n or inflight:
766	                # Admit as many affordable claims as the cap headroom + worker slots allow.
767	                admitted_any = False
768	                while next_idx < n and len(inflight) < _CLAIM_WORKERS:
769	                    anticipated = _anticipated_claim_cost(model_slugs)
770	                    if current_run_cost() + reserved + anticipated > cap:
771	                        break  # cannot afford the next claim right now — fall through to wait/raise.
772	                    reserved += anticipated
773	                    reservation_by_idx[next_idx] = anticipated
774	                    future = pool.submit(
775	                        _compute_one, next_idx, claims[next_idx], contextvars.copy_context()
776	                    )
777	                    inflight.add(future)
778	                    next_idx += 1
779	                    admitted_any = True
780	                if inflight:
781	                    # Wait for the NEXT settle so its actual delta (<= its reservation) can free
782	                    # headroom, then re-evaluate admission.
783	                    done_set, _ = concurrent.futures.wait(
784	                        inflight, return_when=concurrent.futures.FIRST_COMPLETED
785	                    )
786	                    for future in done_set:
787	                        inflight.discard(future)
788	                        # Settle (record result, re-add the ACTUAL delta) and get the settled claim
789	                        # index back, then RELEASE that claim's reservation by the SAME index the
790	                        # admission loop keyed it on. `_settle` reads `future.result()` exactly once
791	                        # (Codex F22 iter-1 P2) — no second extraction here.
792	                        s_idx = _settle(future)
793	                        reserved -= reservation_by_idx.pop(s_idx, 0.0)
794	                elif not admitted_any and next_idx < n:
795	                    # Nothing in flight to free headroom AND the next claim still cannot fit -> the run
796	                    # budget genuinely cannot cover even a single remaining claim. Fail LOUD, pre-spend
797	                    # (no silent thin — §-1.3): this is the HARD cap blocking the overshoot.
798	                    anticipated = _anticipated_claim_cost(model_slugs)
799	                    raise BudgetExceededError(
800	                        f"PG_MAX_COST_PER_RUN cap reservation: claim index {next_idx} needs an "
801	                        f"anticipated ${anticipated:.4f} but only "
802	                        f"${cap - current_run_cost():.4f} headroom remains "
803	                        f"(settled=${current_run_cost():.4f}, cap=${cap:.4f}); blocked pre-spend so "
804	                        f"the parallel 4-role spend cannot overshoot the cap (hard ceiling)."
805	                    )
806	    except RoleTransportError as _rte:
807	        # I-beatboth-006 (#1283) Fix C.3 — the DISCLOSED HARD-HALT branch. With the degrade flag ON
808	        # (default) a force-close is consumed PER-CLAIM upstream (role_pipeline C.1 / judge_adapter
809	        # C.2 / sentinel_adapter) and NEVER reaches here, so the seam keeps adjudicating. A
810	        # `RoleTransportError` reaching `_compute_claim_results` is BY CONSTRUCTION the flag-OFF (or a
811	        # genuinely-unexpected) case — the three adapter `transport.complete()` calls are the only RTE
812	        # sources, and flag-ON each catches it. Convert it to a DISCLOSED outcome (NOT a bare
813	        # coverage=0 teardown): write the `state/halt_*_role_transport_exhausted.md` artifact, tear the
814	        # pool down NON-BLOCKING (same P2.2 cleanup as the broad arm), then re-raise a TYPED
815	        # `RoleTransportExhaustedError` carrying the `abort_role_transport_exhausted` status + artifact
816	        # path. Faithfulness untouched — this is the disclosure/halt path only.
817	        _halt_artifact = _write_role_transport_halt_artifact(_rte)
818	        pool.shutdown(wait=False, cancel_futures=True)
819	        raise RoleTransportExhaustedError(
820	            f"D8 seam role transport exhausted ({type(_rte).__name__}: {_rte}); "
821	            f"PG_ROLE_TRANSPORT_DEGRADE is OFF -> disclosed hard halt "
822	            f"(status={_ROLE_TRANSPORT_EXHAUSTED_STATUS}).",
823	            status=_ROLE_TRANSPORT_EXHAUSTED_STATUS,
824	            halt_artifact=_halt_artifact,
825	        ) from _rte
826	    except BaseException:
827	        # On ANY OTHER failure (BudgetExceededError from the cap OR a propagated worker exception)
828	        # cancel still-pending claims and tear the pool down NON-BLOCKING (P2.2), then re-raise so the
829	        # existing budget-abort / fail-closed path in run_four_role_evaluation handles it. No
830	        # `except: pass` — the error always propagates.
831	        pool.shutdown(wait=False, cancel_futures=True)
832	        raise
833	    else:
834	        pool.shutdown(wait=True)
835	
836	    # Defensive: every index must be filled (a None here would mean a future silently dropped — a
837	    # real bug, never a vacuous pass). Fail loud rather than reduce a partial result set.
838	    for idx, item in enumerate(computed):
839	        if item is None:
840	            raise RuntimeError(
841	                f"run_four_role_evaluation: claim index {idx} produced no result from the "
842	                f"compute pool (fail-closed — a dropped future must never reduce silently)."
843	            )
844	    return [item for item in computed if item is not None]
845	
846	
847	def run_four_role_evaluation(
848	    transport: RoleTransport,
849	    *,
850	    claims: list[FourRoleClaim],
851	    run_dir: Path,
852	    timestamp: str,
853	    coverage_ledger: CoverageLedger,
854	    required_s0_categories: list[str],
855	    model_slugs: dict[str, str],
856	    rewrite_already_attempted: bool = False,
857	    d8_config_path: str | Path | None = None,
858	    campaign_kg_db: str | Path | None = None,
859	    heartbeat_claims_cb=None,
860	) -> FourRoleEvaluationResult:
861	    """Drive the per-claim 4-role pipeline, apply the SINGLE binding D8 gate, persist the KG.
862	
863	    I-meta-002-q1d (#948): when `campaign_kg_db` is given, the snowball KG is persisted to that
864	    CAMPAIGN-scoped sqlite path (shared across the sweep's questions) instead of the per-question
865	    `run_dir`, so question N can reuse questions 1..N-1's VERIFIED claims. Default None preserves the
866	    per-`run_dir` behavior (existing callers/tests unchanged). The sweep runs questions sequentially, so
867	    there are no concurrent writers; anti-poisoning (only VERIFIED rows reusable) is unchanged.
868	
869	    Pure orchestration over the INJECTED `transport` (no network, no spend). For EACH claim:
870	    run `run_claim_pipeline`, collect its `D8ClaimRow` + served-identity records + final
871	    verdict, and — only on a VERIFIED final verdict — credit that claim's canonical
872	    `covered_element_ids` into the ledger. Then run `apply_d8_release_policy` ONCE over the
873	    collected rows (D8 is the headline gate) and write every per-claim outcome to the snowball
874	    `VerifiedClaimGraphStore` under `run_dir`.
875	
876	    Fail-closed guards (Codex P2): raises `ValueError` on an empty claim set, an empty
877	    canonical required-element denominator, a blank/duplicate `claim_id`, or a missing role
878	    slug — never a vacuous pass and never a synthesized id.
879	    """
880	    # (P2) NO vacuous pass: an empty claim set or an empty canonical coverage denominator must
881	    # fail LOUD here, before any D8 call — fraction()==1.0 over an empty required set would
882	    # otherwise release "nothing" as all-clear.
883	    if not claims:
884	        raise ValueError(
885	            "run_four_role_evaluation: no claims supplied; a 4-role evaluation over zero "
886	            "claims cannot produce a release decision (fail-closed, no vacuous pass)."
887	        )
888	    if not coverage_ledger.required_element_ids:
889	        raise ValueError(
890	            "run_four_role_evaluation: coverage_ledger.required_element_ids is empty; the "
891	            "coverage denominator MUST be the canonical per-question required-element set "
892	            "(fail-closed, no vacuous pass over an empty denominator)."
893	        )
894	    # (Codex sub-PR-6 diff P1, LETHAL safety invariant) The coverage NUMERATOR must be rebuilt
895	    # here SOLELY from VERIFIED final verdicts — never trusted from the caller. A prefilled
896	    # `covered_element_ids` would let a Sentinel-UNGROUNDED claim that we correctly downgrade to
897	    # UNSUPPORTED still ride pre-credited coverage and release. Reject a non-empty incoming set
898	    # (fail LOUD), then credit a FRESH internal ledger only on VERIFIED finals below.
899	    if coverage_ledger.covered_element_ids:
900	        raise ValueError(
901	            "run_four_role_evaluation: coverage_ledger.covered_element_ids must be EMPTY on "
902	            "input; the coverage numerator is rebuilt here from VERIFIED final verdicts only, so "
903	            "a prefilled numerator cannot let an ungrounded/downgraded claim ride pre-credited "
904	            "coverage (fail-closed)."
905	        )
906	    internal_ledger = CoverageLedger(
907	        required_element_ids=list(coverage_ledger.required_element_ids),
908	        covered_element_ids=set(),
909	    )
910	    missing_slugs = [key for key in _REQUIRED_ROLE_SLUG_KEYS if not model_slugs.get(key)]
911	    if missing_slugs:
912	        raise ValueError(
913	            f"run_four_role_evaluation: model_slugs missing required role slug(s) "
914	            f"{missing_slugs}; the pipeline needs mirror/sentinel/judge pins."
915	        )
916	
917	    # (P2) NO synthesized ids: every claim must carry a non-blank, unique existing claim_id.
918	    seen_ids: set[str] = set()
919	    for claim in claims:
920	        claim_id = (claim.claim_id or "").strip()
921	        if not claim_id:
922	            raise ValueError(
923	                "run_four_role_evaluation: a claim has a blank claim_id; claim_ids are "
924	                "caller-supplied (verified-sentence / section ids) and never synthesized."
925	            )
926	        if claim_id in seen_ids:
927	            raise ValueError(
928	                f"run_four_role_evaluation: duplicate claim_id {claim_id!r}; ids must be "
929	                f"unique so rewrite/gap traceability does not collide."
930	            )
931	        seen_ids.add(claim_id)
932	
933	    # I-wire-003 B1 (#1317): zero the transport's per-run 429/503 telemetry counter BEFORE compute
934	    # so the sidecar written below reflects THIS query's storm only (not a cumulative multi-query
935	    # total in a long-lived process). Resilient: the counter lives in the OpenRouter transport, but
936	    # resetting it is harmless on the self-host route (the counter simply stays at zero).
937	    reset_rate_limit_counter()
938	
939	    d8_rows: list[D8ClaimRow] = []
940	    all_records: list[RoleCallRecord] = []
941	    final_verdicts: dict[str, str] = {}
942	    # I-meta-002-q1b (#939): per-role-call reasoning log, persisted to four_role_role_calls.jsonl
943	    # for line-by-line review. `reasoning` is its OWN field — NEVER concatenated into the verdict.
944	    role_call_log: list[dict] = []
945	
946	    # I-run11-001 (#1042): COMPUTE the per-claim pipeline (possibly in parallel), then REDUCE on
947	    # the parent thread in ORIGINAL claim order. `computed[idx]` holds `(ClaimPipelineResult,
948	    # cost_delta)` for `claims[idx]`; `cost_delta` is the per-claim verifier spend captured INSIDE
949	    # the worker's isolated `_RUN_COST_CTX` (parallel path) or None (sequential path, where
950	    # RecordingTransport already enforced the LIVE per-call budget on the parent counter).
951	    # Codex iter-2 P1.1: the PARALLEL path now ENFORCES the run-budget cap DURING compute (each
952	    # claim's delta is re-added to the parent counter and the cap re-checked as its future
953	    # completes), so a cap breach raises after only ~(workers-in-flight) claims have spent — the
954	    # reduction below NO LONGER re-adds `cost_delta` (it would double-count). `cost_delta` is kept
955	    # in the returned tuples for audit only.
956	    computed: list[tuple[ClaimPipelineResult, float | None]] = _compute_claim_results(
957	        transport,
958	        claims=claims,
959	        model_slugs=model_slugs,
960	        timestamp=timestamp,
961	        run_dir=run_dir,
962	        heartbeat_claims_cb=heartbeat_claims_cb,
963	    )
964	
965	    role_calls_path = run_dir / FOUR_ROLE_ROLE_CALLS_FILENAME
966	
967	    # I-meta-002-q1d (#948): campaign-scoped KG when given, else per-question run_dir (default).
968	    kg_store = (
969	        VerifiedClaimGraphStore(db_path=campaign_kg_db)
970	        if campaign_kg_db is not None
971	        else VerifiedClaimGraphStore(run_dir=run_dir)
972	    )
973	    try:
974	        # Reduce in INPUT order (zip over claims + the index-ordered `computed` list). Completion
975	        # order NEVER drives any reduction — the parallel path collected results BY INDEX, so this
976	        # is byte-identical to the sequential reduction regardless of which claim finished first.
977	        # Codex iter-2 P1.1: the run-budget cap is now enforced DURING compute inside
978	        # `_compute_claim_results` (parallel path) — the per-claim `cost_delta` was ALREADY re-added
979	        # to the parent counter and the cap re-checked there. This reduction is therefore PARENT-only
980	        # and budget-NEUTRAL: it touches ONLY d8_rows / all_records / final_verdicts / role_call_log /
981	        # coverage / kg_store.write_claim / the incremental log. `cost_delta` is retained in the tuple
982	        # for audit but is NOT re-added here (re-adding would double-count the verifier spend).
983	        for claim, (result, _cost_delta) in zip(claims, computed):
984	            d8_rows.append(result.d8_row)
985	            all_records.extend(result.records)
986	            final_verdicts[claim.claim_id] = result.final_verdict
987	            # I-meta-002-q1b (#939): record each verifier call for this claim with its reasoning
988	            # held SEPARATE from raw_text (the bare verdict/body). This is the verifiers' analogue
989	            # of the generator's reasoning_trace.jsonl.
990	            for record in result.records:
991	                role_call_log.append(
992	                    {
993	                        "claim_id": claim.claim_id,
994	                        "role": record.role,
995	                        "model_slug": record.model_slug,
996	                        "served_model": record.served_model,
997	                        "raw_text": record.raw_text,
998	                        "reasoning": record.reasoning,
999	                    }
1000	                )
1001	
1002	            # I-run11-001 (#1042): INCREMENTALLY persist the role-call log after EACH claim is
1003	            # reduced (rewrite the whole file in claim order — small, fine) so a mid-run hang is
1004	            # monitorable on disk instead of the log only landing after the full loop. The final
1005	            # write below is kept (idempotent: it rewrites the same complete content).
1006	            _write_role_call_log(role_calls_path, role_call_log)
1007	
1008	            # Coverage credit ONLY on a VERIFIED final verdict, against the CANONICAL required
1009	            # ids this claim covers — a dropped/UNSUPPORTED claim adds nothing (denominator is
1010	            # the fixed required set, so this can only ever lower the achieved fraction).
1011	            if result.final_verdict == _VERDICT_VERIFIED:
1012	                internal_ledger.covered_element_ids.update(claim.covered_element_ids)
1013	
1014	            # Snowball KG: persist EVERY outcome (audit); only VERIFIED rows are reusable
1015	            # (anti-poisoning is enforced inside the store). role_verdicts records the raw
1016	            # Mirror/Sentinel/Judge signals for provenance. KG writes stay PARENT-only (single
1017	            # SQLite connection) — never inside a worker (Codex Path-B risk #2).
1018	            kg_store.write_claim(
1019	                claim_text=claim.claim_text,
1020	                claim_id=claim.claim_id,
1021	                verdict=result.final_verdict,
1022	                role_verdicts={
1023	                    "mirror_classification": (
1024	                        result.mirror_result.classification
1025	                        if result.mirror_result is not None
1026	                        else None
1027	                    ),
1028	                    "sentinel_verdict": (
1029	                        result.sentinel_result.verdict.value
1030	                        if result.sentinel_result is not None
1031	                        else None
1032	                    ),
1033	                    "raw_judge_verdict": result.raw_judge_verdict,
1034	                    "final_verdict": result.final_verdict,
1035	                },
1036	                timestamp=timestamp,
1037	            )
1038	        kg_path = Path(kg_store.db_path)
1039	    finally:
1040	        kg_store.close()
1041	
1042	    # I-meta-002-q1b (#939): persist the per-role-call reasoning log next to the run — one JSON
1043	    # object per line, `reasoning` in its own field, NEVER mixed into the verdict. Reviewable
1044	    # line-by-line alongside the generator's reasoning_trace.jsonl. (Kept as the final write even
1045	    # though I-run11-001 also writes incrementally above — idempotent same-content rewrite.)
1046	    _write_role_call_log(role_calls_path, role_call_log)
1047	
1048	    # I-wire-003 B1 (#1317): persist the fail-loud "429s seen / claims certified" telemetry sidecar
1049	    # so the NEXT run is judged on whether the storm shrank. Claims-certified is a SWEEP concept (a
1050	    # VERIFIED final verdict), computed HERE from final_verdicts; 429s-seen is the transport counter
1051	    # snapshot. Best-effort write (a telemetry I/O failure must never abort the verdict path).
1052	    try:
1053	        _certified = sum(1 for v in final_verdicts.values() if v == _VERDICT_VERIFIED)
1054	        _rl = rate_limit_counter_snapshot()
1055	        (run_dir / FOUR_ROLE_RATE_LIMIT_TELEMETRY_FILENAME).write_text(
1056	            json.dumps(
1057	                {
1058	                    "rate_limit_hits": _rl,
1059	                    "rate_limit_hits_total": _rl.get("total", 0),
1060	                    "claims_total": len(final_verdicts),
1061	                    "claims_certified": _certified,
1062	                    "judge_concurrency_limit": judge_concurrency_limit(),
1063	                },
1064	                indent=2,
1065	                sort_keys=True,
1066	            )
1067	            + "\n",
1068	            encoding="utf-8",
1069	        )
1070	    except OSError as exc:
1071	        logger.warning(
1072	            "[polaris graph] I-wire-003 B1: failed to write the 429 telemetry sidecar at %s: %s",
1073	            run_dir / FOUR_ROLE_RATE_LIMIT_TELEMETRY_FILENAME, exc,
1074	        )
1075	
1076	    # The D8 threshold is loaded from config (LAW VI, pure file read — no network).
1077	    config = load_d8_policy_config(d8_config_path)
1078	
1079	    decision: ReleaseDecision = apply_d8_release_policy(
1080	        d8_rows,
1081	        required_s0_categories=required_s0_categories,
1082	        coverage_ledger=internal_ledger,
1083	        coverage_threshold=config.coverage_threshold,
1084	        rewrite_already_attempted=rewrite_already_attempted,
1085	    )
1086	
1087	    # I-perm-001 (#1195) slice 2: compute the always-release-aware outcome HERE, where the
1088	    # required-S0 set + the per-claim verdicts are natively available. `compute_release_outcome`
1089	    # reads PG_ALWAYS_RELEASE (default OFF -> released == decision.release_allowed, byte-identical).
1090	    _final = final_verdicts or {}
1091	    _zero_verified = not any(v == "VERIFIED" for v in _final.values())
1092	    # zero_usable_evidence = the NATIVE evidence signal (Codex slice-2 P1): no claim cites ANY
1093	    # evidence document. `not final_verdicts` (no claims) was too weak — a claims-present-but-
1094	    # all-unsupported run with no cited evidence must still zero-grounding hard-block. Mirrors the
1095	    # replay harness's audit_map evidence_ids signal.
1096	    _zero_usable_evidence = not any((c.evidence_documents or []) for c in claims)
1097	    _missing_s0 = {
1098	        reason[len("d8_s0_must_cover_missing:"):]
1099	        for reason in decision.held_reasons
1100	        if reason.startswith("d8_s0_must_cover_missing:")
1101	    }
1102	    _required_s0 = set(required_s0_categories)
1103	    _safety_floor_insufficient = bool(_required_s0) and _required_s0 <= _missing_s0
1104	    # B5/B7 (operator-ratified 2026-06-14): the FABRICATED narrowing (ship-minus-the-claim instead
1105	    # of whole-report block) is SAFE only because the report_redactor excises the fabricated claim's
1106	    # prose. The redactor is gated by PG_REDACT_HELD_UNSUPPORTED (default ON; the kill-switch is
1107	    # "0"/"false"/"False" for offline-test isolation only — same predicate as the runner at the
1108	    # reconcile_report_against_verdicts call site). When redaction is OFF, FABRICATED stays a hard
1109	    # block (is_hard_block) so a fabricated claim can never ship as asserted prose.
1110	    _redaction_active = os.environ.get("PG_REDACT_HELD_UNSUPPORTED", "1").strip() not in (
1111	        "0", "false", "False",
1112	    )
1113	    release_outcome = compute_release_outcome(
1114	        decision,
1115	        zero_verified=_zero_verified,
1116	        zero_usable_evidence=_zero_usable_evidence,
1117	        safety_floor_insufficient=_safety_floor_insufficient,
1118	        coverage_fraction=internal_ledger.fraction(),
1119	        redaction_active=_redaction_active,
1120	    )
1121	
1122	    return FourRoleEvaluationResult(
1123	        release_allowed=decision.release_allowed,
1124	        held_reasons=decision.held_reasons,
1125	        gaps=decision.gaps,
1126	        final_verdicts=final_verdicts,
1127	        records=all_records,
1128	        coverage_fraction=internal_ledger.fraction(),
1129	        fabricated_occurrence_latched=decision.fabricated_occurrence_latched,
1130	        needs_rewrite=decision.needs_rewrite,
1131	        kg_path=kg_path,
1132	        release_outcome=release_outcome,
1133	    )
1134	
1135	
1136	# --- M3b seam: the single thin core both the sweep branch and the offline test call --------
1137	# The builder is a KEYWORD-ARGUMENT closure that the SEAM calls AFTER generation with the
1138	# run-local objects (`multi`, `template`, `slug`, `domain`, `ev_pool`); it PRODUCES a bundle
1139	# with `.inputs` (FourRoleEvaluationInputs) and `.audit_map` (dict[str, dict]). It is wired in
1140	# scripts/dr_benchmark/run_gate_b.py over native_gate_b_inputs.build_native_gate_b_inputs +
1141	# the evidence normalization. The builder takes run-local objects (NOT captured at construction)
1142	# because `multi`/`ev_pool` only exist INSIDE run_one_query, after generation — the seam supplies
1143	# them. Kept as a structural `Callable` so this module never imports the builder (LAW VII CLI
1144	# isolation; the builder lives in roles/, the closure in scripts/).
1145	FourRoleBundleBuilder = Callable[..., object]
1146	
1147	# Filename of the per-claim audit map persisted next to the run (Codex M3 P2 #2). The SEAM
1148	# writes it; the builder does NO file I/O.
1149	FOUR_ROLE_CLAIM_AUDIT_FILENAME = "four_role_claim_audit.json"
1150	
1151	# I-meta-002-q1b (#939): filename of the per-role-call reasoning log (one JSON object per line)
1152	# `run_four_role_evaluation` writes next to the run — the verifiers' analogue of the generator's
1153	# reasoning_trace.jsonl, with `reasoning` held SEPARATE from the verdict for line-by-line review.
1154	FOUR_ROLE_ROLE_CALLS_FILENAME = "four_role_role_calls.jsonl"
1155	
1156	
1157	def run_four_role_seam(
1158	    transport: RoleTransport,
1159	    *,
1160	    run_dir: Path,
1161	    timestamp: str,
1162	    four_role_input_builder: FourRoleBundleBuilder | None = None,
1163	    four_role_inputs: FourRoleEvaluationInputs | None = None,
1164	    multi: object = None,
1165	    template: object = None,
1166	    slug: str | None = None,
1167	    domain: str | None = None,
1168	    ev_pool: object = None,
1169	    campaign_kg_db: str | Path | None = None,
1170	    heartbeat_claims_cb=None,
1171	) -> FourRoleEvaluationResult:
1172	    """Resolve the 4-role inputs (builder WINS), run the SINGLE binding D8 gate, persist audit.
1173	
1174	    This is the seam core extracted so BOTH the guarded `run_one_query` branch and the offline
1175	    seam test exercise the SAME code (no copy-paste of the override logic). Precedence (Codex
1176	    M3 P2 #1): if `four_role_input_builder` is provided it WINS — the SEAM calls it AFTER
1177	    generation with the run-local objects (`multi`, `template`, `slug`, `domain`, `ev_pool`) so
1178	    it PRODUCES the inputs+audit bundle from the finished report, and the SEAM writes
1179	    `bundle.audit_map` to `run_dir / FOUR_ROLE_CLAIM_AUDIT_FILENAME` (json, sorted keys) so every
1180	    claim_id is traceable alongside the run. Otherwise a directly-supplied static
1181	    `four_role_inputs` is used as-is (unit/static path; it carries no audit_map, so nothing is
1182	    written). If BOTH are None the branch fails closed (the sweep never synthesizes inputs).
1183	
1184	    The run-local objects are passed through (NOT captured by the closure at construction)
1185	    because `multi`/`ev_pool` only exist inside `run_one_query` after generation; the builder
1186	    cannot have closed over them when the caller constructed it. Duck-typed `object` here so this
1187	    seam never imports the generator's `MultiSectionResult` (LAW VII CLI isolation).
1188	
1189	    Pure orchestration over the INJECTED `transport` (no network, no spend) plus one JSON write.
1190	    """
1191	    if four_role_input_builder is not None:
1192	        # BUILDER WINS: produce the inputs+audit bundle from the finished report + native
1193	        # contract (supplied the run-local objects HERE), then run the gate over bundle.inputs.
1194	        bundle = four_role_input_builder(
1195	            multi=multi,
1196	            template=template,
1197	            slug=slug,
1198	            domain=domain,
1199	            ev_pool=ev_pool,
1200	        )
1201	        inputs = bundle.inputs
1202	        result = run_four_role_evaluation(
1203	            transport,
1204	            claims=inputs.claims,
1205	            run_dir=run_dir,
1206	            timestamp=timestamp,
1207	            coverage_ledger=inputs.coverage_ledger,
1208	            required_s0_categories=inputs.required_s0_categories,
1209	            model_slugs=inputs.model_slugs,
1210	            rewrite_already_attempted=inputs.rewrite_already_attempted,
1211	            campaign_kg_db=campaign_kg_db,
1212	            heartbeat_claims_cb=heartbeat_claims_cb,
1213	        )
1214	        # The SEAM (not the builder) persists the per-claim audit map alongside the run.
1215	        (run_dir / FOUR_ROLE_CLAIM_AUDIT_FILENAME).write_text(
1216	            json.dumps(bundle.audit_map, indent=2, sort_keys=True) + "\n",
1217	            encoding="utf-8",
1218	        )
1219	        return result
1220	
1221	    if four_role_inputs is None:
1222	        raise ValueError(
1223	            "run_four_role_seam: PG_FOUR_ROLE_MODE is on and a transport was injected, but "
1224	            "neither a four_role_input_builder nor static four_role_inputs was supplied; the "
1225	            "sweep does not synthesize them (fail-closed)."
1226	        )
1227	
1228	    # Static path (no builder): use the caller-supplied inputs as-is (no audit_map to persist).
1229	    return run_four_role_evaluation(
1230	        transport,
1231	        claims=four_role_inputs.claims,
1232	        run_dir=run_dir,
1233	        timestamp=timestamp,
1234	        coverage_ledger=four_role_inputs.coverage_ledger,
1235	        required_s0_categories=four_role_inputs.required_s0_categories,
1236	        model_slugs=four_role_inputs.model_slugs,
1237	        rewrite_already_attempted=four_role_inputs.rewrite_already_attempted,
1238	        campaign_kg_db=campaign_kg_db,
1239	        heartbeat_claims_cb=heartbeat_claims_cb,

content.content:
1	"""POLARIS SCALE workstream (WORKSTREAM X, I-deepfix-001 #1344).
2	
3	Capability-first Telus SCALE deliverable — a parallel track that EARNS
4	internal-DB access by demonstrating the SAME weight-and-consolidate pipeline
5	at scale on a large internal DB + big attachment pile.
6	
7	Modules here are NEW and self-contained (parallel-safety): they never edit the
8	existing retrieval / generator / synthesis files. They funnel ingested internal
9	documents into the SAME weighted pipeline by emitting the SAME ``SearchCandidate``
10	shape carrying an operator-set INSTITUTIONAL weight (disclosed), and they rank by
11	the SAME ``weight_mass`` semantics — a WEIGHT into the existing machine.
12	
13	DNA (CLAUDE.md §-1.3): WEIGHT don't FILTER; CONSOLIDATE don't DROP; the
14	faithfulness engine is the only hard gate. Nothing here caps / targets / thins /
15	drops a source to move a number. Low-weight internal docs STAY at low weight.
16	"""
17	
18	from src.polaris_graph.scale.local_corpus_backend import (
19	    LocalCorpusBackend,
20	    LocalCorpusConfig,
21	    LocalCorpusError,
22	)
23	from src.polaris_graph.scale.mineru_vllm_config import (
24	    MineruBackendConfigError,
25	    MineruVllmConfig,
26	    resolve_mineru_backend,
27	)
28	from src.polaris_graph.scale.scale_harness import (
29	    ScaleHarnessError,
30	    ScaleReport,
31	    run_scale_ingest,
32	)
33	
34	__all__ = [
35	    "LocalCorpusBackend",
36	    "LocalCorpusConfig",
37	    "LocalCorpusError",
38	    "MineruBackendConfigError",
39	    "MineruVllmConfig",
40	    "resolve_mineru_backend",
41	    "ScaleHarnessError",
42	    "ScaleReport",
43	    "run_scale_ingest",
44	]
